PERMANENT PROMOTION TO CHIEF WARRANT OFFICER, W-4 DOR/EFF DATE 01 JUNE 2026 Colburn Patrick John 7818 PERMANENT PROMOTION TO CHIEF WARRANT OFFICER, W-3 No officers promoted in this category.
2. If a selected officer does not decline in writing prior to the projected date of rank (noted above in paragraph 1), that officer is considered to have accepted the promotion on the date indicated. An officer who chooses to decline promotion must submit the declination in writing to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-806) within thirty (30) days of the release of this NAVADMIN.
3. The Commanding Officer (CO), the Secretary of the Navy, or his designee, has the authority to delay projected promotions. Upon the exercise of a delay, the officer shall be notified in writing before the effective date of the promotion and not be tendered the promoting appointment. The delay authority will immediately forward the notification of delay with supporting documentation to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-83), copy to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-806).
4. COs are reminded of their responsibility to ensure the officers they are promoting are mentally, physically, morally and professionally qualified in accordance with section 14311 of title 10, U.S. Code. Per reference (a), officers are ineligible for promotion if they have failed the most recent Physical Fitness Assessment. COs shall verify the promoting officer's information in the Physical Readiness Information Management System to ensure compliance with reference (a) and delay the officers promotion in accordance with paragraph 3 of this NAVADMIN, if required.
5. Prepare a Delivery of Permanent Appointment (NAVPERS FORM 1421/7) (Rev 10-13). Use this NAVADMIN as reference (a). Use section of title 10, U.S. Code 14308 as reference (b) for permanent lieutenants and above. Use section of title 10, U.S. Code 12242 as reference (b) for chief warrant officers. Mail the completed form to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-806) or email via My Navy Career Center at askmncc.fct@navy.mil.
6. Training and Administration of Reserve (TAR) Officers should review reference (b) and (c) for minimum time-in-grade (TIG)/service-in-grade (SIG) requirements for purposes of Active-Duty voluntary retirement. (NOTE: TIG TAR LCDR voluntary retirements changed with National Defense Authorization Act 2021; Update to reference (b) is pending.) TAR officers and those officers temporarily on active-duty anticipating release from active-duty under reference (d) may accept promotion without incurring any additional active-duty obligation.
7. New photographs are required for all officers within three months after acceptance of promotion. See reference (e) for specific requirements.
8. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 115/26 - NAVY WOUNDED WARRIOR ANNOUNCES THE TEAM NAVY ROSTER FOR 2026 WARRIOR GAMES
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 1341624-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 141624Z MAY 26 MID120041246872U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO COMDT COGARD WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 115/26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNIC WASHINGTON DC/N95/MONTH//
SUBJ/NAVY WOUNDED WARRIOR ANNOUNCES THE TEAM NAVY ROSTER FOR 2026 WARRIOR GAMES//
REF/A/DOC/CNIC WASHINGTON DC/CNICINST 1740.1// AMPN/REF A IS THE CNICINST 1740.1 NAVY WOUNDED WARRIOR PROGRAM//
RMKS/ 1. This NAVADMIN congratulates the following Sailors and Coast Guardsmen for their selection to Team Navy for the 2026 Warrior Games.
GSM1 Blair, Christina HMC Borrero, Cesar (ret) GSM1 Bray, Kiiursti MT2 Delgado, Benjamin FCA2 Delmer, William CAPT Fairchild, Gregory SA Fleming-Jones, Aspen LS2 Garcia, Christopher (ret) CTR2 Girard, Briana (ret) GM2 Gordillo Gomez, Jose MK2 Hamilton III, William MM3 Hatori, Tyler MM1 Jew, Jasmine CS1 Kilpatrick, Christina LS1 Leon, Matthew MMN1 Lerch, Lenna AO1 Livingston, Dustin HM2 Miller, Chelsea (ret) HM3 Mutesi, Hanifah RSSN Pennington, Ma'khai OS1 Piotrowski, Derek IT1 Raimondi, Francine AO3 Rivera, Christina AOAN Rodriguez Medina, Edmarie (ret) GM1 Ruff, Hillary (ret) PR1 Shoemaker, James (ret) PS2 Solarin, Olutoyosi (ret) YNCS Swibold, Gaylin AM1 Trosper, Thomas HM1 Walker, Demetrius (ret)
2. The Warrior Games are an annual adaptive sports competition during which teams from the Army, Marine Corps, Navy/Coast Guard, Air Force/Space Force, and Special Operations Command demonstrate the incredible potential of wounded warriors. During the June 2026 games in San Antonio, TX, nearly 200 wounded, ill, and injured warriors will spend 8 days competing in 12 adaptive individual and team sports. The full schedule and livestream broadcast are available at https://dodwarriorgames.com/.
3. Navy Wounded Warrior is the official Department of the Navy program responsible for coordinating the personalized non-medical care that Sailors, Coast Guardsmen, and their families or caregivers need following a serious injury or illness. Qualifying injuries and illnesses may be physical, psychological, combat-related, or non-combat-related and may not be visible to those around them.
4. In addition to providing Members with information, referrals, and advocacy, Navy Wounded Warrior also offers enrollees opportunities to participate in adaptive sports and reconditioning events, including the Warrior Games. These activities are designed to improve enrollees' physical and mental wellness, regardless of limitations, through the use of modified equipment. Navy commands and leadership triads are highly encouraged to support requests for participation and/or refer their qualifying personnel to Navy Wounded Warrior. Individual commands will not incur any travel expenses as all costs are covered by the Navy Wounded Warrior program should an enrolled Member be selected to attend.
5. Sailors and Coast Guardsmen may self-refer to Navy Wounded Warrior, or be referred by others, including their medical team, command, or family.
6. For more information visit https://www.navywoundedwarrior.com, call 855-NAVY WWP / 855-628-9997, or e-mail navywoundedwarrior.fct@navy.mil.
7. Released by Mr. M. H. Swartz, Director, Navy Staff.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

PROMOTION TO CHIEF WARRANT OFFICER, W-2 DOR/EFF DATE 01 JUNE 2026 Hernandez Hermes D 7371
2. If a selected officer does not decline promotion in writing prior to the projected date of rank (noted above in paragraph 1), that officer is considered to have accepted the promotion on the date indicated. An officer who chooses to decline promotion must submit the declination in writing to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-806) within thirty (30) days of the release of this NAVADMIN.
3. The Commanding Officer (CO), the Secretary of the Navy, or his designee, has the authority to delay projected promotions. Upon the exercise of a delay, the officer shall be notified in writing before the effective date of the promotion and not be tendered the promoting appointment. The delay authority will immediately forward the notification of delay with supporting documentation to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-83), copy to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-806).
4. COs are reminded of their responsibility to ensure the officers they are promoting are mentally, physically, morally and professionally qualified in accordance with section 624 of title 10, U.S. Code. Per reference (a), officers are ineligible for promotion if they have failed the most recent Physical Fitness Assessment. COs shall verify the promoting officer's information in the Physical Readiness Information Management System to ensure compliance with reference (a) and delay the officers promotion in accordance with paragraph 3 of this NAVADMIN, if required.
5. Prepare a Delivery of Permanent Appointment (NAVPERS form 1421/7)(Rev 10-13). Use this NAVADMIN as reference (a). Use section 624 of title 10, U.S. Code as reference (b) for permanent lieutenants and above. Use section 578 of title 10, U.S. Code as reference (b) for chief warrant officers. Mail the completed form to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-806) or email via My Navy Career Center at askmncc.fct@navy.mil.
6. Officers should review reference (b) and (c) for minimum time-in-grade (TIG)/service-in-grade(SIG) requirements for purposes of voluntary retirement. (NOTE: TIG/SIG requirements for active-duty LCDR voluntary retirements changed with National Defense Authorization Act 2021; Update to reference (b) is pending.) Training and Administration of Reserve officers and those officers temporarily on active duty anticipating release from active-duty under reference (d) may accept promotion without incurring any additional active-duty obligation.
7. New photographs are required for all officers within three months after acceptance of promotion. See reference (e) for specific requirements.
8. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 110/26 - SPECIAL DUTY ASSIGNMENT PAY PROGRAM ANNUAL RECERTIFICATION
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 1331434-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 131434Z MAY 26 MID120041243150U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 110/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/JUL//
SUBJ/SPECIAL DUTY ASSIGNMENT PAY PROGRAM ANNUAL RECERTIFICATION//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAV/27JAN26// REF/B/DOC/DODI/11JAN19//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 1160.6D, SPECIAL DUTY ASSIGNMENT PAY PROGRAM. REF B IS DOW INSTRUCTION 1340.26 CHANGE 1, ASSIGNMENT AND SPECIAL DUTY ASSIGNMENT PAY.//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN provides notice of the opening of the Special Duty Assignment Pay (SDAP) annual recertification window.
2. The major changes to the SDAP recertification process are outlined in the OPNAVINST 1160.6D instruction, enclosure (2) as a streamlined process for the annual certification of SDAP. The recertification window will close on 30 September 2026. Commands that fail to complete the recertification process by the deadline will have SDAP payments stopped for all affected members. It is recommended that commands review the updated instructions to become familiar with the recertification process.
3. The recertification process can be obtained from the MyNavy HR website at: https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/References/Pay-Benefits/SDAP//, then click the current "OPNAVINST 1160.6 (Jan 2026)."
4. Released by Rear Admiral Jennifer S. Couture, N13.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

RMKS/1. The Chief of Naval Operations is pleased to announce the Fiscal Year 2025 Navy Shore Sailor of the Year: OS1(SW/IW) Kyle P. Willis, EUCOM HQ
2. Petty Officer Willis was selected from among five exceptional finalists, each representing the finest Petty Officers serving at shore commands throughout the Navy. Petty Officer Willis will be Meritoriously Advanced to Chief Petty Officer upon successful completion of Chief Petty Officer initiation.
3. Congratulations to Petty Officer Willis and the below Navy Shore Sailor of the Year finalists: a. LS1(SW/AW) Denise M. Branch, NAVSUP FLT LOG CTR SAN DIEGO b. AWO1(NAC/AW) Samuel S. Conzone, OPNAV SEA DUTY COM PT MUGU c. LN1(SW/AW/EXW) Veronica C. Lockett, RLSO SE d. MA1(FMF/AW) Terry J. Smith, SWFPAC BANGOR WA
4. The competition was exceptionally strong - a true testament to the professionalism and talent of Sailors across Navy Shore Commands worldwide. Each finalist exemplified excellence and represented their command with honor and distinction. Bravo Zulu!
5. Built in the Foundry - Tempered in the Fleet - Forged to Fight.
6. ADM Daryl Caudle, 34th Chief of Naval Operations sends.//

item.title: NAVADMIN 108/26 - IMPLEMENTATION OF LIEUTENANT SPOT PROMOTION OPPORTUNITY (CORRECTED COPY)
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 1281524-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 081524Z MAY 26 MID120041235367U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 108/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/MAY//
SUBJ/IMPLEMENTATION OF LIEUTENANT SPOT PROMOTION OPPORTUNITY (CORRECTED COPY)//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAV/13MAR26 AMPN/REF A IS SECNAVINST 1421.3N, TEMPORARY SPOT PROMOTION OF OFFICERS.//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces a modification to the temporary spot promotion process for officers, expanding it to include Lieutenant (LT) billets. In line with reference (a), LT spot promotion is now authorized only for LT billets within the Naval nuclear propulsion program.
2. Limited Duty Officer (LDO) program is foundational to the operational readiness of the entire Navy. Without the technical expertise and deckplate leadership of LDOs, the Navy could not operate Nuclear Aircraft Carriers (CVN), man new construction submarines, or fill critical maintenance leadership roles at our shipyards and across the Fleet. LDOs support every surface combatant or submarine at some point in the ship's life span, from new construction support in the shipyard, standing vital watches at sea, or through the maintenance mission getting our ships and submarines back into the fight. Within this indispensable community, the Nuclear LDO program stands as a vital pillar of the naval nuclear propulsion program; a pathway forged by excellence, commitment, and proven performance. In addition to traditional assignments on nuclear powered warships, nuclear LDOs perform a critical role as naval reactors field office representatives, executing the nuclear regulatory function at waterfronts and nuclear shipyards around the world. Drawn from the enlisted ranks, nuclear LDOs embody the very best of technical mastery and deck plate leadership. They don't just join the wardroom, they elevate it. These officers carry with them real-world experience, unwavering accountability, and the respect earned through years of action. Their impact resonates from the deckplate to the command level, shaping the strength and future of the force. Senior leadership doesn't just value nuclear LDOs, they rely on them. Therefore, several initiatives have been recently implemented to help recognize the challenging assignments nuclear LDOs fill and better compensate for their hard-earned efforts.
3. Spot promotion to the grade of LT serves to match the officers' rank with their level of responsibility and critical skills. Spot promotions are intended for officers serving in billets designated for the next higher grade.
4. The LDO(N) community seeks top talent from the nuclear enlisted community, including our most senior enlisted, to build a cadre of technical experts. Retaining the invaluable experience of our senior enlisted leaders through this program is a community-wide priority. Those selected are eligible for a nuclear officer accession bonus and annual incentive pay. When coupled with spot promotion to LT, this provides significant benefits for the officer and their family.
5. Fleet Feedback. Recent Fleet LDO feedback has resulted in several changes and benefits added to further reward LDO(N) a. Geo-stable detailing. b. Shift Engineer opportunity for LDOs (receive Shift Engineer pay). c. Mentorship programs. d. More frequent town halls, occurring every 2 months.
6. Fleet Engagement. Command leadership is highly encouraged to actively seek highly motivated Sailors who qualify for the nuclear LDO program and who they feel would be great additions to their wardroom. a. All Surface commands and Submarine Immediate Superior in Commands (ISIC) are provided with a comprehensive list of qualified Sailors who are encouraged to apply for a commission as a nuclear LDO. b. Submarine and Nuclear Officer Distribution (PERS-422) is executing world-wide engagement at all Fleet concentration areas and training commands to increase awareness of the program. Additionally, Navy Personnel Command will engage with individual command TRIADs, departmental leadership, and provide one-on-one assessments by request in addition to providing just-in-time mentorship for current nuclear LDOs to improve recruitment techniques. c. PERS-424 and PERS-422 will provide additional touchpoints to convey the importance of the LDO community and value of mentoring junior Sailors early in their career to further this discussion. d. PERS-42 will engage with CVN Reactor Officers and Assistant Reactor Officers at the 2026 Reactor Officer training symposium. e. Details regarding the fiscal year 2026 Fleet engagement efforts have been promulgated to the Fleet via separate correspondence.
7. Spot Promotion Eligibility and implementation: a. Spot promotion to LT will occur for eligible officers at 2 years of service when serving in specified billets. b. The following billets will be considered for spot promotion: (1) CVN technical assistants (2) Submarine tender division officers (3) Select naval reactors field office assignments c. PERS-42 will coordinate directly with commands on the details of spot promotions.
8. PERS-42/N133 will communicate this policy with command and departmental leadership via virtual or face-to-face briefings to explain the specifics on management and execution efforts.
9. For questions regarding the utilization of LT spot promotions, command and/or departmental leadership should contact Officer and Enlisted Nuclear Program Office (OPNAV N133) at (703) 604-5489 or PERS-422 at (901)-874-3938. They can both be reached via email at nukeldo@us.navy.mil.
10. This NAVADMIN will remain in effect until 1 October 2026.
11. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 107/26 - C-NOte #8: CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT AS WARFIGHTING ADVANTAGE
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 1281517-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 081517Z MAY 26 MID120041234986U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 107/26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/CNO/MAY//
SUBJ/C-NOte #8: CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENT AS WARFIGHTING ADVANTAGE//
RMKS/1. This C-NOte builds on The Way We Fight (C-NOte #4), in which I described how we ensure naval combat power remains credible, resilient, and lethal. We do that through disciplined execution, deliberate reflection, and intentional improvement. A Navy that learns slower than it fights will lose. A Navy that learns during the fight and adapts faster than the adversary wins. Learning is how we generate decision advantage, close kill chains faster, and increase survivability under pressure. We must learn faster than the threat. That is not an aspiration - it's a requirement.
2. History is clear - decisive maritime advantage belongs to the force that adapts fastest. Platforms, weapons, and technology matter but our enduring advantage is our ability to learn continuously, honestly, and at scale. In an era defined by a pacing threat, compressed timelines, and contested environments, learning cannot be episodic or isolated. It must be institutional, operational, and Fleet-wide.
3. We are building a Navy that learns from operations, exercises, maintenance, experimentation, training, and near misses - continuously and across communities. Our objective is clear: identify problems early, determine root causes, assess and elevate risk appropriately, solve decisively, and scale solutions across the Force with speed. This reduces the time from observation to Fleet-wide correction from months to days - directly improving mission success and combat effectiveness. This is foundational to warfighting advantage across the Foundry, the Fleet, and the Fight.
4. To institutionalize this, we are implementing the Tiered Learning System (TLS) - a structured architecture led by the Office of Warfighting Advantage (OWA) that links commands, warfare communities, and echelons into a single learning ecosystem. TLS is not a process - it's a warfighting system that accelerates adaptation across our maritime kill webs. It ensures insights are analyzed, elevated, and shared at the level required to prevent recurrence across the Force.
5. Fleet, Force, and SYSCOM Improvement Offices (FIOs) form the backbone of TLS. Embedded across Echelon II and III organizations, they provide the structure and continuity to translate lessons into sustained improvement. Commanders own learning. FIOs enable it. Accountability remains in the command. FIOs drive disciplined problem solving, integrate lessons learned, track corrective actions, and reinforce a culture of intellectual honesty and transparency that rewards early identification of risk.
6. At the enterprise level, OWA serves as the Echelon I FIO, integrating and directing the Navy's learning ecosystem. OWA aligns governance, education, analytics, and accountability to ensure Fleet insights translate into enterprise action. It develops problem-solving capability, ensures execution and closure, and improves decision visibility across the Force - shifting us from stove-piped lessons to continuous warfighting learning.
7. This system enables a globally deployed Navy to learn as a single force. Through TLS, we will learn from near misses before failure, conduct root cause analysis, not treat symptoms, solve at the lowest capable level, elevate systemic barriers, identify trends across the force, and rapidly disseminate leading practices. A Force that learns faster can be trusted to operate at higher levels of delegated autonomy under degraded communications. This is Enhanced Mission Command in practice - autonomy pushed down, visibility pushed up, accountability throughout.
8. We have historically excelled at documenting lessons but not always institutionalizing them. TLS closes that gap by enforcing disciplined problem solving from observation to root cause through sustained correction across the Force. TLS operationalizes the Warfighting Learning Loop: Observe. Analyze. Fix. Verify. Scale. We will not treat symptoms but will determine and eliminate root causes, validate solutions, and ensure they are institutionalized so problems do not recur elsewhere in the Fleet.
9. Institutional learning requires modern tools that allow leaders to see across the Force. We are expanding systems such as the Single Reporting Critique Program (SRCP) and advanced analytics to capture critiques, near-miss data, and performance trends. These capabilities enable us to identify root causes, detect systemic risk, recognize emerging trends early, and support decision-making with data-driven insight and artificial intelligence. This is how we move from reactive reporting to predictive warfighting - anticipating failure and acting before the adversary. We will measure effectiveness through clear measures of performance and measures of effectiveness, including speed of correction, reduction in repeat failures, increased near-miss reporting, and improved mission success rates.
10. Organizational learning is not a staff function - it is a warfighting capability. If we are not learning faster than our adversaries, we are giving them the advantage. A learning Fleet gains decision advantage, resilience, and adaptability under pressure. In competition, crisis, and conflict, learning at speed reduces risk, increases readiness, and wins battles. The Navy Warfighting Concept demands agility and imposes dilemmas on adversaries - learning makes those advantages enduring.
11. Our goal is not perfection - it is relentless improvement in pursuit of excellence. Every member of the Navy will contribute by surfacing problems early, reporting hazards and near misses, participating in root cause analysis, sharing leading practices, executing corrective actions, and driving improvement in their area of responsibility. This effort integrates with the Fleet Learning Continuum - linking exercises, experimentation, wargaming, and operations into a single learning enterprise. TLS strengthens the Foundry, sharpens the Fleet, and increases our lethality in the Fight.
12. Built in the Foundry - Tempered in the Fleet - Forged to Fight.
13. ADM Daryl Caudle, 34th Chief of Naval Operations sends.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 106/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2027 RESERVE OFFICER RETENTION AND CONTINUATION BOARDS
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 1261344-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 061344Z MAY 26 MID320018688426U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC
NAVADMIN 106/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/NI/MAY//
SUBJ/FISCAL YEAR 2027 RESERVE OFFICER RETENTION AND CONTINUATION BOARDS//
REF/A/DOC/SECNAV/19DEC25// NARR/REF A IS THE FISCAL YEAR 2027 NAVY RESERVE OFFICER RETENTION AND CONTINUATION POLICY AND PLAN
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces the Fiscal Year 2027 (FY-27) Reserve Officer Retention and Continuation boards. This process applies to officers of the Selected Reserve, Individual Ready Reserve, and Standby Reserve-Active (USNR-S1). FY-27 boards for Training and Administration of the Reserve officers are addressed separately via NAVADMIN 021/26.
2. The FY-27 Reserve Officer Retention and Continuation boards will convene on 18 January 2027 and 19 July 2027. In accordance with reference (a), applications will be reviewed for eligibility on a monthly basis, in support of semi-annual Continuation boards. These boards allow eligible Reserve Officers to request to be retained or continued on the Reserve Active Status List (RASL) up to statutory limitations as outlined in reference (a) and in accordance with the needs of the Navy. Only those categories of officers described in reference (a) are eligible for retention and continuation.
3. Per reference (a), applicants must meet the eligibility requirements of paygrade, age, and designator limitations, along with the attrition dates listed in paragraph 4. Applicants may request further retention or continuation to remain on the RASL if previously approved.
4. Attrition dates: a. To be considered for the 18 January 27 board, applicants need to have an attrition date no earlier than 01 February 2027 and no later than 31 July 2027. b. To be considered for the 19 July 27 board, applicants need to have an attrition date no earlier than 01 August 2027 and no later than 31 January 2028.
5. Application submission deadlines for the 18 January and 19 July boards. Completed applications for the 18 January board, must be submitted and received no later than 2359 Central Standard Time on 15 November 2026 to be considered. Completed applications for the 19 July board, must be submitted and received no later than 2359 Central Standard Time on 15 May 2027 to be considered.
6. Additional application policy and procedures are specified in reference (a), and other relevant information can be found by going to the MyNavy HR website at https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/Career-Management/Reserve-Personnel-Mgmt/Officers/Continuation-Retention/
7. Available quotas for each community will align with BUPERS-315 annual promotion planning efforts and are limited based on end strength and community health needs for each community.
8. Applicants will e-mail completed applications with all required enclosures to PERS-911-D@us.navy.mil. The subject line of the e-mail shall read: FY-27 Reserve Officer Retention/Continuation Request. Electronic submissions are preferred, however applicants may submit request packages to the following address:
Navy Personnel Command (PERS-911) 5720 Integrity Drive Millington, TN 38055-0092
9. Upon approval of the board proceedings, the results will be posted on the Reserve Officer Retention and Continuation Board webpage. Applicants can expect to see the results posted within 10 days of board adjournment. Within 2 weeks of board results being posted, applicants will receive official correspondence stating the new approved attrition date to remain on the RASL.
10. To close out the FY-26 board process, the last eligibility screenings for FY-26 will take place on 31 August 2026. To be considered, applicants need to have an attrition date no later than 31 January 2027. Completed applications must be submitted and received no later than 2359 Central Standard Time on 15 August 2026.
11. For policy questions, contact CAPT Chris Seeley, Reserve Personnel Administration, Division Director (PERS-91), phone (901) 874-4482, e-mail christopher.n.seeley.mil@us/navy.mil. For eligibility and package submission questions, contact LT Shelbie Cain, Reserve Officer Status Branch Head (PERS-911), phone (901) 874-4753, e-mail shelbie.e.cain.mil@us.navy.mil or Ms. Jeanitta Edwards, Reserve Officer Status Branch Supervisor (PERS-911), phone (901) 874-2753, e-mail jeanitta.s.edwards.civ@us.navy.mil.
12. This NAVADMIN will remain in effect until superseded or canceled, whichever occurs first.
13. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 104/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2027 PERMANENT MILITARY INSTRUCTOR SELECTION BOARD
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 1241818-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 041818Z MAY 26 MID320018676840U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 104/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/APR//
SUBJ/FISCAL YEAR 2027 PERMANENT MILITARY INSTRUCTOR SELECTION BOARD//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAV/01OCT21// AMPN/REF A IS OPNAVINST 1520.40C, PERMANENT MILITARY PROFESSOR AND PERMANENT MILITARY INSTRUCTOR PROGRAMS.//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces the Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 Permanent Military Instructor (PMI) selection board for officers reporting to the United States Naval Academy (USNA) in July 2027. The FY27 PMI selection board may select up to a total of 8 PMIs from a pool of eligible officers, selected for or serving in the permanent grade of Lieutenant Commander (LCDR) (O4). Applicants must be released by their current community.
2. Reference (a) provides specific application and eligibility guidance. Applications are due to the Out-Service Education Placement and Special Fellowships Division (PERS-443), no later than 1 October 2026, submitted via encrypted transmission on DoD SAFE (https://safe.apps.mil) to PERS-440 (PERS-440_submit.fct@navy.mil), and to LCDR Jenna Quist (jenna.m.quist.mil@us.navy.mil); or mailed to:
Navy Personnel Command (PERS-443) 5720 Integrity Drive Millington, TN 3855-4400
3. For requirements and a list of available academic disciplines, refer to the MyNavy Education advertisement on the MyNavy HR website at: https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/CareerManagement/Fellowships/PMI/.
4. Officer Community Managers will review applicants for release based on their community health and needs. PERS-443 will review all PMI applicants to ensure eligibility guidelines have been met prior to releasing the applicant to the board.
5. Points of contact: a. PMI Education Placement is LCDR Jenna Quist, (901) 874-4060 or via email at jenna.m.quist.mil@us.navy.mil b. USNA PMI Lead is LCDR Justin Downs, (410) 293-6810 or via email at downs@usna.edu
6. This NAVADMIN will remain in effect until superseded or canceled, whichever occurs first.
7. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//

item.title: NAVADMIN 105/26 - EXERCISE-EXERCISE-EXERCISE ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY FOR SIMULATED HURRICANE PILIPO AFTER STORM PASSAGE (HURREX 26)
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ PTTUZYUW RHHMHAA0001 1242305-UUUU--RHMCSUU. ZNR UUUUU P 041200Z MAY 26 ZYB MID320018677517U FM COMPACFLT PEARL HARBOR HI TO NAVADMIN INFO SECDEF WASHINGTON DC JOINT STAFF WASHINGTON DC CNO WASHINGTON DC CMC WASHINGTON DC CDR USPACOM HONOLULU HI CDR USEUCOM VAIHINGEN GE CDR USAFRICOM STUTTGART GE CDR USCENTCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USNORTHCOM PETERSON AFB CO CDR USSOUTHCOM MIAMI FL CDR USSOCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USTRANSCOM SCOTT AFB IL CDR USSTRATCOM OFFUTT AFB NE DIRSSP WASHINGTON DC DARPA ARLINGTON VA DCMA HQ ALEXANDRIA VA DFAS HQ ARLINGTON VA DIA WASHINGTON DC DLA FT BELVOIR VA DTRA ALEX WASHINGTON DC NGA WASHINGTON DC MDA WASHINGTON DC NSA FT GEORGE G MEADE MD USCENTCOM COMMAND CENTER MACDILL AFB FL COMUSFLTFORCOM NORFOLK VA CNIC WASHINGTON DC COMPACFLT PEARL HARBOR HI BT UNCLAS
SUBJ/EXERCISE-EXERCISE-EXERCISE ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY FOR SIMULATED HURRICANE PILIPO AFTER STORM PASSAGE (HURREX 26)//
NAVADMIN 105/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/PACFLT/N1/MAY//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAVINST 3006.1/01MAR2011// REF/B/DOC/DODINST 3001.02/03MAY2010// REF/C/DOC/DODINST 1015.15/20MAR2008// REF/D/DOC/NAVSOP-1000/12DEC2002// REF/E/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171821ZMAR09// REF/F/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171846ZMAR09//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 3006.1, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH CATASTROPHIC EVENTS, NAVY PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY POLICY. REF B IS DODINST 3001.02, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH NATURAL OR MAN-MADE DISASTERS. REF C IS DODINST 1015.15, PROCEDURES AND SUPPORT FOR NON-FEDERAL ENTITIES AUTHORIZED TO OPERATE ON DOD INSTALLATIONS (ENCL 4). REF D IS FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT POLICY MANUAL. REF E IS ALARACT 070/2009, CONUS EVACUATION ALLOWANCES. REF F IS ALARACT 071/2009, NONCOMBATANT EVACUATION ORDER/OCONUS EVACUATION ALLOWANCES.//
RMKS/1. THIS ORDER TO ACCOUNT APPLIES TO ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS WITHIN THE COMMANDS DESIGNATED IN PARAGRAPH 2B. ECHELON II COMMANDERS AND ALL REGIONAL COMMANDERS SHALL ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION TO ALL SUBORDINATE AND TENANT COMMANDS AND DETACHMENTS FOR IMMEDIATE ACTION UPON RECEIPT.
2. THE FOLLOWING GAOI HAS BEEN ESTABLISHED FOR HURRICANE PILIPO: OAHU AND KAUAI ISLANDS A. THE FOLLOWING COUNTIES WITHIN THE STATE OF HAWAII: HONOLULU AND KAUAI B. ONLY THE FOLLOWING COMMANDS WILL MUSTER FOR HURRICANE PILIPO: CNI NAVMAG PEARL HARBOR HICKAM UIC 61062; CNRHI CREDO UIC 69072; NAVY CLOSURE TF - RED HILL UIC 61049; PMRF BARKING SANDS SEC DET UIC 3536A; CNI PMRF HAWREA BARKING SANDS UIC 61064; JB PEARL HARBOR HICKAM SEC DET UIC 46188; JB PEARL HARBOR HICKAM TPU UIC 46424; JB HARBOR HICKAM ARIZONA DET UIC 47707; HARBOR PATROL UNT JB PH HICKAM UIC 58052; NAVY WOUNDED WARRIOR PEARL HARBOR UIC 62288; JOINT BASE PEARL HARBOR HICKAM UIC 62813; COMNAVREG HAWAII UIC 61449.
3. ALL COMMANDS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. COMMANDING OFFICERS (CO) AND/OR COMMANDING OFFICER REPRESENTATIVES (COR) WILL ENSURE THAT THEIR COMMAND'S NAVY FAMILY ACCOUNTABILITY AND ASSESSMENT SYSTEM (NFAAS) DATABASE IS CURRENT AND ACCURATE, TO INCLUDE PHONE NUMBERS AND ADDRESSES. ENSURE ANY RECENT CHECK-INS TO YOUR COMMAND WHO HAVE NOT HAD THEIR CONTACT INFORMATION UPDATED IN NFAAS, COMMUNICATE RECALL INFORMATION TO THEIR RESPECTIVE CHAIN OF COMMAND. B. FOLLOWING STORM PASSAGE/CPA, MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS WITHIN THE COMMANDS DESIGNATED IN PARAGRAPH 2B. ALL CORS/SPONSORS WITHIN THE COMMANDS DESIGNATED IN PARAGRAPH 2B WILL RECEIVE NOTIFICATION FROM NFAAS VIA TEXT/EMAIL TO MUSTER THEMSELVES AND DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS. COS AND/OR CORS WILL ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION OF REQUIREMENT FOR ACCURATE AND TIMELY ACCOUNTABILITY VIA NFAAS. IF MEMBERS ARE UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, COS/CORS WILL DETERMINE ACCURATE STATUS AND WHEREABOUTS OF THEIR PERSONNEL AND MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR THEM AT HOME, WORK, TELEWORK, EVACUATED, OR DISPLACED LOCATION, OR NOT IN THE GAOI WITH SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED, TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OTHER). C. COMMANDS SHALL DIRECT CORS/SPONSORS TO MUSTER IN NFAAS, AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL. IF NFAAS IS NOT AVAILABLE, COMMANDS/MEMBERS ARE TO REPORT THE STATUS OF PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY (PA) TO THE AFFECTED REGION OPERATIONS CENTER (ROC) OR DIRECT CHAIN OF COMMAND. D. DO NOT DELAY REPORTING ON PERSONNEL UNTIL ALL INFORMATION IS COMPLETE. REPORT AND ACCOUNT MUSTERING INFORMATION WHEN OBTAINED, INCLUDING THE POSTING OF AMPLIFYING DATA IN THE NOTES AREA ON THE INDIVIDUAL'S ACCOUNTING LINE.
4. ALL CORS/SPONSORS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. ACCOUNT FOR THEMSELVES AND DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL. B. IF UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, SPONSORS SHOULD CONTACT THEIR COR, COMMAND MUSTERING OFFICER, OR SUPERVISOR TO CHECK IN (MUSTER). C. SPONSORS WILL PROVIDE COMMAND REPRESENTATIVES WITH CURRENT RESIDENCE, DISPLACED LOCATION, OR IF NOT IN THE GAOI, WITH SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED,TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OTHER).
5. A NEEDS ASSESSMENT HAS NOT BEEN INITIATED.
6. FOR NFAAS TECHNICAL SUPPORT, CONTACT THE NFAAS HELP DESK AT 866-946-9183 OR VIA EMAIL AT NFAAS(AT).NAVY.MIL.
7. IF UNABLE TO MUSTER VIA NFAAS, YOUR COR, YOUR MUSTERING POC, OR YOUR CHAIN OF COMMAND, CONTACT THE BELOW POCS (LOCATION/PHONE/EMAIL): A. NPC ECC/877-414-5358/NPC.CAT.CAPTAIN(AT)NAVY.MIL. B. PMRF CDO/808-651-6402/ W_KAUA_PMRF-ALLCDO_US(AT)NAVY.MIL. C. JBPHH CDO/808-864-2460/ USN.JBPHH.JBPHHPEARLHI.MBX.PRLH-CDONSP(AT)US.NAVY.MIL. D. CNRH ROC/808-473-3216/ USN.JBPHH.JBPHHPEARLHI.MBX.CNRH-ROC-BWO(AT)US.NAVY.MIL. E. PACFLT CAT BWC/808-474-5452/5453/CPF.CATBWC(AT)NAVY.MIL. F. NFAAS PROGRAM 202-765-6011/douglas.aguilarquezada.civ(AT)us.navy.mil, ethan.t.audetat.mil(AT)us.navy.mil.
8. RELEASED BY K. ARCHIBALD, CAPT, COMPACFLT N1B.//

item.title: NAVADMIN 103/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2027 NAVY RESERVE NATIONAL COMMAND AND SENIOR OFFICER APPLY BOARD
item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ ROUTINE R 011453Z MAY 26 MID320018670533U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 103/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/MAY//
SUBJ/FISCAL YEAR 2027 NAVY RESERVE NATIONAL COMMAND AND SENIOR OFFICER APPLY BOARD//
REF/A/DOC/COMNAVRESFORNOTE 5400/17APR26/ REF/B/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/271415Z SEP 19//
NARR/REF A IS COMNAVRESFOR NOTICE 5400, FISCAL YEAR 2027 NATIONAL COMMAND AND SENIOR OFFICER APPLY BILLET SCREENING AND ASSIGNMENT PROCEDURES. REF B IS NAVADMIN 220/19, ELECTRONIC SUBMISSION OF LETTERS TO PROMOTION AND SELECTION BOARDS THROUGH MYNAVY PORTAL.
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces the convening of the Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 Navy Reserve National Command and Senior Officer APPLY Board to convene on 10 August 2026. All Selected Reserve (SELRES) officer communities will utilize the APPLY Board process for command, leadership, and senior officer non-command assignments.
2. Eligible officers desiring a billet assignment are required to complete and submit all applicable sections of the APPLY application. Applications must be submitted via the Reserve Forces Manpower Tools (RFMT) website available at https://rfmt.private.navyreserve.navy.mil/. Officers are encouraged to place the maximum number of billet choices on their dream sheets to increase the opportunity for assignment. Additionally, officers are highly encouraged to note their willingness to accept suggested billets along with any travel limitations for billets not listed on their dream sheets as doing so will enable the board to select the best qualified applicants.
3. All correspondence to the board must be received no later than 2359 Central Daylight Time (CDT), 30 July 2026. Correspondence received after 2359 CDT on 30 July 2026 will not be accepted and will not be considered by the board. Submission methods are detailed in paragraph 5.
4. The July 2026 O6 Fitness Report (FITREP) will be accepted until 2359 CDT on 4 August 2026 and must be submitted as an enclosure to a Letter to The Board (LTB) addressed to the APPLY board president. Enclosures other than the July 2026 O6 FITREP submitted after the regular correspondence deadline in line with paragraph 3, will not be considered by the board.
5. Board correspondence can be submitted via one of the following methods (listed from most to least preferred method): a. Electronic Submission of Selection Board Documents (ESSBD) is the preferred method to submit a LTB in accordance with reference (b). Candidates must access the document services application through MyNavy Portal at https://mn3p.navy.mil/web/advancement-and-promotion/overview or the BUPERS Online main menu. If required, the ESSBD users guide is available on the MyNavy HR selection board support web page at https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/Career-Management/Boards/Selection-Board-Support/. If unable to submit an LTB package using ESSBD (e.g., low bandwidth, system outage, etc.), candidates should submit via one of the below alternative methods. b. E-mail to hrscselboard@us.navy.mil. Subject line of the e-mail should read: FY27 Navy Reserve National Command and Senior Officer APPLY Board (#391) ICO (Rank First Name Last Name, Designator). c. Standard (U.S. Postal Service) mail, if encrypted e-mail is not available, to: MyNavy Career Center FY27 Navy Reserve National Command and Senior Officer APPLY Board (#391) 5720 Integrity Drive Bldg 768, Room E302 Millington, TN 38055-9200 d. Overnight/Express Mail requiring signature: MyNavy Career Center FY27 Navy Reserve National Command and Senior Officer APPLY Board (#391) 5640 Ticonderoga Loop Millington, TN 38055-9200
e. Applicants may contact MyNavy Career Center to confirm receipt of their package and ask questions concerning supplemental/additional correspondence at 833-330-MNCC/833-330-6622, 901-874-MNCC/901-874-6622 or via e-mail at askmncc@us.navy.mil.
6. A SELRES officer must be qualified in line with reference (a) to be considered for an assignment. A qualified officer must complete and submit a dream sheet in RFMT containing at least one billet listed on their application to be considered by the APPLY board for a billet.
7. An APPLY eligible officer who is not affiliated with the Reserve Component in a drilling status (e.g., Active Status Pool/Voluntary Training Unit, on Vacant Billet Active Duty for Operational Support (VB-ADOS) formally Recall, Planned Support-Active Duty for Operational Support (PS-ADOS), and Mobilization) is still required to complete and submit a dream sheet to participate in the APPLY board. Non-affiliated officers and any officers experiencing difficulty submitting a dream sheet within RFMT, should contact Commander, Navy Reserve Forces Command (COMNAVRESFORCOM) N122 at seniorofficerapplycnrfc@us.navy.mil.
8. APPLY board assignments are assumed to be accepted by selected officers, whereby no further action is required. Officers who desire to decline awarded billets must do so within 7 days following release of official results via the RFMT website.
9. Point of contact. All APPLY questions or concerns should be addressed to COMNAVRESFORCOM (N122) APPLY Team via e-mail at: seniorofficerapplycnrfc@us.navy.mil.
10. This NAVADMIN will remain in effect until superseded or canceled, whichever occurs first.
11. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 102/26 - HEALTH OF THE FORCE SURVEY
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 1201333-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 301333Z APR 26 MID120041222428U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 102/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/APR//
SUBJ/HEALTH OF THE FORCE SURVEY//
RMKS/1. The Navy's Health of the Force (HOF) Survey will open for data collection on 15 May 2026 and remain open through 30 June 2026 to increase participation and ensure results accurately reflect key demographic groups and communities. Request all Active Duty and Reserve Component Sailors participate in this voluntary and confidential survey. It takes about 20 minutes to complete and can be accessed on military or personal computers and on mobile devices at: https://usnavy.gov1.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_8HOVNyL1XHhlT1Q .
2. Although participation in the survey is optional, a best practice is to incorporate the survey into daily battle rhythms or duty section trainings, to encourage Sailor participation at every level.
3. To access the survey, participants will be asked to provide their Department of Defense identification number. This information is only used to limit participation to Navy Service Members and to minimize unnecessary demographic questions. At no point will Navy link answers back to the individual.
4. HOF data is critical to our understanding of key personnel issues that directly influence warfighter effectiveness, retention, and readiness. With sufficient participation, results are available at the Unit Identification Code level. Data can also be rolled up for Immediate Superior in Command and to support Type command/Systems Command analytics.
5. Many leaders from across the Navy already have access to the interactive HOF dashboard from last year. To access, simply refer to the HOF dashboard link on the Navy Survey webpage. If, for some reason, you do not have access to the dashboard, there is a form on the website for requesting access.
6. Point of contact for any questions about the survey or the HOF dashboard is Dr. Jennifer Jebo, Office of Warfighter Experience, OPNAV N176 at jennifer.l.jebo.civ@us.navy.mil.
7. This NAVADMIN will remain in effect until 1 August 2026.
8. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 100/26 - C-NOte #7: GOLDEN FLEET INITIATIVE AND OUR FUTURE FLEET DESIGN
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 1191917-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 291917Z APR 26 MID120041219183U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 100/26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/CNO/APR//
SUBJ/C-NOte #7: GOLDEN FLEET INITIATIVE AND OUR FUTURE FLEET DESIGN//
RMKS/1. In December 2025, the President announced the Golden Fleet Initiative (GFI) - an unmistakable statement of America's resolve and commitment to maritime superiority. GFI is our strategic approach to deliver greater combat capability and capacity to our warfighters at speed and scale. Built on three pillars - enhancing maritime dominance, revitalizing the industrial base, and reforming business practices - GFI becomes the foundation of our Future Fleet Design by strengthening how we build in the Foundry, generate readiness in the Fleet, and prevail in the Fight.
2. As outlined in the U.S. Navy Fighting Instructions, our differentiated value to the Nation is the ability to project sovereign combat power from the sea - globally, persistently, and on our terms. That advantage is being challenged. Adversaries are investing in long-range fires, sensing networks, and systems designed to hold us at risk. GFI ensures we retain the mass, lethality, and adaptability required to deter aggression and, if necessary, win decisively.
3. The Fiscal Year 2027 budget advances this effort by funding 34 ships and investing in the Future Fleet Design - from the new battleship BBG(X) and frigates FFG(X) to additional submarines and destroyers. We remain committed to carrier strike groups and stronger amphibious capabilities. This reflects a deliberate high-low mix of tailorable forces that enables both high-end combat and day-to-day global operations.
4. Mass matters - but only when it is lethal, ready, and sustainable. We are building a Navy that can generate decisive combat power, not merely compete. GFI requires a balanced mix of exquisite and attritable platforms, crewed and uncrewed systems, and a battle force that can distribute, reaggregate, and strike with precision. Distribution is not dispersion - it is synchronized combat power that complicates enemy decisions and imposes cost.
5. Capability must arrive at the speed of war - readiness cannot be surged. The Foundry must deliver relevant ships, payloads, and networks faster. The Fleet must absorb and employ them with urgency and discipline. The Fight demands commanders who act decisively under the Enhanced Mission Command Framework and manage risk with clarity and confidence.
6. For Sailors, this is tangible. You will receive capability faster. You will operate more reliable and lethal systems. You will integrate with unmanned platforms and advanced networks that extend your reach and awareness. Payloads will evolve faster than the platforms that carry them and you will serve aboard ships built to win, protect your shipmates, and deliver overwhelming effects under fire.
7. In the next fight, expectations will rise. Communications will be contested and decision timelines compressed. You must be ready to think, decide, and act at speed. Competence and trust at every level will determine whether we seize and sustain the advantage. Above all, warfighting fundamentals built on sound maintenance, alert watchstanding, and tactical proficiency - remain non-negotiable.
8. As we build our future fleet, we must find the right mix of tailored forces and tailored offsets, coupled with the general-purpose force to match mission requirements and provide surge capability when required. It must deliver readiness today and enable decisive overmatch tomorrow. This balance is how we generate credible combat forces at scale and sustain advantage over time.
9. Working together with Congress and Industry, the Navy's GFI will deliver a Future Fleet Design that is ready, resilient, and lethal - capable of sustaining operations in degraded and denied environments, absorbing losses, and striking the enemy hard and fast.
10. Our objective is clear: out-think, out-maneuver, and out-fight any adversary. GFI strengthens how we build, train, and fight as a naval force. This is how we deliver peace through strength. This is how we bring to bear our differentiated value to the Nation. This is how we win.
11. Built in the Foundry - Tempered in the Fleet - Forged to Fight.
12. ADM Daryl Caudle, 34th Chief of Naval Operations sends.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 101/26 - NAVY UNIFORM UPDATES TO NAVY WORKING UNIFORM AND SERVICE DRESS BLUES
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 1192149-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 292149Z APR 26 MID120041219656U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 101/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/APR//
SUBJ//NAVY UNIFORM UPDATES TO NAVY WORKING UNIFORM AND SERVICE DRESS BLUES//
REF/A/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/171631ZAUG23// REF/B/DOC/COMNAVPERSCOM/23AUG21// REF/C/DOC/COMNAVDIST WASHINGTON DC/16APR25//
NARR/REF A IS NAVADMIN 197/23, NAVY WORKING UNIFORM TYPE III WEAR IN THE NATIONAL CAPITAL REGION. REF B IS NAVPERS 15665J, U.S. NAVY UNIFORM REGULATIONS. REF C IS NAVAL DISTRICT WASHINGTON INSTRUCTION 1020.6C, UNIFORM POLICY AND REGULATIONS INSTRUCTION.//
RMKS/1. These uniform changes are being made as discussed in C-NOte #1 (Sailors First) as an initial step in our assessment and modifications of our uniforms. This NAVADMIN expands Service Dress Blue (SDB) wear and authorizes the wear of the Navy Working Uniform (NWU) Type III within the Pentagon augmenting reference (a). Uniform regulations for the National Capital Region (NCR) outside of the Pentagon per references (b), and (c) remain unchanged.
2. SDB Wear. Effective immediately, wear of the SDB Jacket or Eisenhower jacket for E-7 and above in office buildings or work spaces is optional throughout the fleet. This allows walking within the confines of a building without the SDB Jacket or Eisenhower jacket covering the white shirt and tie. Additionally, the black garrison cap is authorized as an acceptable substitute for the combination cover for wear with the SDB coat or Eisenhower jacket. This will allow individuals flexibility what to wear when working in an office setting while wearing SDBs. Additionally, it allows an individual to determine which cover makes the most sense for the situation.
3. NWU Type III Wear in the Pentagon. In coordination with the Acting Secretary of the Navy (SECNAV), effective 4 May 2026, NWU Type III wear is authorized in the Pentagon. This change is being made to ensure the Navy is aligned with the uniforms being worn in the Pentagon by other members of the Joint Force and in an effort to continue to instill a warrior ethos in our Sailors. Personnel shall adhere to the following while wearing NWU Type IIIs within the Pentagon and those assigned to headquarters staffs within the NCR. a. NWU Type III wear is authorized when a uniform is worn commuting to and from work using privately owned vehicles, government conveyance, public commuter transportation (metro bus, metro rail, rideshare, commuter vans, taxis, and ferries), and slug lines outside the Pentagon. Wear is also authorized in office environments at headquarters staffs within the NCR unless otherwise directed. b. Flight suits are not included in this NWU Type III authorization. Flight suits are only authorized in the NCR for personnel in an actual flight duty status for that day. c. NWU Type IIIs worn in the Pentagon shall be in good serviceable condition. Uniform items that are unserviceable, specifically those exhibiting tears, excessive fading, or visible damage do not meet established appearance standards and are not authorized for wear within the Pentagon. d. For members assigned to Joint, Office of the Secretary of War and other non-Department of the Navy organizations within the Pentagon, the prescribed uniform of the day will be per your respective leadership's policy. e. Additionally, Service Dress Uniforms remain required for duty (e.g., meetings, conferences, etc.) outside the Pentagon (e.g., Capitol Hill engagements).
4. Retain this NAVADMIN until policy changes are incorporated in reference (b), superseded or canceled, whichever occur first.
5. Released by Mr. Matt Swartz, Director of the Navy Staff.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ ROUTINE R 272025Z APR 26 MID180038733927U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 099/26
SUBJ/FY26 PROJECTED SHIP INACTIVATION SCHEDULE (UPDATE #2)//
REF/A/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/ 121421Z SEP 25// REF/B/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/ 201948Z FEB 26// REF/C/DOC/OPNAVINST 5400.44B/20230419// REF/D/DOC/OPNAVINST 4770.5J/20210904//
NARR/REFERENCE A IS NAVADMIN 195/25, FY26 PROJECTED SHIP INACTIVATION SCHEDULE, WHICH LISTS NON-CONTENTIOUS SHIPS PRIOR TO CONGRESS PASSING FY26 AUTHORIZATION AND APPROPRIATIONS ACTS. REFERENCE B IS NAVADMIN 034/26, FY26 PROJECTED SHIP INACTIVATION SCHEDULE (UPDATED COPY) FOLLOWING PASSAGE OF FY26 AUTHORIZATION AND APPROPRIATIONS ACTS. REFERENCE C IS NAVY ORGANIZATION CHANGE MANUAL (NOCM) FOR SUBMITTING ORGANIZATIONAL CHANGE REQUESTS (OCR) TO INCLUDE SHIP DECOMMISSIONINGS OR INACTIVATIONS. REFERENCE D IS OPNAV POLICY AND GUIDANCE FOR THE INACTIVATION, RETIREMENT, AND DISPOSITION OF U.S. NAVAL VESSELS. //
POC/ROGER RABER/CIV/N9IS/LOC: WASHINGTON DC/TEL: 703-693-9547/EMAIL: ROGER.W.RABER.CIV(AT)US.NAVY.MIL//
RMKS/1. This message supersedes references (a) and (b) and shall be read in its entirety to ensure all stakeholders in the ship inactivation process are aware of the projected retirement schedule for fiscal year 2026 (FY26), their respective responsibilities, necessary follow-up actions, and planning required.
2. To facilitate fleet planning efforts to conduct decommissioning continuous maintenance availability (CMAV) or inactivation availability (INAC), the projected schedule for inactivating U.S. battle force and non-battle force naval vessels in FY26 is promulgated as follows:
Ship Name Proj Inactive Date Post Inactive Status USS NEWPORT NEWS (SSN 750) 31JAN26 RECYCLE USS ALEXANDRIA (SSN 757) 04AUG26 RECYCLE USS GEORGIA (SSGN 729) 31JUL26 RECYCLE USS SHILOH (CG 67) 30SEP26 LSA USS LAKE ERIE (CG 70) 30SEP26 LSA USS FORT WORTH (LCS 3) 31JUL26 DISMANTLE USS GERMANTOWN (LSD 42) 29SEP26 LSA USNS RED CLOUD (T-AKR 313) 30SEP26 XFER TO MARAD USNS WATKINS (T-AKR 315) 01JUL26 XFER TO MARAD USNS POMEROY (T-AKR 316) 01APR26 XFER TO MARAD USNS VADM K. R. WHEELER (T-AG-5001) 01JUL26 XFER TO MARAD USNS JOHN ERICSSON (T-AO 194) 31JUL26 LSA USNS PECOS (T-AO 197) 31JUL26 XFER TO MARAD USNS BIG HORN (T-AO 198) 31MAR26 XFER TO MARAD
3. Per reference (c), Fleet Commanders shall submit an Organizational Change Request for commissioned U.S. ships to formally notify the Office of the Chief of Naval Operations (OPNAV) of a ship's decommissioning, inactivation, or end of service. Submit revisions due to operational schedule changes per references (c) and (d). It is the responsibility of Commander, U.S. Fleet Forces Command and Commander, U.S. Pacific Fleet in coordination with their respective TYCOM to ensure the appropriate lower echelon commands are notified of any changes in the ship inactivation schedules, as well as Integrated Warfare (OPNAV N9I) and OPNAV resource sponsor.
4. Adjustments to paragraph 2 ship inactivation's that cross the current fiscal year must be coordinated with OPNAV N9I due to Congressional requirements for execution year force structure changes that differ from what Congress authorized/appropriated and signed into law by the President. OPNAV shall promulgate changes to the inactivation fiscal year as required.
5. Per reference (d), the ships listed in paragraph 2 as Logistics Support Assets (LSA) will be used as a primary means of cannibalization and equipment removal for ships. Cannibalizations and equipment removal from these vessels are authorized without replacement.
6. The ship's commanding officer, masters, or Immediate Superior in Command, shall submit a final naval message (normally transmitted in conjunction with the decommissioning ceremony) announcing the ship's official retirement date and include a brief history of the significant events in the life of the ship per reference (d). The Naval History and Heritage Command (NAVHISTHERITAGE WASHINGTON DC) and Naval Vessel Register Custodian (NVR NORFOLK VA), shall be included as INFO addresses.
7. Released by RADM M. D. Behning, Acting Deputy Chief of Naval Operations for Warfighting Requirements and Capabilities (N9).
