ASHEVILLE USS EMORY S LAND USS FRANK CABLE USS JEFFERSON CITY USS MINNESOTA USS SPRINGFIELD BT UNCLAS
SUBJ/EXCERCISE EXCERCISE EXCERCISE ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY IN CONJUNCTION WITH GUAM TYPHOON EX26
NAVADMIN 082-26//
MSGID/GENADMIN/COMPACFLT PEARL HARBOR HI/N1/APR//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAVINST 3006.1/01MAR2011// REF/B/DOC/DODINST 3001.02/03MAY2010// REF/C/DOC/DODINST 1015.15/20MAR2008// REF/D/DOC/CNICINST 3006.1/25FEB2026// REF/E//NAVSOP-1000/12DEC2002// REF/F/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171821ZMAR09//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 3006.1, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH CATASTROPHIC EVENTS, NAVY PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY POLICY. REF B IS DODINST 3001.02, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH NATURAL OR MAN-MADE DISASTERS. REF C IS DODINST 1015.15, PROCEDURES AND SUPPORT FOR NON-FEDERAL ENTITIES AUTHORIZED TO OPERATE ON DOD INSTALLATIONS (ENCL 4). REF D IS CNICINST 3006.1, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY UTILIZING NAVY FAMILY ACCOUNTABILITY AND ASSESSMENT SYSTEM POLICY AND RESPONSIBILITIES REF E IS FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT POLICY MANUAL. REF F IS ALARACT 070/2009, CONUS EVACUATION ALLOWANCES.
RMKS/1. THIS ORDER TO ACCOUNT APPLIES TO ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, NAF EMPLOYEES, NEX EMPLOYEES, OVERSEAS CONTRACTORS, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS WITHIN THE ESTABLISHED GEOGRAPHIC AREA OF INTEREST (GAOI) DESIGNATED IN PARAGRAPH 2. ECHELON II COMMANDERS AND ALL REGIONAL COMMANDERS SHALL ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION TO ALL SUBORDINATE AND TENANT COMMANDS AND DETACHMENTS FOR IMMEDIATE ACTION UPON RECEIPT.
2. THE FOLLOWING GAOI HAS BEEN ESTABLISHED FOR GUAM TYPHOON EX26: ALL U.S. TERRITORY OF GUAM AND SAIPAN.
3. ALL COMMANDS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. ENSURE YOUR COMMAND'S NAVY FAMILY ACCOUNTABILITY AND ASSESSMENT SYSTEM (NFAAS) DATABASE IS CURRENT AND ACCURATE, INCLUDING ALL PHONE NUMBERS AND ADDRESSES. ENSURE ANY RECENTLY CHECKED-IN PERSONNEL WHOSE CONTACT INFORMATION HAS NOT YET BEEN UPDATED IN NFAAS PROVIDE RECALL INFORMATION THROUGH THEIR RESPECTIVE CHAIN OF COMMAND UNTIL THEIR RECORDS ARE UPDATED. ADDITIONALLY, ENSURE DESIGNATED COR(S) ARE PROPERLY ASSIGNED IN NFAAS FOR EACH OF YOUR COMMAND UICS. B. FOLLOWING STORM PASSAGE OR CLOSEST POINT OF APPROACH (CPA), COMMANDS MUST MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE PERSONNEL, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, NAF EMPLOYEES, NEX EMPLOYEES, OVERSEAS CONTRACTORS, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS WITHIN THE GAOI. ALL COR(S)/SPONSORS IN THE AFFECTED GAOI WILL RECEIVE NOTIFICATION FROM NFAAS VIA TEXT OR EMAIL TO MUSTER THEMSELVES AND THEIR DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS. CO(S) AND/OR COR(S) WILL ENSURE THE WIDEST DISSEMINATION OF THE REQUIREMENT FOR ACCURATE AND TIMELY ACCOUNTABILITY VIA NFAAS. IF PERSONNEL ARE UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, CO(S)/COR(S) WILL DETERMINE THE ACCURATE STATUS AND WHEREABOUTS OF THEIR PERSONNEL AND MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR THEM AS AT HOME, AT WORK, TELEWORKING, EVACUATED, DISPLACED, OR NOT IN THE GAOI WITH THE SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED, TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/ RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OR OTHER). C. COMMANDS SHALL DIRECT COR(S)/SPONSORS TO MUSTER IN NFAAS AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL/. IF NFAAS IS UNAVAILABLE, COMMANDS AND MEMBERS SHALL REPORT PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY (PA) STATUS TO THE AFFECTED REGION OPERATIONS CENTER (ROC) OR THROUGH THEIR DIRECT CHAIN OF COMMAND. D. DO NOT DELAY REPORTING PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY UNTIL ALL INFORMATION IS COMPLETE. REPORT MUSTERING INFORMATION AS IT IS OBTAINED, AND UPDATE ACCORDINGLY. INCLUDE ANY AMPLIFYING INFORMATION IN THE NOTES FIELD OF THE INDIVIDUALS ACCOUNTING LINE IN NFAAS.
4. ALL COR(S)/SPONSORS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. ACCOUNT FOR THEMSELVES AND THEIR DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL/. B. IF UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, SPONSORS SHALL CONTACT THEIR COR, COMMAND MUSTERING OFFICER, OR SUPERVISOR TO CHECK IN (MUSTER). C. SPONSORS SHALL PROVIDE COMMAND REPRESENTATIVES WITH THEIR CURRENT RESIDENCE, DISPLACED LOCATION, OR, IF NOT IN THE GAOI, THEIR SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED, TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OTHER).
5. NEEDS ASSESSMENT HAS NOT BEEN INITIATED.
6. FOR NFAAS TECHNICAL SUPPORT, CONTACT THE NFAAS HELP DESK AT 866-946-9183 OR VIA EMAIL AT NFAAS(AT)NAVY.MIL.
7. IF UNABLE TO MUSTER VIA NFAAS, YOUR COR, YOUR MUSTERING POC, OR YOUR CHAIN OF COMMAND, CONTACT THE BELOW POCS (LOCATION/PHONE/EMAIL):
A. JOINT REGION MARIANAS BATTLEWATCH/671-349-4004/ROCBWT.JRM.FCT(AT)FE.NAVY.MIL. B. CJRM REGIONAL PA REP/671-349-4155/JONATHAN.C.RYAN.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL. C. NAVAL BASE GUAM EOC/671-488-7147/NBGCDO(AT)FE.NAVY.MIL. D. CNIC BWC/202-433-4321/CNIC.N36.BWC(AT)NAVY.MIL. E. PACFLT CAT BWC/808-474-5452/5453/CPF.CATBWC(AT)NAVY.MIL. F. NFAAS PROGRAM MGRS/202-433-0763/ETHAN.T.AUDET.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL OR DOUGLAS.AGUILARQUEZADA.CIV(AT)US.NAVY.MIL./202-765-6011.
7. RELEASED BY CAPT KEITH ARCHIBALD, N1B, U.S. PACIFIC FLEET/808-471-0470/KEITH.ARCHIBALD.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL.//
BT #0002 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 079/26 - 2026 ASSOCIATION OF OLD CROWS OUTSTANDING NAVY UNIT AWARD NOMINATIONS
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0961602-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 061602Z APR 26 MID320018627007U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 079/26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N2N6/APR//
SUBJ/2026 ASSOCIATION OF OLD CROWS OUTSTANDING NAVY UNIT AWARD NOMINATIONS//
REF/A/DOC/AOC/09FEB2026// AMPN/REF A IS THE AOC LETTER TO CNO FOR ANNUAL AWARD NOMINATIONS.//
POC/BIXBY/CTTCM/OPNAV N2N6WT/TEL:(571)256-8542/EMAIL:GARY.W.BIXBY2.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN solicits for the annual Association of Old Crows (AOC) Outstanding Navy Unit Award nominations. Per reference (a), the AOC awards one Navy unit from each of three categories (air/sea/shore) that have shown significant contributions in Electromagnetic Warfare (EW) and Information Warfare/Cyber Warfare.
2. Nominations should outline specific contributions made by each unit. All nominations must be submitted using the AOC Community Awards submission form located online at the EW/ELINT Community INTELINK documents page (https://intelshare.intelink.gov/sites/ctt/SitePages/Home.aspx). Contact POC via email for question. Endorsement letters will be accepted as attachments. Classified supplements may also be submitted via GENSER email.
3. Email nominations via Echelon II Command to Office of the Chief of Naval Operations (OPNAV) point of contact (POC) NLT COB Friday, 31 July 2026. Echelon II commands may submit a total of three nominations, no more than one per award category. ISICs should make every effort to submit at least one unit to their Type Commander taking into account EW readiness.
4. Thank you for recognizing our best warfighters.
5. Released by Ms. Jennifer Edgin, Acting Deputy Chief of Naval Operations for Information Warfare, OPNAV N2N6.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 078/26 - FIELDING OF NAVY ADVANCED ANTICONVULSANT SYSTEM (AAS) FOR NERVE AGENT TREATMENT
item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0931702-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 031702Z APR 26 MID180038693049U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 078/26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N9/APR//
SUBJ/FIELDING OF NAVY ADVANCED ANTICONVULSANT SYSTEM (AAS) FOR NERVE AGENT TREATMENT//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAV/24APR25// REF/B/DOC/JRO/24AUG07// REF/C/DOC/NWDC/16APR19// REF/D/DOC/OPNAV/2APR24// REF/E/DOC/BUMED/17APR23//
NARR/ REF A IS OPNAVINST 3400.10J, CHEMICAL, BIOLOGICAL, RADIOLOGICAL, AND NUCLEAR DEFENSE REQUIREMENTS SUPPORTING OPERATIONAL READINESS. REF B IS ADVANCED NERVE AGENT COUNTERMEASURES CAPABILITY DEVELOPMENT DOCUMENT. REF C IS NTRP 4-02.22, MULTI-SERVICE TACTICS, TECHNIQUES, AND PROCEDURES FOR TREATMENT OF CHEMICAL WARFARE AGENT CASUALTIES AND CONVENTIONAL MILITARY CHEMICAL INJURIES. REF D IS OPNAVINST 6700.3, MANAGEMENT OF MEDICAL AND DENTAL ASSEMBLAGE MATERIEL FOR NAVAL EXPEDITIONARY HEALTH SERVICE SUPPORT. REF E IS MANUAL OF THE MEDICAL DEPARTMENT - ARTICLE 21-5.
POC/DR LILIYA SILAYEVA/OPNAV ARLINGTON VA/(571)256-8033/EMAIL: LILIYA.P.SILAYEVA.CTR@US.NAVY.MIL// POC/LCDR SAMSON OYELAMI/FLEET FORCES COMMAND NORFOLK VA/(757)836-0107/EMAIL: SAMSON.F.OYELAMI.MIL@US.NAVY.MIL//
RMKS/1. PER REFERENCES (A) THROUGH (E), THIS MESSAGE OUTLINES THE PROCESS FOR REQUISITIONING THE ADVANCED ANTICONVULSANT SYSTEM (AAS) FOR THE FLEET AND EXPEDITIONARY FORCE TO REPLACE THE LEGACY CONVULSANT ANTIDOTE FOR NERVE AGENT (CANA).
2. AS THE EXECUTIVE AGENT (EA) FOR CHEMICAL, BIOLOGICAL, RADIOLOGICAL, AND NUCLEAR DEFENSE (CBRND), OPNAV N96 COORDINATES THE INITIAL FIELDING OF CBRN MEDICAL COUNTERMEASURES THROUGH THE JOINT PROGRAM EXECUTIVE OFFICE FOR CBRND (JPEO-CBRND) AT NO COST TO THE NAVY, PER REFERENCE (A). AAS, DESIGNED TO REPLACE CANA, IS A SINGLE-CHAMBER AUTOINJECTOR THAT DELIVERS 10 MG OF MIDAZOLAM FOR THE TREATMENT OF SEIZURES CAUSED BY EXPOSURE TO NERVE AGENTS, PER REFERENCE (B). IN CONDITIONS DICTATED BY REFERENCE (C), UNITS WILL ISSUE EACH SERVICE MEMBER ONE AAS, WHICH THEY WILL CARRY TO BE ADMINISTERED BY BUDDY-AID FOR USE IN THE PREVENTION AND TREATMENT OF SEIZURES FROM NERVE AGENT POISONING.
3. ADMINISTRATIVE ACTIONS. THE FOLLOWING ADMINISTRATIVE ACTIONS ARE REQUIRED IN SUPPORT OF THE TRANSITION FROM CANA TO AAS: A. ADDITION OF AAS TO THE APPROVED AMAL: PER REFERENCE (D), ENSURE AAS IS CAPTURED IN EACH UNIT'S TABLES OF ALLOWANCE (TOA) AND CROSS-REFERENCED TO A COMPLETE ASSEMBLAGE LIST MAINTAINED WITHIN THE NAVAL ASSEMBLAGE INFORMATION LOGISTICS SYSTEM (NAILS), MEDICAL ASSEMBLAGE METADATA SUITE (MAMS), OR THE MEDICAL CONTINGENCY REQUIREMENTS WORKFLOW (MCRW) MANAGED BY DEFENSE LOGISTICS AGENCY (DLA) - FUNCTIONAL EXECUTIVE AGENT MEDICAL SUPPORT. THE NSN FOR THIS ISSUANCE OF NAVY AAS IS 6505-31-019-3788/NDC 71053-595-01 (480 EA). FUTURE REQUISITIONS FULFILLED THROUGH DLA-TROOP SUPPORT (TS) (AFTER THE PRODUCT HAS ENTERED THE SUSTAINMENT PHASE - I.E., AT COST TO NAVY) WILL HAVE THE FOLLOWING NSNS: 6505-31-018-8242/ NDC 71053-0595-02 U/I (40 BOX) AND 6505-31-019-0519/ NDC 71053-0595-03 U/I (1 EA). B. COMPLIANCE WITH REGULATORY ACCOUNTABILITY FOR SCHEDULE III, IV, AND V CONTROLLED SUBSTANCES: PER REFERENCE (E), ENSURE APPROPRIATE HANDLING, STORAGE, INITIAL INVENTORY, AND REGULAR AUDITS OF INVENTORY OF AAS. C. USFF IS FORMULATING DISPOSAL REQUIREMENTS AND PROCEDURES FOR LEGACY CANA INVENTORY, WHICH OPNAV WILL PROMULGATE VIA SEPARATE MESSAGE.
4. REQUISITION OF AAS: UNITS WILL COORDINATE WITH OPNAV N96 AND USFF MEDLOG POCS FOR REQUISITION APPROVAL BEFORE ROUTING TO DLA-TS FOR FINAL EXECUTION AND RELEASE FOR SHIPMENT. ADDITIONAL QUANTITIES REQUIRED MUST BE COORDINATED THROUGH OPNAV N96 UNTIL AAS BECOMES AVAILABLE FOR PURCHASE THROUGH DLA-TS LATER IN CY26.
5. RELEASED BY RADM MARK D. BEHNING, ACTING DEPUTY CHIEF OF NAVAL OPERATIONS, WARFIGHTING REQUIREMENTS AND CAPABILITIES (N9).// BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 076/26 - NOTICE OF CONVENING FY-27-2 TEST PILOT SCHOOL SELECTION BOARD
item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ ROUTINE R 020108Z APR 26 MID320018618883U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 076/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/MAR//
SUBJ/NOTICE OF CONVENING FY-27-2 TEST PILOT SCHOOL SELECTION BOARD//
REF/A/DOC/BUPERS/06JAN21// NARR/REF A IS BUPERSINST 1500.62F, TEST PILOT SCHOOLS//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces convening of the U.S. Naval Test Pilot School (USNTPS) Selection Board on 18 June 2026. This board will select highly qualified Naval Aviators and Naval Flight Officers (NFO) for USNTPS. It will also select one fixed-wing pilot to attend the French Test Pilot School, Ecole du Personnel Navigant d'Essais et de Reception (EPNER)and one rotary-wing pilot for Empire Test Pilot School (ETPS). TPS graduates have significant and long-lasting influence on platforms and systems which directly impact the future warfighting capability of the Fleet. Request command and Fleet community assistance to identify candidates with strong operational performance and ideally, strong academic backgrounds in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics fields of study.
2. To be eligible for board consideration, a signed command-endorsed application in accordance with reference (a), must be received by MyNavy Career Center (MNCC) no later than 15 May 2026. Applicants are encouraged to review reference (a) in detail to ensure their package complies with application guidelines. Applicants with missing required information may be removed from consideration. Additional documentation (including Wing/CAG endorsements, letters of recommendation, FITREPs, etc.) will be accepted until 5 June 2026, but must be submitted by the applicant, as a letter to the board. The e-mail address(es), phone number(s), official college transcripts, Anthropometric Data Measurement Record, a current PRT and BCA Detail Report from PRIMS-2 (subparagraph 8h), last FITREP, service agreement, and post-TPS test squadron assignment preferences of the applicant must be included with the application. Current contact information (ashore/afloat email and phone) of Commanding Officer must also be included to expedite notification, if selected.
3. Typical post-TPS assignments for graduates are listed below and are subject to change. Post-TPS assignments are typically finalized halfway through the TPS course of instruction. a. VX-20: VP, VQ, VAW, VRC b. HX-21: All Helo, VRM c. VX-23 / VX-31: VFA, VAQ d. VX-30: VP, VAW, VFA, VAQ e. UX-24: All communities
4. The command endorsement shall explicitly state whether the applicant, if selected, will be allowed and available to report for aircraft pre-arrival training by the following dates: a. Fixed-wing Pilots: (1) 1 August 2026 (Preferred) (2) 1 September 2026 (Required) b. Tiltrotor Pilots: 1 October 2026 c. Rotary Wing Pilots: 15 October 2026 d. NFOs or UAS operators: 1 December 2026 Endorsements with availability dates after these respective dates may be removed from consideration and not reviewed by the board.
Inability for the applicant to meet the above dates may be mitigated by conducting initial training in a Temporary Additional Duty (TEMADD) status at the expense of the endorsing command. If this is required, it must be explicitly stated in the command endorsement that the endorsing command will provide TEMADD funds until the applicant can officially detach. If the applicant has official change of duty orders and is projected to transfer prior to the board convening date of 18 June 2026, a gaining command endorsement is required in addition to the endorsement from the applicant's current command.
5. USNTPS has a cooperative program with Purdue University wherein Purdue teaches asynchronous online pre-arrival courses which serve as a technical refresher and provide 4 credits towards a master's degree. Candidates selected for USNTPS will receive directions for immediate enrollment in Purdue Online within two weeks of selection board results. Required pre-arrival coursework on foundational subjects (e.g., calculus and mechanics) will commence online on 24 August 2026 and will run concurrent to the preparatory aircraft training described above, dependent on the applicable timeline. In cases where the pre-arrival online courses precede aircraft training, candidates are expected to complete the online courses while still attached to their current command. If operational conflicts exist which prevent Purdue Online course registration and pre-arrival course completion, selected candidates should contact USNTPS promptly to discuss contingencies.
6. Application package submissions: a. The preferred method of application submission is electronic. E-mail encrypted PDF submissions to cscselboard@navy.mil and carbon copy richard.s.westerfield.mil@us.navy.mil and brandy.l.dempster-theisen.ctr@us.navy.mil. If required, please visit https://dod411.gds.disa.mil/ to download the required mailbox certificates to encrypt the email. As a backup, applications may be submitted via DODSAFE. If unable to send encrypted through either of the above options, please contact richard.s.westerfield.mil@us.navy.mil and brandy.l.dempster-theisen.ctr@us.navy.mil for assistance. b. If unable to send electronically, applications may be sent via U.S. Postal Service to the following address:
MyNavy Career Center FY-27-2 Test Pilot School Selection Board (Board #380) 5720 Integrity Drive Millington, TN 38055
c. Applications sent via FEDEX/UPS/DHL should use the following address:
MyNavy Career Center FY-27-2 Test Pilot School Selection Board (Board #380) 5640 Ticonderoga Loop, BLDG 768, RM E302 Millington, TN 38055-6300
7. Applicants should confirm receipt of their application package and all correspondence by calling MNCC at 1-833-330-MNCC or DSN 882-6622.
8. Eligibility: a. Designated Naval Aviators and NFOs (13X0 and 1510 designators) through the grade of Lieutenant Commander (O4) who are qualified for flight duties and have not been terminated from flying status are eligible for selection. b. The preferred unrestricted line (URL, 13X0) aviators are O3 and O4 aviators with detailer forecasted career timing to complete the selected TPS program and a minimum 24-month follow on test tour prior to their next career milestone (e.g. Department Head). c. Aerospace Engineering Duty Officer (1510) aviators through the grade of lieutenant commander may be selected if they are able to serve a 36-month follow on test tour and still meet career milestones. d. Applicants determined to have insufficient career timing to meet the minimum test tour of 24 months may have their application removed from consideration. Applicants may address any timing considerations in their personal statement. e. Applicants should ensure they have a valid government passport that will not expire prior to completion of the class. f. USNTPS operates a diverse set of fixed-wing jet, propellor, and rotary-wing aircraft. Core aircraft include H-60, H-72, T-6, and T-38. T-6 and T-38 ejection seat weight limits are 103-245 lb. All applicants must be able report to USNTPS pre-arrival training within the above weight limits. Applicants which have been anthropometrically or otherwise disqualified from a specific aircraft type, class, model, or series must include such information as part of the application package. Applicants who currently exceed ejection seat weight limits or are disqualified from a type, class, model, or series aircraft which preclude safe operation of TPS core aircraft may have their application removed from consideration. g. Applicants must have a current Tier 5 investigation (T5) or Tier 5 reinvestigation (T5R) with eligibility for Top Secret/Sensitive Compartmented Information (TS/SCI) before transferring to this command. If the applicant does not have the aforementioned eligibility, then the command supporting the applicant will initiate a request for eligibility, requesting interim TS/SCI eligibility and ensuring the eligibility request been accepted and is being processed. Applicants unable to obtain a TS/SCI clearance may not be eligible for this assignment. h. Applicants are encouraged to have completed a calendar year 2026 (CY2026) Cycle-2 PRT (26-2: 1 July through 31 December) prior to reporting to pre arrival training.
9. Applicants should periodically check for updates and additional information via MyNavy HR at https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/Career-Management/Boards/Administrative/Test-Pilot/.
10. Points of contact are CDR Richard S. Westerfield, PERS-434B, via e-mail at richard.s.westerfield.mil@us.navy.mil or Ms. Brandy Theisen, PERS-434B1, via e-mail at brandy.l.dempster-theisen.ctr@us.navy.mil.
11. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 077/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2027 PERMANENT MILITARY PROFESSOR SELECTION BOARD
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0920130-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 020130Z APR 26 MID320018618996U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 077/26 FM CNO WASHINGTON DC //N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC///N1//
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/APR//
SUBJ/FISCAL YEAR 2027 PERMANENT MILITARY PROFESSOR SELECTION BOARD//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAV/01OCT21// AMPN/REF A IS OPNAVINST 1520.40C, PERMANENT MILITARY PROFESSOR AND PERMANENT MILITARY INSTRUCTOR PROGRAMS.//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces the Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 Permanent Military Professor (PMP) selection board for officers reporting to graduate school in August 2027. The FY27 PMP selection board may select up to a total of 14 PMPs from a pool of eligible Active Duty, Training and Administration of the Reserves, and Selected Reserve Officer applicants for assignment to the United States Naval Academy, Naval War College or Naval Postgraduate School (NPS). Applicants must be released by their current community.
2. Reference (a) provides specific application and eligibility guidance. Applications are due to the Out Service Education Placement and Special Fellowships Division (PERS-443), no later than 29 May 2026, submitted via encrypted transmission on DoD SAFE (https://safe.apps.mil) to PERS-440 (PERS440_submit.fct@navy.mil), and to LCDR Jenna Quist (jenna.m.quist.mil@us.navy.mil); or mailed to:
Navy Personnel Command (PERS-443) 5720 Integrity Drive Millington, TN 3855-4400
3. For requirements and a list of available academic disciplines, refer to the My Navy Education advertisement on the MyNavy HR website at: https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/Career-Management/Fellowships/PMP.
4. Officer Community Managers will review applicants for release based on their community health and needs. PERS-443 will review all PMP applicants to ensure eligibility guidelines have been met prior to releasing the applicant to the board.
5. Points of contact: a. PMP Community Manager is CDR Erik Moss, (901) 874-4511 or via e-mail at erik.e.moss.mil@us.navy.mil b. USNA PMP Lead is CAPT Dave Durkin, (410) 293-6635 or via email at durkin@usna.edu c. NPS PMP Lead is CAPT Warren Tomlinson, (831) 656-2877 or via email at wwtomlin@nps.edu d. NWC PMP Lead is CAPT Andrea Cameron, (401) 856-5320 or via email at andrea.cameron@usnwc.edu
6. This NAVADMIN will remain in effect until superseded or canceled, whichever occurs first.
7. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 071/26 - NAVY WHITE HOUSE SOCIAL AIDE CALL FOR APPLICANTS
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0911813-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 011813Z APR 26 MID320018617916U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//DNS
NAVADMIN 071/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/DNS/APR//
SUBJ/NAVY WHITE HOUSE SOCIAL AIDE CALL FOR APPLICANTS//
REF/A/DOC/WHMO Policy HQ18-004/19SEP18// REF/B/DOC/DODI 4515.14/14APR22//
NARR/REF A IS THE WHITE HOUSE MILITARY OFFICE POLICY DEFINING THE MILITARY SOCIAL AIDE PROGRAM. REF B IS THE DOD INSTRUCTION DEFINING THE WASHINGTON LOCAL COMMUTING AREA.//
RMKS/1. The Navy White House Military Social Aide Program is looking for talented Junior Naval Officers in the grade of O-2 to O-4 to serve as Social Aides at the White House, as established in reference (a).
2. The program consists of approximately 60 military officers from all branches. Social Aides fill a crucial role at the White House in assisting the President and First Lady of the United States as they host guests and dignitaries from around the world. Social Aides assist the President with diplomatic protocol at State events, meetings with the leaders of Congress and the Federal Judiciary, Medal of Honor ceremonies, and other significant social events.
3. Social Aides' role at the White House is in addition to their primary military duties. Eligible Officers meet the following requirements: a. Stationed at a command in the National Capital Region (NCR), as defined in reference (b), and including Anne Arundel County, MD b. Have at least 2 years remaining on NCR orders c. Have at least 18 months remaining before becoming eligible to promote to O-5 d. Hold a clearance of Secret or higher and eligible to pass Presidential Support Duty security screenings e. Receive full command endorsement
4. Expected time commitment includes: at least one (1) event per week throughout the year, roughly 40 hours over the first two weeks of December in support of the White House Holiday season, and the flexibility to support short notice requests (24-48 hours) at the White House.
5. Interested Officers are invited to contact LT Nikolas Marquez-Maya at Nikolas.marquezmaya.mil@us.navy.mil to request an application. Completed applications are due on 1MAY26. Favorably screened applicants can expect an in-person interview in the weeks following application submission.
6. Released by Vice Admiral Michael E. Boyle, Navy Staff.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 075/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2027 NAVY ACTIVE DUTY AND RESERVE COMPONENT ENLISTED ADVANCEMENT SELECTION AND SCREEN BOARDS FOR CHIEF PETTY OFFICER
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0912013-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 012013Z APR 26 MID320018618254U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 075/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/APR//
SUBJ/FISCAL YEAR 2027 NAVY ACTIVE DUTY AND RESERVE COMPONENT ENLISTED ADVANCEMENT SELECTION AND SCREEN BOARDS FOR CHIEF PETTY OFFICER//
REF/A/DOC/BUPERS/21JAN26// REF/B/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/221540ZDEC22// REF/C/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/201421ZNOV23// REF/D/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/251840ZJUN25 REF/E/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/102127ZDEC20// REF/F/DOC/MILPERSMAN/14JUN18// REF/G/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/271415ZSEP19//
NARR/REF A IS BUPERSINST 1430.16H, ADVANCEMENT MANUAL FOR ENLISTED PERSONNEL OF THE U.S. NAVY AND U.S. NAVY RESERVE. REF B IS NAVADMIN 288/22, HIGH YEAR TENURE PLUS PILOT. REF C IS NAVADMIN 277/23, HIGH YEAR TENURE PLUS PILOT INDEFINITE EXTENSION. REF D IS NAVADMIN 138/25, HIGH YEAR TENURE PLUS POLICY UPDATE FOR SELECTED RESERVE AND TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE RESERVES ENLISTED COMMUNITIES. REF E IS NAVADMIN 320/20, IMPLEMENTATION OF NAVY ENLISTED CLASSIFICATION CODE N33Z IDENTIFYING NUCLEAR ENGINEERING WATCH SUPERVISOR AND PROPULSION PLANT WATCH SUPERVISOR QUALIFICATIONS. REF F IS MILPERSMAN 1220-040, SUBMARINE QUALIFICATIONS DESIGNATION FOR ENLISTED MEMBERS. REF G IS NAVADMIN 220/19, ELECTRONIC SUBMISSION OF LETTERS TO PROMOTION AND SELECTION BOARDS THROUGH MYNAVY PORTAL.//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN provides guidance for the Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 Navy Active and Reserve Component Enlisted Advancement Selection and Screen Boards for Chief Petty Officer. The Active Component (AC) and Training and Administration of the Reserve (TAR) Cycle is 270 and the Selected Reserve (SELRES) Cycle is 118. For board purposes, the Reserve Component (RC) board will consider TAR under Cycle 270, SELRES and those serving on voluntary and involuntary recall and mobilization orders under Cycle 118. Mobilized individual ready reserve and canvasser recruiter personnel are considered under the SELRES requirements for advancement.
2. Unless exempted in line with reference (a), AC eligible candidates will participate in the Senior Enlisted Marketplace (SEM) screening board. Exempted candidates will participate in enlisted advancement selection boards. SEM does not apply to Nuclear (EMN, ETN, MMN), Musician (E7), Special Warfare Operator, Special Warfare Boat Operator, personnel currently assigned within the Enlisted Aide Program, Flag Writer Program, TAR, and SELRES. Reference (a) should be read in its entirety.
3. Examination status verification reports (ESVR) will be available on the Navy Enlisted Advancement System (NEAS) website https://neas.ncdc.navy.mil/ or via MyNavy Portal (MNP) at https://mn3p.navy.mil/web/advancement-and-promotion/navy-advancement-center. Select "Advancement Profile Sheet - ESO" under "NEAS Resources".
4. RC Timeline: a. 2 May 2026. Rating change deadline. b. 21 May 2026. 2359 CST: Letter to the board (LTB) deadline. c. 1 June 2026. Board convenes. d. 12 June 2026. Board adjourns.
5. AC Timeline: a. 23 May 2026. Rating change deadline. b. 11 June 2026. 2359 CST: LTB deadline. c. 22 June 2026. Board convenes. d. 17 July 2026. Board adjourns.
6. Eligibility Criteria. Candidates must meet and maintain the eligibility requirements outlined in chapters 2 and 11 of reference (a) and below, through adjournment of the board. The authoritative sources required to reflect candidate eligibility are the Enlisted Master File for Active Duty and TAR and the Navy Standard Integrated Personnel System for SELRES and those serving on voluntary and involuntary recall and mobilization orders, mobilized individual ready reserve and canvasser recruiter personnel. Refer to paragraph 616 of reference (a) for candidate validation procedures. Eligibility requirements include: a. Candidates must be selection board eligible in the correct competitive rate and group on their profile sheet. Candidates should register for e-mail notification for profile sheet updates via MNP at https://mn3p.navy.mil/web/advancement-and-promotion/navy-advancement-center/#. Select "Advancement Profile Sheet - Individual email notification" under the "NEAS Resources" card. Information may change on a weekly basis and both command and Sailor should continue to verify. Sailor access to profile sheets is available via MNP at https://mn3p.navy.mil/web/advancement-and-promotion/navy-advancement-center/##. Select "Advancement Profile Sheet - Individual" under "NEAS Resources". b. Terminal eligibility date: 1 January 2027. c. Time-In-Rate date (TIR): 1 January 2024. If an early promote (EP) TIR waiver is granted, the evaluation used to grant the EP TIR waiver must either be in the member's Official Military Personnel File (OMPF) or submitted to PERS-802 via email. If not in the member's OMPF, the evaluation must also be submitted to the board president via LTB to be viewed by the board. Documents forwarded to PERS-802 are not made available to the board and LTBs are not made available to PERS-802. Failure to provide the evaluation as detailed will result in a candidate being not considered by the board. d. High year tenure (HYT) and HYT Plus. In line with references (b) and (c), all fleet reserve requests are considered voluntary unless superseded by policy update. As of 30 September 2025, HYT Plus Pilot indefinite extension has concluded for SELRES and TAR Sailors, per reference (d). Sailors may still serve past established HYT length of service dates but must submit standard individual waiver requests. e. In line with reference (a), Sailors with approved fleet reserve requests will not be considered by the selection board. f. Security clearance requirements. Ratings requiring a security clearance are AC, AE, AG, AO, AT, AWF, AWO, AWR, AWS, AWV, AZ, CTI, CTM, CTR, CTT, CWT, EOD, ET, FC, FCA, GM, HT, IC, IS, IT, LN, MA, MC, MN, ND, OS, QM, RP, RW, SB, SO, STG and YN, as well as all nuclear and submarine ratings. An eligibility determination of "No Determination Made" constitutes an unfavorable adjudication. g. Rate specific and competitive group eligibility requirements. Candidates must meet special requirements for certain ratings, as outlined in paragraph 203 of reference (a). (1) Nuclear trained Sailors must hold additional Navy Enlisted Classification (NEC) N33Z to indicate engineering watch supervisor or propulsion plant watch supervisor qualification in line with reference (e). (2) Non-nuclear submarine Sailors designated "SG" or "SP" as of board convening date are not eligible for advancement consideration. Refer to reference (f) for submarine qualification designations.
7. Record review and communication with selection board: refer to reference (g). a. LTBs should be submitted via the Electronic Submission of Selection Board Documents (ESSBD). If unable to use ESSBD and formore information on LTBs, visit the following link: https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/Career-Management/Boards/Active-Duty-Enlisted/General-Information/. b. All LTBs and each enclosure must contain the candidates full name and 10-digit Department of Defense Identification (DODID) number. Candidates must verify that the correct board number is on their signed cover letter. Board numbers are: (1) Board #335 for SELRES. (2) Board #336 for TAR. (3) Board #360 for AC SEM screen board. (4) Board #361 for AC SEM exempted ratings.
8. Board results will be posted via BUPERS Online and released by subsequent NAVADMIN, with follow-on guidance for the MyNavy Assignments process for Sailors screened to compete in the marketplace. Board selectee profile sheets will be published by Naval Education and Training Professional Development Center (NETPDC) and displayed in both NEAS and MNP websites.
9. Additional information regarding this cycle will be posted on the MyNavy HR website. a. AC boards: https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/Career-Management/Boards/Active-Duty-Enlisted/CPO-Selection-Boards/. b. RC boards: https://mynavyhr.navy.mil/Career-Management/Boards/Reserve-Enlisted/CPO-Selection-Boards/.
10. Points of Contact (Do not utilize to submit LTBs) a. MyNavy Career Center, (833) 330-6622 or via e-mail at askmncc@us.navy.mil. b. Board Eligibility Concerns, including "BUPERS INVAL" profile sheet status and the submission of evaluations in support of EP TIR waivers: Selection Board Eligibility Branch (PERS-802): (901) 874 4537/DSN 882 e-mail at NPC_enlisted_selbd_elig.fct@navy.mil. c. NETPDC (N321), (850) 473-6148/DSN 753 or via e-mail at usn.pensacola.netpdc.mbx.netpdc-n321-discrepancies@us.navy.mil.
11. This message will remain in effect until superseded or canceled.
12. Released by VADM Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1//.
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 074/26 - 2026 CNO NAVAL HISTORY ESSAY CONTEST
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0911951-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 011951Z APR 26 MID320018618186U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//DNS
NAVADMIN 074/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/DNS/APR//
SUBJ/2026 CNO NAVAL HISTORY ESSAY CONTEST//
RMKS/ 1. The Chief of Naval Operations (CNO) announces the 2026 CNO Naval History Essay Contest, commemorating the enduring legacy of America's Navy and the 250th anniversary of our Nation. As the Navy continues to operate at a strategic inflection point, this contest provides an opportunity to reflect on our history, deepen our understanding of the present, and strengthen the intellectual readiness required for the future fight. Participants are invited to submit their essays no later than 30 May 2026.
2. The Naval History and Heritage Command (NHHC) is the lead for the contest. The United States Naval Institute (USNI) will support contest execution, publication, and awards.
3. CNO invites submissions for the 2026 CNO Naval History Essay Contest that use the power of naval history to illuminate the serious challenges confronting today's Navy. As we enter what may be the most consequential era in American sea power, the demands on our Fleet and our Sailors have never been greater. Great power competition, proliferating threats, rapid technological convergence, and an increasingly contested maritime domain require fresh thinking informed by historical experience. Across 250 years of American naval operations, from the age of sail to the nuclear era, our Navy has repeatedly adapted to strategic disruption, advanced technology, and global instability. Participants are encouraged to examine those historical touchpoints and derive insights that sharpen our understanding of how to generate, deploy, project power ashore and fight a Fleet that is resilient, agile, globally present and combat credible. This contest is an opportunity to harness the intellectual capital of the Fleet and the Nation, leveraging history to strengthen the Foundry, the Fleet, and the way we Fight.
4. Areas of historic interest for the 2026 contest continue to highlight how lessons from the past can inform the Navy's modernization and operational challenges. Submissions may explore: a. Historic Approaches to Defending the Rules-Based Maritime Order. From early American antipiracy patrols to Cold War freedom-of-navigation operations, the Navy has long served as a guarantor of maritime security. How has the Navy historically countered threats to maritime law, and what can these examples teach us as competitors challenge the global order? b. Historic Approaches to Technological Disruption and Warfighting Transformation. Examples include the transition from sail to steam, the rise of naval aviation, and the advent of nuclear propulsion. How can these past transformations inform today's integration of artificial intelligence, autonomy, and distributed maritime operations? c. Historic approaches to maritime dominance. From great power rivalries in the 19th century to the Pacific campaigns of the 20th century, the Navy has consistently adapted to long-term strategic competition. What historical lessons should guide our approach to deterrence and multi-domain rivalry today given expected adversaries across the spectrum of emerging threat? d. America as a maritime nation. From protecting early American trade to enabling a global forward presence, sea power has been the bedrock of U.S. prosperity and security. How has sea power historically shaped national prosperity, deterrence, and the ability to project power far from home and what will need to change going forward? e. The Enduring Impact of the American Sailor. For nearly 250 years, the ingenuity, grit, and courage of the American Sailor have provided the Navy's decisive edge. What historic examples, from the leadership of John Paul Jones to the heroism at Leyte Gulf, best illuminate the Sailor as our main weapon system and competitive advantage?
5. CNO Intent. CNO priorities are clear: Foundry, Fleet, and Fight. To deliver the world's most powerful Fleet and be ready for the Fight, we must strengthen our most critical advantage - our people. The intent of this contest is to generate bold, actionable ideas that will sharpen our warfighting edge, applying insights drawn from the past to successfully navigate the challenges of the present. Consistent with the CNO's vision and theory of victory, the contest seeks to stimulate analysis that strengthens our ability to deliver peace through strength, calibrate our course, and transform how we solve the Navy's toughest problems through the lens of the U.S. Navy Fighting Instructions. As the Navy accelerates shipbuilding and repair, modernizes force-generation, and integrates cutting-edge technologies into a Future Fleet Design, understanding how previous generations confronted similar inflection points is essential. The contest invites authors from across the Fleet, the Joint Force, academia, and the maritime community to examine historic approaches to deterrence, warfighting, and sea power-and to connect those lessons directly to the Foundry, the Fleet, and the way we Fight today. The goal is clear: harness history to ensure that America's Navy remains the most lethal, survivable, and globally capable maritime force in the world.
6. Eligibility. Essays are accepted in the following categories: a. Professional Historian Category Eligibility: (1) U.S. and international professional historians, including history museum curators, archivists, history teachers/professors, and individuals with history-related doctoral degrees. (2) Authors of books on naval history (not including self-published works); or (3) Civilians and active-duty members of the military who have published historical articles in an established historical or naval journal or magazine. b. Rising Historian Category: (1) Those that do not fall in the Professional Category and are either active duty, reserve, veteran, and federal civilian personnel of the Navy, Marine Corps, Coast Guard, and Merchant Marine. (2) Members of foreign militaries serving in official billets within the above services. c. Midshipmen and Cadets Category: (1) Students at the U.S. Naval Academy, U.S. Coast Guard Academy, U.S. Merchant Marine Academy, and the State Maritime Academies. (2) Students enrolled in an official Navy Reserve Officer Training Corps program.
7. Submission Requirements: a. Deadline: 2359 Eastern Time on 30 May 2026. b. Citations: Essays must include numbered footnotes or endnotes. c. Word Count: 3,500 words (Professional Category) or 3,000 words (Rising and Midshipmen/Cadet Categories). d. Co-Authoring: Permitted in the Rising Category only. e. Original Work: Submissions must be original, unpublished, and not under review elsewhere. The use of large language models (e.g. ChatGPT) is not authorized for generating substantive content for review. f. Multiple Submissions: Allowed; only one winning essay per author. g. Entry Submission: (1) Professional Historian Category: https://www.usni.org/cnonhessaycontestprofessional (2) Rising Historian Category: https://www.usni.org/cnonhessaycontestrising (3) Midshipmen and Cadet Category: https://www.usni.org/cnonhessaycontestmidn-cadet h. Eligibility Confirmation: Provide a short biography affirming eligibility. i. Word Count: Include on title page. j. Anonymity: Do not include any identifying information in the essay itself.
8. Essays will be evaluated based on: a. Relevance to the specified areas of interest (para 4). b. Insightfulness and originality. c. Depth and quality of research. d. Clarity and readability.
9. Judging: a. A six-member panel will select winners in the Rising Historian Category. b. Separate six-member panels will evaluate Professional and Midshipmen/Cadet submissions.
10. Awards: a. Invitational travel orders to the 2026 CNO Naval History Essay Awards Reception (details to be announced). b. Cash Prizes (provided by USNI): (1) First Place - $5,000 (Professional and Rising) (2) First Place - $4,000 (Midshipmen/Cadet) (3) Second Place - $2,500 (Professional and Rising) (4) Second Place - $2,000 (Midshipmen/Cadet) (5) Third Place - $1,500 (Rising) (6) Third Place - $1,000 (Midshipmen/Cadet) c. Publication in USNI Proceedings and Naval History (for select essays). d. Copper sheathing from USS CONSTITUTION (NHHC). e. Recognition on the NHHC website. f. One-year USNI membership and Naval History Magazine subscription.
11. Additional information: a. https://www.history.navy.mil/get-involved/essay-contest.html b. Questions for NHHC: stephen.a.hill6.civ@us.navy.mil c. Writing guidance: essayquestions@usni.org
12. Released by VADM Michael E. Boyle, Director, Navy Staff//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 072/26 - FY-27 ACTIVE CHIEF PETTY OFFICER SELECTION AND MARKETPLACE SCREEN BOARD OBSERVER OPPORTUNITIES FOR COMMANDING AND EXECUTIVE OFFICERS (CORRECTED COPY)
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0911959-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 011959Z APR 26 MID320018618206U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 072/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/APR//
SUBJ/FY-27 ACTIVE CHIEF PETTY OFFICER SELECTION AND MARKETPLACE SCREEN BOARD OBSERVER OPPORTUNITIES FOR COMMANDING AND EXECUTIVE OFFICERS (CORRECTED COPY)//
REF/A/DOC/BUPERS/29MAR25// AMPN/REF A IS BUPERSINST 1401.5D, SUPPLEMENTAL GUIDANCE FOR U.S. NAVY SELECTION BOARDS.//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN solicits applications from current Commanding Officers (COs) and Executive Officers (XOs) to serve as observers for the Fiscal Year 2027 (FY27) Active Duty Chief Petty Officer (CPO) Selection and Marketplace Screen Board. The experience provides invaluable insight into the selection board process and senior enlisted leader development.
2. Interested COs and XOs must be available to travel to Millington, TN, on dates that will fall between 22 June to 17 July 2026. Please specify in your application which specific dates from the following you are applying for: June 24-26, June 30-1 July, July 6-8. In the event the dates you apply for are not available, you may be contacted to provide a second selection.
3. When in an observer status, officers will serve in a non-voting capacity in board deliberation spaces and will have no direct contact with board members.
4. Observers will be limited to 7 per deliberation space and opportunities will be dispersed across communities.
5. Prior to submitting an application, review reference (a) which can be found at: https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/References/BUPERSInstructions/.
6. To apply, eligible officers must complete the Enlisted Selection Board Member/Recorder Application (NAVPERS 1400/2) and submit it electronically to the NPC Force Master Chief at william.p.houlihan.mil@us.navy.mil. The NAVPERS 1400/2 form can be downloaded from the MyNavy HR website at https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/References/Forms/NAVPERS/.
7. The deadline for application submission is 1 May 2026.
8. All costs associated with travel and per diem for selected observers will be the responsibility of the observer's parent command.
9. For any questions regarding the application process, please contact the NPC Force Master Chief at (901) 874-3030 or via email at: William.p.houlihan.mil@us.navy.mil. Additionally, all logistics will be coordinated from the office of the NPC Force Master Chief. Rental cars are recommended and please be aware that lodging on Naval Support Activity Mid South may not be available.
10. This opportunity is an excellent professional development experience. Your participation is highly encouraged.
11. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.////
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 070/26 - MARCH 2026 (CYCLE 271) ACTIVE-DUTY AND TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE RESERVE E5 AND E6 NAVY-WIDE ADVANCEMENT EXAMINATIONS AND RATING KNOWLEDGE EXAMS - UPDATED GUIDANCE
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0891953-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 301953Z MAR 26 MID320018613019U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 070/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/JAN//
SUBJ/MARCH 2026 (CYCLE 271) ACTIVE-DUTY AND TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE RESERVE E5 AND E6 NAVY-WIDE ADVANCEMENT EXAMINATIONS AND RATING KNOWLEDGE EXAMS - UPDATED GUIDANCE//
REF/A/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/131839ZJAN26// REF/B/DOC/BUPERS/21JAN26//
NARR/REF A IS NAVADMIN 008/26, ACTIVE DUTY AND TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE RESERVE E5 AND E6 NAVYWIDE ADVANCEMENT EXAMINATIONS AND RATING KNOWLEDGE EXAMS. REF B IS BUPERSINST 1430.16H, ADVANCEMENT MANUAL FOR ENLISTED PERSONNEL OF THE U.S. NAVY AND U.S. NAVY RESERVE.
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN modifies reference (a), providing updated guidance for the Cycle 271 March 2026 exam cycle. The exam administration window for Cycle 271 has been extended through 30 April 2026 in support of Sailors participating in Operation Epic Fury.
2. Every effort must be made to administer exams during the prescribed window. Extending the deadline will ensure receipt of exams and maximum participation for all eligible Sailors and minimize the administrative burden of a missed exam administration. Do not administer exams after 30 April 2026. Destroy exams in accordance with reference (b) when exams are received too late or unable to administer due to operations.
3. Guidelines and actions for commands unable to administer exams: a. Non-Billet Based Advancement (BBA) (legacy) Sailors: (1) In accordance with reference (a), submit an exception to policy (ETP) requesting a standard score comparison of Cycle 272 (fall exam) against Cycle 271 (spring exam) due to missed exams. (2) ETP requests must cite the reason for the missed exam (for example: unable to administer exam due to operational constraints, did not receive exams within prescribed window). b. BBA Sailors (1) For Sailors who have taken a Rating Knowledge Exam (RKE) during a previous exam cycle and have an active Detailing Market Eligibility Indicator (DMEI), no action is required. For Sailors who previously participated in a Navy Wide Advancement Examination, that exam score will be applied to Cycle 271, no action is required. (2) For BBA eligible candidates who have not taken an RKE during a previous exam cycle, commands will generate an ETP waiver requesting standard score application for Cycle 271. (a) The waiver must contain the UIC, full name of candidate, Department of Defense Identification (DoD ID), and exam rate. ETP letter and Candidate Listing by Activity templates are included with NAVADMIN posting. (b) BBA eligibility requirements in accordance with reference (b) will supersede this waiver. Remove BBA ineligible Sailors from ETP waivers. (c) Waiver requests must be emailed directly to Naval Education and Training Professional Development Center (NETPDC N321) no later than 30 April 2026, to the following email: usn.pensacola.netpdc.mbx.netpdc-n321-discrepancies@us.navy.mil.
4. Due to this extended exam cycle administration, advancement results will be significantly delayed. Despite the delay in results, advancements or advancement opportunities will not be compromised.
5. Points of contact for NETPDC and Enlisted Advancement Policy (OPNAV N132C): a. NETPDC: usn.pensacola.netpdc.mbx.netpdc-n321-discrepancies@us.navy.mil. b. OPNAV N132C: NXAG_N132C@navy.mil
6. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 069/26 - ACADEMIC YEAR 2026-2027 NAVAL WAR COLLEGE FLEET SEMINAR PROGRAM
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0852211-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 262211Z MAR 26 MID320018606702U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 069/26
PASS TO OFFICE CODES: INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N3N5N7//
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N3N5N7/MAR//
SUBJ/ACADEMIC YEAR 2026-2027 NAVAL WAR COLLEGE FLEET SEMINAR PROGRAM//
REF/A/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/231709ZJAN26// REF/B/DOC/OSW/22MAY25//
NARR/REF A IS NAVADMIN 017/26, ACADEMIC YEAR 2026-2027 WARFIGHTER EDUCATION AND PROFESSIONAL STUDIES (WEPS) PROGRAM AND NAVAL WAR COLLEGE FLEET SEMINAR FULL TIME PILOT OPPORTUNITY. REF B IS OSW MEMORANDUM, PERMANENT CHANGE OF STATION TARGETED REDUCTIONS REVIEW AND PERSONNEL POLICY CHANGES.//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces the U.S. Naval War College (NWC) Fleet Seminar Program (FSP) which offers intermediate level Joint Professional Military Education (JPME-I) through a set of three courses: Strategy and War (S and W), Theater Security Decision Making (TSDM) and Joint Maritime Operations (JMO). Each course normally requires one academic year to complete, which runs from September through the following May. All three courses are not offered simultaneously at all locations. There is no tuition fee and course materials are provided on a loan basis at no cost to the students. Applicants must apply by 31 May 2026.
2. Background: a. The NWC FSP will be offered in 15 regional locations in the United States for academic year 2026-2027. Planned locations and courses are: (1) Annapolis, MD (S and W, TSDM and JMO) (2) Bangor, WA (TSDM) (3) Dahlgren, VA (JMO) (4) Everett, WA (S and W) (5) Great Lakes, IL (TSDM) (6) Jacksonville, FL (TSDM and JMO) (7) Mayport, FL (S and W) (8) New Orleans, LA (JMO) (9) Newport, RI (S and W, TSDM and JMO) (10) Norfolk, VA (S and W, TSDM and JMO) (11) Patuxent River, MD (TSDM) (12) Pearl Harbor, HI (S and W, TSDM and JMO) (13) Pensacola, FL (Whiting Field) (S and W) (14) San Diego, CA (S and W, TSDM and JMO) (15) Washington, DC (S and W, TSDM and JMO) b. Seminars meet once a week in the evening for 34 weeks. Completion of all three courses results in the award of a College of Naval Command and Staff diploma, as well as credit for JPME-I. The FSP also provides a path to attain the NWC master's degree through the College of Distance Education (CDE) Graduate Degree Program (GDP). FSP students may apply for the GDP following successful completion of their first FSP course. The FSP at all locations is conducted in-step with each other, so students who transfer to or are assigned temporary additional duty at another location may attend class at the new location.
3. Criteria: a. NWC accepts program applications from commissioned officers (active and reserve) and civilian employees of the federal government, subject to the following eligibility requirements: (1) Members of the sea services (Navy, Marine Corps and Coast Guard) in the paygrade of O-3 or above, while officers from the other services must be in the paygrade of O-4 or above. Civilian employees of the federal government in the grade of GS-11 and above, or equivalent, are also eligible for enrollment. (2) All applicants must have previously earned at least a baccalaureate degree. b. Qualified individuals currently stationed in areas listed in paragraph two, or those who expect transfer to those sites prior to 1 September 2026 are encouraged to apply.
4. Application Process. Follow the enrollment procedures on the NWC College of Distance Education FSP website at https:(slash)(slash)usnwc.edu(slash)college-of-distance-education(slash)Fleet-Seminar-Program(slash). The website contains the most current information; the application period will remain open until 31 May 2026.
5. The Naval War College Fleet Seminar - Full-Time pilot program builds upon the proven model of the Fleet Seminar Program announced in reference B for LT and senior URL and IWL officers. This program leads to a Master of Arts in Defense and Strategic Studies from the Naval War College via the Fleet Seminar Program and College of Distance Education. This pilot program provides officers time to attend the three required core JPME I courses (Strategy and War, Theater Security Decision Making, and Joint Maritime Operations) concurrently for 10 months as a full-time student. Upon completion of JPME I, the student officer will have up to an additional 8 months to complete the electives required to earn a master's in National Security Studies from the U.S. Naval War College's College of Distance Education per NWC program requirements. Elective coursework will be completed via NWC program requirements which includes in person and distance learning options. Officers assigned to Fleet Seminar - Full Time will be administratively assigned to the closest senior Navy Reserve Officer Training Corps (NROTC) or Navy Support unit. The JPME Phase I core courses will commence in early September 2026 and conclude in late May 2027. For more information regarding the NWC Fleet Seminar - Full-Time pilot program, please see reference A.
6. Applicants and selections for Fleet Seminar Full Time (FSFT) pilot opportunity will be determined by the appropriate communities per reference A. Contact your detailer for more information regarding FSFT.
7. Points of contact are Ms. Ruth Chapman who can be reached at (401) 856-5530 or via email at fsp(at)usnwc.edu, Mr. Walter Stiles who can be reached at walter.stiles(at)usnwc.edu, and Mr. Tom Alex who can be reached via email at thomas.alex(at)usnwc.edu.
8. Request widest dissemination of the above information. Regional coordinators at the sites listed in paragraph two are requested to pass this information to all Army, Marine Corps, Air Force, Space Force, and Coast Guard units and all reserve units located within safe-driving distance of a FSP site.
9. This message will remain in effect until superseded or 31 May 2026, whichever occurs first.
10. Released by VADM Y. M. Davids, Deputy Chief of Naval Operations for Operations, Plans, Strategy, and Warfighting Development (N3N5N7).//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 068/26 - COMMEMORATION OF THE BATTLE OF OKINAWA 81ST ANNIVERSARY MESSAGE TO THE FLEET
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0851554-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 261554Z MAR 26 MID320018605884U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 068/26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/CNO/MAR//
SUBJ/COMMEMORATION OF THE BATTLE OF OKINAWA 81ST ANNIVERSARY MESSAGE TO THE FLEET//
RMKS/1. From April 1 to June 22, 1945, American forces engaged in the final and bloodiest battle of the Pacific War: the Battle of Okinawa. Codenamed Operation ICEBERG, this 82-day campaign was the largest amphibious assault in the Pacific theater and a stark preview of the cost of invading mainland Japan. As we commemorate the 81st anniversary, we honor the immense sacrifice of the Soldiers, Sailors, and Marines who fought and reflect on the brutal lessons in lethality that this battle imprinted upon our naval heritage.
2. The fight for Okinawa was a "typhoon of steel," a relentless clash against a deeply entrenched and fanatical enemy. The Japanese defenders turned the island's rugged terrain into a fortress, fighting a brutal war of attrition from a labyrinth of caves and fortified ridges. Simultaneously, our fleet endured the most intense kamikaze attacks of the war, a testament to the enemy's resolve and the terrifying nature of the fight. More than 12,000 Americans, including Army Lieutenant General Simon Bolivar Buckner Jr., the highest-ranking U.S. officer killed in the war, gave their lives to secure the island. The victory at Okinawa was a clear demonstration of our military's overwhelming power, but it came at a staggering cost, underscoring the absolute necessity of decisive and overwhelming combat lethality.
3. The legacy of Okinawa lives in our Navy's Fighting Instructions today. It reminds us that deterrence is not abstract. It is built on credible combat power, resilient logistics, hardened installations, and warfighters who are trained to win in combat. It is our duty to carry forward this legacy. Let us remember the sacrifices made on the bloody ridges and in the waters off Okinawa by dedicating ourselves to mastering our craft, honing our warfighting skills, and ensuring our Navy remains the most lethal and dominant maritime force the world has ever known.
4. I encourage all commands to pause and reflect on the Battle of Okinawa, not only on the sacrifice but on the lessons. Study the fight. Internalize the cost. And, recommit to building combat-credible forces that deliver our differentiated value to the Nation and the Joint Force. The legacy forged on the ridges of Okinawa and in the waters offshore is the bedrock of our maritime power. Our charge is clear: be ready - be lethal.
5. Built in the Foundry - Tempered in the Fleet - Forged to Fight.
6. Released by ADM Daryl Caudle, 34th Chief of Naval Operations and MCPON John Perryman, 17th Master Chief Petty Officer of the Navy.
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 067/26 - FY-27 ACTIVE DUTY NAVY NUCLEAR LIMITED DUTY OFFICER IN SERVICE PROCUREMENT SELECTION BOARD RESULTS
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0831440-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 241440Z MAR 26 MID320018600859U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 067/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/MAR//
SUBJ/FY-27 ACTIVE DUTY NAVY NUCLEAR LIMITED DUTY OFFICER IN SERVICE PROCUREMENT SELECTION BOARD RESULTS//
REF/A/DOC/MANMED/06FEB23// AMPN/REF A IS NAVMED P 117, MANUAL OF MEDICAL DEPARTMENT.// REF/B/DOC/BUPERSINST 1540.41F/21JAN25// AMPN/REF B IS QUALIFICATION AND ASSIGNMENT OF PERSONNEL IN THE NAVAL NUCLEAR PROPULSION PROGRAM.//
RMKS/1. Congratulations to selectees of the FY-27 Active Duty Navy Nuclear Limited Duty Officer (LDO) In-Service Procurement Board.
2. For LDO selectees, read name, commissioning month/year. All appointments are effective on the first day of the commissioning month. Members are directed to verify their select status via BUPERS Online.
LIMITED DUTY OFFICER ENSIGN NUCLEAR POWER - 620X Allen Dominick A 0827 Ballis Hunter J 0926 Bergbauer Joseph J 1126 Brown Jerrol R 1126 Chekal Blake A 1026 Combs Tiler W 0127 Dunn Robert J 1026 Elenich Alan M 1126 Everett David A 0927 Floyd William K 0127 Garon Michael Y 0127 Gerry Joshua A 0327 Gines Steven M 1026 Grady Daniel P 0527 Gray Tyler S 0127 Harris Shane M 0127 Hemby Bryan K 1126 Hidalgo Michael A 0327 Hoehn Logan P 0827 John David I 0927 Karasevich Nathan A 1126 Karlsrud Ian M 0227 Koehn Jalen M 1026 Macintosh Dorian A 0927 Mcmahon Connor A 0827 Mcquade Alexander M 0327 Meade Kyle L 0527 Mendoza Anthony 0427 Millmaker Sheridan L 0427 Philipp Sawyer L 0427 Rajewski Alex R 0527 Rice Denton C 0427 Richardson Christian X 0227 Rogers Matthew W 0827 Rokosz Stephen A 1026 Ross Marshall S 1126 Smith Morgan B 0927 Steimel James H 0227 Taylor Kai C 0327 Thigpen Eugene B 1026 Thomas Kyle A 0927 Tolbert Nathan W 0527 Trevino Wesley R 0827 Wilbanks Alexander C 0827 Williams David M 0227
3. Each applicant should take pride in knowing that the superior performance documented in the evaluations reviewed by the board required the members to dedicate long hours in making hard choices to ensure only the best and fully qualified applicants were selected. I am confident that each selectee will justify their selection time and again in their new career as naval officers. Those not selected are highly encouraged to seek out an LDO/CWO in your area for advice on ways to grow professionally and improve opportunity for future selection. Bravo Zulu to all who were willing to answer the call.
4. This message is not authority to issue appointments. Frocking is prohibited. COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS 806) will deliver appointments to MyNavy Career Center (MNCC) Millington approximately 60 days prior to the commissioning date. MNCC will forward appointments to the Transaction Service Center (TSC) Great Lakes, who will distribute directly to the respective CPPAs. TSC Great Lakes will also process the conversion from enlisted to officer status for LDOs, for all commands other than those who have the ability to process their own (CVN, SPECWAR, etc.). TSC Great Lakes can be contacted directly at the below email address.
5. Commanding Officers are directed to: a. Notify selectee. b. Notify PERS 803 via official correspondence not later than 10 days following release of this message of selectees who do not accept appointment. Selectees who decline their commission within this 10 day period will be considered by the FY-27 Enlisted Selection Boards. After the 10 day period, PERS 802 will invalidate all FY-26 Active Duty Navy Nuclear LDO In Service Procurement Selection Board selectees' eligibility for the FY-27 Active Duty Enlisted Selection Boards. c. Per reference (a), ensure LDO selectees obtain a commissioning physical within 90 days preceding the appointment month. Notify the Nuclear LDO detailer (PERS 422B) upon completion. d. Hold execution of all permanent change of station orders in abeyance, if applicable and immediately notify PERS 40 of member's selection. e. Administer official physical fitness assessment (PFA) within 4 months of commissioning date. Selectees must hand carry test results to Officer Indoctrination School, Newport, RI. If selectee fails to achieve satisfactory medium, notify Nuclear LDO detailer (PERS 422B). Hold commissioning in abeyance until successful completion of PFA. f. Hold appointment in abeyance if it is determined that a selectee is no longer mentally, physically, morally or professional qualified or if they fail to meet current PFA requirements. Immediately forward documentation citing reason to PERS 803. 1. Notify PERS-422B if member is no longer in compliance with reference (b).
6. If a Nuclear LDO selectee's enlistment (including any extensions) expires after the date of appointment, they are not reenlistment eligible unless appointment is declined.
7. Each selectee should contact their Nuclear LDO officer detailer (PERS-422B) for submission requirements with regard to personal information and history cards, and inform PERS 806 of address changes to facilitate appointment delivery.
8. Points of Contact a. PERS 422B point of contact for Nuclear LDO In service Procurement Board, issues/questions is LT Ryan Kuhn at (901) 874 3938/DSN 882. b. PERS 806 point of contact for issuance of oath and commissioning documents issues/questions is Mr. Robert Franklin, at (901) 874 3294/DSN 882 or email at officer_appt.fct(at)navy.mil. c. MNCC point of contact for distribution of oath and commissioning documents is askmncc(at)us.navy.mil. d. TSC Great Lakes point of contact for oath of office distribution and conversion questions is M GRLK TSCEOPP(at)navy.mil.
9. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
