item.title: NAVADMIN 064/26 - NATIONAL INTELLIGENCE UNIVERSITY
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0821444-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 231444Z MAR 26 MID320018598241U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN XMT CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 064/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N2N6/MAR//
SUBJ/NATIONAL INTELLIGENCE UNIVERSITY//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAV/17JAN17//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 3870.6A, OBLIGATED SERVICE REQUIREMENTS FOR INFORMATION WARFARE COMMUNITY ENLISTED PERSONNEL TO ATTEND THE NATIONAL INTELLIGENCE UNIVERSITY.//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN solicits applications from active-duty enlisted Information Warfare Community (IWC) personnel for enrollment in National Intelligence University (NIU)'s full-time Master of Science of Strategic Intelligence (MSSI) and Master of Science and Technology Intelligence (MSTI) programs. Non-IWC enlisted personnel, who possess the requisite security clearance and meet NIU's eligibility requirements, may request an exception via their detailers and the NIU Navy Service Chair (see POC information below).
2. NIU is the Intelligence Community's sole accredited, federal degree-granting institution. It is a unique and technologically advanced university that focuses on the profession of intelligence and is the only institution of higher education in the nation that allows its students to study and complete research in the Top Secret/Sensitive Compartmentalized Information (TS/SCI) arena. NIU is committed to offering and continuously maintaining an in-depth curriculum intended to enhance the desired analytical skills and competencies of intelligence analysis to include critical thinking, communications, engagement, and leadership.
3. As NIU transitions from the Office of the Director of National Intelligence (ODNI) to the National Defense University (NDU), the Bachelor of Science in Intelligence (BSI) program will be paused for at least the 2026-2027 academic year. Therefore, applications for the BSI program will not be accepted. This NAVADMIN continues to solicit applications for the Master of Science of Strategic Intelligence (MSSI) and Master of Science and Technology Intelligence (MSTI) programs.
4. Selectees will be assigned to NIU as full-time students under permanent change of station orders to Bethesda, MD with an August start. As full-time students, Sailors will be required to carry a full academic schedule year-round. Degree requirements are expected to be completed in 12 months.
5. Each degree program has specific eligibility requirements which can be found on the NIU website at https://www.ni-u.edu/eligibility/. Competitive MSSI/MSTI applicants will have a cumulative GPA of 3.0 or higher on a 4.0 scale. All applicants must hold or be eligible for a Top Secret/Sensitive Compartmented Information (TS/SCI) clearance. Orders to NIU require a fully adjudicated TS/SCI clearance.
6. Per reference (a), enlisted applicants for the MSSI or MSTI programs must obligate for 60 months of service. Obligated service reflects the total time while in the education program and post-education. Selectees will be required to reenlist more than one year early prior to enrollment if they are unable to satisfy the obligated service requirement. An extension will become binding upon enrollment at NIU.
7. Potential full-time applicants must be active duty and shore tour eligible, in line with Sea-Shore Flow. They should begin their application process by contacting their command career counselors. If the NIU class start time requires the applicant to depart their current command early, they must request command concurrence before continuing the application process. Next, applicants must contact their detailers to discuss the implications for their current assignments and careers. Detailer release is required for this program. Failure to gain release may result in withdrawal of selection. Applicants should receive detailer release no later than 31 October 2026. Once the applicant has been released, the applicant will notify the NIU Navy Service Chair (see POC information below). The applicant will then submit their application via the instructions on the NIU website at https://ni-u.edu/admissions/. Finally, the NIU Navy Service Chair will submit a Service Nomination Memo to NIU Admissions on behalf of all full-time Navy applicants. Full application packages are due to NIU admissions no later than 31 January 2027.
8. Upon selection, member must request (via command career counselor) an in-rate quota in the Career Waypoints (C-WAY) System. Those members not in their C-WAY window should submit a "special circumstance" application indicating reason code of "other", and the career counselor will be prompted to add a note indicating selection to the NIU program. Selection via "special circumstance" application does not guarantee an in-rate C-WAY quota and is not considered part of an individual's performance indicator in the C-WAY process.
9. Final selection results will be disseminated by NIU via e-mail to selectees. Selectees will be issued PCS orders to arrive at NIU no later than one week prior to the beginning of NIU student in-processing in August 2027.
10. Reservists are not eligible for NIU's full-time program but are eligible for NIU's part-time programs. Additional details can be found at https://www.ni-u.edu/academics/. Note that service nominations are not required for NIU's part-time programs.
11. General information about NIU can be found at https://www.ni-u.edu/#.
12. Point of contact is CDR Jay McVann via e-mail at jay.p.mcvann(at)odni.gov and CDR Dan Kwiatkowski via email at daniel.l.kwiatkowski(at)odni.gov
13. This message will remain in effect until superseded.
14. Released by Ms. Jennifer Edgin, Acting Deputy Chief of Naval Operations for Information Warfare, OPNAV N2N6.
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 066/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2026 COMMON MILITARY TRAINING REQUIREMENTS
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0821906-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 231906Z MAR 26 MID320018598980U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 066/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTION DC/N1/MAR//
SUBJ/FISCAL YEAR 2026 COMMON MILITARY TRAINING REQUIREMENTS//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAVINST 1500.22J/11SEP2025// REF/B/SECWAR MEMORANDUM/30SEP2025//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 1500.22J, COMMON MILITARY TRAINING PROGRAM. REF B IS SECRETARY OF WAR MEMORANDUM REDUCTION OF MANDITORY TRAINING REQUIREMENTS TO RESTORE MISSION FOCUS.
RMKS/1. In line with reference (a), this NAVADMIN announces that Fiscal Year 2026 (FY26) Common Military Training (CMT) topics are available at MyNavyPortal https://www.mnp.navy.mil/group/training-education-qualifications/cmt.
2. In accordance with reference (b), FY26 CMT requirements have been reduced and optimized, prioritizing command-delivered, in-person training to align with the CNO's "Total Sailor: Fit to Fight" vision and the Foundry's focus on warfighting readiness. These changes provide critical knowledge necessary for mission success, secure operations, and personal resilience while respecting your time.
3. Streamlined Approach. Your feedback drove smarter training that cut redundant topics, reduced frequency (e.g. Controlled Unclassified Information now biennial), and provided alternative materials saving time and maintaining effectiveness. As directed by reference (b), integrated multi-topic training review and development continues. Updates will be communicated via MyNavyPortal.
4. Mandatory CMT Topics (Required for All): a. Naval Criminal Investigative Service Counterintelligence and Insider Threat Awareness and Reporting b. Department of War Cyber Awareness Challenge 2026 c. Operations Security Annual Training d. Sexual Assault Prevention and Response Awareness e. Suicide Prevention f. Records Management in the Department of the Navy: Everyone's Responsibility
5. This NAVADMIN will remain in effect until superseded or 30 September 2026, whichever occurs first.
6. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 065/26 - C-NOte #6: EXCELLENCE ASHORE
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0821738-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 231738Z MAR 26 MID320018598719U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 065/26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/CNO/MAR//
SUBJ/C-NOte #6: EXCELLENCE ASHORE//
RMKS/1. The Foundry is the bedrock of our naval power. Our ability to project force, sustain combat operations, and win our Nation's wars begins not at sea, but ashore - at the pier, on the flightline, in the shipyard, and at the gates of our installations. Our shore enterprise generates the readiness, capacity, and endurance that make maritime dominance possible. Ships, aircraft, and weapons do not become combat power on their own. They are built, trained, maintained, supplied, and made ready by the Foundry. If we are to fight and win, we must demand excellence not only in the Fleet, but across every installation that generates it.
2. Installations are warfighting platforms. Our standard is excellence everywhere combat power is produced. By formally assessing and recognizing our highest-performing installations, we reinforce a fundamental truth: shore commands are not supporting actors - they are essential elements of the warfighting system. The Fleet's ability to deploy, persist, and fight forward begins on our piers, our ranges, our training facilities, and in our maintenance activities. And, like all warfighting platforms, they must operate within a chain of command that integrates risk with accountability. That's why the alignment of Region and Base Commanders under both the Fleet Commanders and the Commander, Navy Installations Command (CNIC) has been so important. The Foundry is where victory starts as an operational platform.
3. Installations generate the force. Our installations are where we build our most important weapon system - the Sailor. From recruit training to advanced warfare schools, the shore enterprise forges the skill, discipline, and resilience that define a combat-credible Navy. Our shipyards, airfields, logistics hubs, and waterfronts sustain the Fleet across the entire lifecycle of readiness - maintenance, modernization, training, certification, deployment, and reset. Operating day and night, our installations enable the Fleet to stay forward, stay ready, and stay lethal. This work is carried out by our Total Force - Active and Reserve Sailors, civilians, and contracted teammates - working as one team to generate and sustain combat power. A Navy that cannot generate readiness ashore cannot fight at sea.
4. Excellence ashore makes victory at sea possible. When it comes to warfighting, there is no line between the Fleet and the Shore. After decades of underinvestment, we are restoring the Foundry because readiness cannot be surged if it is not built first. The Shore Battle "E" recognizes installations that set the standard for readiness generation, Sailor quality of service, and operational support to the Fleet. This recognition reflects my commitment to treating our installations as warfighting platforms, investing in the infrastructure that underwrites combat credibility, and holding ourselves to the same high standards ashore that we demand at sea.
5. The installations listed below have demonstrated excellence in generating combat-ready forces, sustaining Fleet operations, and delivering the quality of service our Sailors and families deserve. Their performance strengthens the entire Navy, and they have earned the inaugural Shore Battle "E." Fleet Concentration / Extra Large Installation: NAVBASE San Diego, CA Large Installation: NAVSTA Mayport, FL Medium Installation: NAS Whiting Field, FL Small CONUS Installation: NAF El Centro, CA Small OCONUS Installation: PRMF Barking Sands, HI
6. Built in the Foundry - Tempered in the Fleet - Forged to Fight.
7. ADM Daryl Caudle, 34th Chief of Naval Operations sends.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 063/26 - ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY IN OAHU, HAWAII
item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ PTTUZYUW RHHMHAA0014 0792140-UUUU—-RHMCSUU. ZNR UUUUU P 202140Z MAR 26 ZYB MID320018593806U FM COMPACFLT PEARL HARBOR HI TO NAVADMIN INFO SECDEF WASHINGTON DC JOINT STAFF WASHINGTON DC CNO WASHINGTON DC CMC WASHINGTON DC CDR USPACOM HONOLULU HI CDR USEUCOM VAIHINGEN GE CDR USAFRICOM STUTTGART GE CDR USCENTCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USNORTHCOM PETERSON AFB CO CDR USSOUTHCOM MIAMI FL CDR USSOCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USTRANSCOM SCOTT AFB IL CDR USSTRATCOM OFFUTT AFB NE DIRSSP WASHINGTON DC DARPA ARLINGTON VA DCMA HQ ALEXANDRIA VA DFAS HQ ARLINGTON VA DIA WASHINGTON DC DLA FT BELVOIR VA DTRA ALEX WASHINGTON DC NGA WASHINGTON DC MDA WASHINGTON DC NSA FT GEORGE G MEADE MD USCENTCOM COMMAND CENTER MACDILL AFB FL COMUSFLTFORCOM NORFOLK VA CNIC WASHINGTON DC COMPACFLT PEARL HARBOR HI BT UNCLAS
SUBJ/ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY IN OAHU, HAWAII//
NAVADMIN 063/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/PACFLT/N1/MAR//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAVINST 3006.1/01MAR2011// REF/B/DOC/DODINST 3001.02/03MAY2010// REF/C/DOC/DODINST 1015.15/20MAR2008// REF/D/DOC/NAVSOP-1000/12DEC2002// REF/E/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171821ZMAR09// REF/F/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171846ZMAR09//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 3006.1, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH CATASTROPHIC EVENTS, NAVY PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY POLICY. REF B IS DODINST 3001.02, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH NATURAL OR MAN-MADE DISASTERS. REF C IS DODINST 1015.15, PROCEDURES AND SUPPORT FOR NON-FEDERAL ENTITIES AUTHORIZED TO OPERATE ON DOD INSTALLATIONS (ENCL 4). REF D IS FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT POLICY MANUAL. REF E IS ALARACT 070/2009, CONUS EVACUATION ALLOWANCES. REF F IS ALARACT 071/2009, NONCOMBATANT EVACUATION ORDER/OCONUS EVACUATION ALLOWANCES.//
RMKS/1. THIS ORDER TO ACCOUNT APPLIES TO ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, NAF EMPLOYEES, NEX EMPLOYEES, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS ASSIGNED TO THE FOLLOWING GEOGRAPHIC AREA OF INTEREST: OAHU, HAWAII. ECHELON II COMMANDERS AND ALL REGIONAL COMMANDERS SHALL ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION TO ALL SUBORDINATE AND TENANT COMMANDS AND DETACHMENTS FOR IMMEDIATE ACTION UPON RECEIPT.
2. THE GEOGRAPHIC AREA OF INTEREST (GAOI) FOR THIS ACCOUNT IS OAHU, HAWAII.
3. ALL COMMANDS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. COMMANDING OFFICERS (CO) AND/OR COMMANDING OFFICER REPRESENTATIVES (COR) WILL ENSURE THAT THEIR COMMAND'S NAVY FAMILY ACCOUNTABILITY AND ASSESSMENT SYSTEM (NFAAS) DATABASE IS CURRENT AND ACCURATE, TO INCLUDE PHONE NUMBERS AND ADDRESSES. ENSURE ANY RECENT CHECK-INS TO YOUR COMMAND WHO HAVE NOT HAD THEIR CONTACT INFORMATION UPDATED IN NFAAS, COMMUNICATE RECALL INFORMATION TO THEIR RESPECTIVE CHAIN OF COMMAND. B. IMMEDIATELY MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, NAF EMPLOYEES, NEX EMPLOYEES, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS IN THE GAOI. ALL CORS/SPONSORS IN THE AFFECTED GAOI WILL RECEIVE NOTIFICATION FROM NFAAS VIA TEXT/EMAIL TO MUSTER THEMSELVES AND DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS. COS AND/OR CORS WILL ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION OF REQUIREMENT FOR ACCURATE AND TIMELY ACCOUNTABILITY VIA NFAAS. IF MEMBERS ARE UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, COS/CORS WILL DETERMINE ACCURATE STATUS AND WHEREABOUTS OF THEIR PERSONNEL AND MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR THEM AT HOME, WORK, TELEWORK, EVACUATED, OR DISPLACED LOCATION, OR NOT IN THE GAOI WITH SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED, TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OTHER). C. COMMANDS SHALL DIRECT CORS/SPONSORS TO MUSTER IN NFAAS, AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL. IF NFAAS IS NOT AVAILABLE, COMMANDS/MEMBERS ARE TO REPORT THE STATUS OF PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY (PA) TO THE AFFECTED REGION OPERATIONS CENTER (ROC) OR DIRECT CHAIN OF COMMAND. D. DO NOT DELAY REPORTING ON PERSONNEL UNTIL ALL INFORMATION IS COMPLETE. REPORT AND ACCOUNT MUSTERING INFORMATION WHEN OBTAINED, INCLUDING THE POSTING OF AMPLIFYING DATA IN THE NOTES AREA ON THE INDIVIDUAL'S ACCOUNTING LINE.
4. ALL CORS/SPONSORS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. ACCOUNT FOR THEMSELVES AND DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS VIA THE ALERT! TEXT NOTIFICATION AND RESPONSE MESSAGE AND OR AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL. B. IF UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, SPONSORS SHOULD CONTACT THEIR COR, COMMAND MUSTERING OFFICER, OR SUPERVISOR TO CHECK IN (MUSTER). C. SPONSORS WILL PROVIDE COMMAND REPRESENTATIVES WITH CURRENT RESIDENCE, DISPLACED LOCATION, OR IF NOT IN THE GAOI, WITH SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED, TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OTHER).
5. A NEEDS ASSESSMENT HAS BEEN INITIATED.
6. FOR NFAAS TECHNICAL SUPPORT, CONTACT THE NFAAS HELP DESK, EMAIL AT PAAS.FCT(AT)US.NAVY.MIL
7. IF UNABLE TO MUSTER VIA NFAAS, YOUR COR, YOUR MUSTERING POC, OR YOUR CHAIN OF COMMAND, CONTACT THE BELOW POCS (LOCATION/PHONE/EMAIL): A. MNCC /833-330-6622/ASKMNCC(AT)NAVY.MIL. B. CNRH ROC/808-473-3216/ USN.JBPHH.JBPHHPEARLHI.MBX.CNRH-ROC-BWO(AT)US.NAVY.MIL. C. CNIC BWC/202-433-4321/CNIC.N36.BWC(AT)NAVY.MIL. D. PACFLT CAT BWC/808-474-5452/5453/CPF.CATBWC(AT)NAVY.MIL. E. NFAAS PROGRAM MGRS/202-765-6011/ DOUGLAS.AGUILARQUEZADA.CIV(AT)US.NAVY.MIL OR/ ETHAN.T.AUDETAT.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL.
8. RELEASED BY K. ARCHIBALD, CAPT, COMPACFLT N1.//
BT 0014 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 062/26 - ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY IN EUCOM AND AFRICOM
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ PTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0761339-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU P 171339Z MAR 26 MID320018580204U FM COMUSFLTFORCOM NORFOLK VA TO NAVADMIN INFO SECDEF WASHINGTON DC JOINT STAFF WASHINGTON DC CNO WASHINGTON DC CMC WASHINGTON DC CDR USPACOM HONOLULU HI CDR USEUCOM VAIHINGEN GE CDR USAFRICOM STUTTGART GE CDR USCENTCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USNORTHCOM PETERSON AFB CO CDR USSOUTHCOM MIAMI FL CDR USSOCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USTRANSCOM SCOTT AFB IL CDR USSTRATCOM OFFUTT AFB NE DIRSSP WASHINGTON DC DARPA ARLINGTON VA DCMA HQ ALEXANDRIA VA DFAS HQ ARLINGTON VA DIA WASHINGTON DC DLA FT BELVOIR VA DTRA ALEX WASHINGTON DC NGA WASHINGTON DC MDA WASHINGTON DC NSA FT GEORGE G MEADE MD USCENTCOM COMMAND CENTER MACDILL AFB FL COMUSFLTFORCOM NORFOLK VA CNIC WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS UNCLAS
SUBJ/ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY IN EUCOM AND AFRICOM//
NAVADMIN 062/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/USFFC NORFOLK VA/N1/JUL//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAVINST 3006.1/01MAR2011// REF/B/DOC/DODINST 3001.02/03MAY2010// REF/C/DOC/DODINST 1015.15/20MAR2008// REF/D/DOC/NAVSOP-1000/12DEC2002// REF/E/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171821ZMAR09// REF/F/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171846ZMAR09//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 3006.1, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH CATASTROPHIC EVENTS, NAVY PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY POLICY. REF B IS DODINST 3001.02, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH NATURAL OR MAN-MADE DISASTERS. REF C IS DODINST 1015.15, PROCEDURES AND SUPPORT FOR NON-FEDERAL ENTITIES AUTHORIZED TO OPERATE ON DOD INSTALLATIONS (ENCL 4). REF D IS FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT POLICY MANUAL. REF E IS ALARACT 070/2009, CONUS EVACUATION ALLOWANCES. REF F IS ALARACT 071/2009, NONCOMBATANT EVACUATION ORDER/OCONUS EVACUATION ALLOWANCES.//
RMKS/1. THIS ORDER TO ACCOUNT APPLIES TO ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, NAF EMPLOYEES, NEX EMPLOYEES, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS ASSIGNED TO THE FOLLOWING GEOGRAPHIC AREA OF INTEREST: EUCOM AND AFRICOM. ECHELON II COMMANDERS AND ALL REGIONAL COMMANDERS SHALL ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION TO ALL SUBORDINATE AND TENANT COMMANDS AND DETACHMENTS FOR IMMEDIATE ACTION UPON RECEIPT.
2. THE GEOGRAPHIC AREA OF INTEREST (GAOI) FOR THIS ACCOUNT IS EUCOM AND AFRICOM.
3. ALL COMMANDS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. COMMANDING OFFICERS (CO) AND/OR COMMANDING OFFICER REPRESENTATIVES (COR) WILL ENSURE THAT THEIR COMMAND'S NAVY FAMILY ACCOUNTABILITY AND ASSESSMENT SYSTEM (NFAAS) DATABASE IS CURRENT AND ACCURATE, TO INCLUDE PHONE NUMBERS AND ADDRESSES. ENSURE ANY RECENT CHECK-INS TO YOUR COMMAND WHO HAVE NOT HAD THEIR CONTACT INFORMATION UPDATED IN NFAAS, COMMUNICATE RECALL INFORMATION TO THEIR RESPECTIVE CHAIN OF COMMAND. B. IMMEDIATELY MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, NAF EMPLOYEES, NEX EMPLOYEES, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS IN THE GAOI. ALL CORS/SPONSORS IN THE AFFECTED GAOI WILL RECEIVE NOTIFICATION FROM NFAAS VIA TEXT/EMAIL TO MUSTER THEMSELVES AND DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS. COS AND/OR CORS WILL ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION OF REQUIREMENT FOR ACCURATE AND TIMELY ACCOUNTABILITY VIA NFAAS. IF MEMBERS ARE UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, COS/CORS WILL DETERMINE ACCURATE STATUS AND WHEREABOUTS OF THEIR PERSONNEL AND MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR THEM AT HOME, WORK, TELEWORK, EVACUATED, OR DISPLACED LOCATION, OR NOT IN THE GAOI WITH SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED, TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OTHER). C. COMMANDS SHALL DIRECT CORS/SPONSORS TO MUSTER IN NFAAS, AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL. IF NFAAS IS NOT AVAILABLE, COMMANDS/MEMBERS ARE TO REPORT THE STATUS OF PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY (PA) TO THE AFFECTED REGION OPERATIONS CENTER (ROC) OR DIRECT CHAIN OF COMMAND. D. DO NOT DELAY REPORTING ON PERSONNEL UNTIL ALL INFORMATION IS COMPLETE. REPORT AND ACCOUNT MUSTERING INFORMATION WHEN OBTAINED, INCLUDING THE POSTING OF AMPLIFYING DATA IN THE NOTES AREA ON THE INDIVIDUAL'S ACCOUNTING LINE.
4. ALL CORS/SPONSORS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. ACCOUNT FOR THEMSELVES AND DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS VIA THE ALERT! TEXT NOTIFICATION AND RESPONSE MESSAGE AND OR AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL. B. IF UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, SPONSORS SHOULD CONTACT THEIR COR, COMMAND MUSTERING OFFICER, OR SUPERVISOR TO CHECK IN (MUSTER). C. SPONSORS WILL PROVIDE COMMAND REPRESENTATIVES WITH CURRENT RESIDENCE, DISPLACED LOCATION, OR IF NOT IN THE GAOI, WITH SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED, TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OTHER).
5. FOR NFAAS TECHNICAL SUPPORT, CONTACT THE NFAAS HELP DESK AT 866-946-9183 OR VIA EMAIL AT NFAAS(AT).NAVY.MIL.
6. IF UNABLE TO MUSTER VIA NFAAS, YOUR COR, YOUR MUSTERING POC, OR YOUR CHAIN OF COMMAND, CONTACT THE BELOW POCS (LOCATION/PHONE/EMAIL): A. MNCC /833-330-6622/ASKMNCC(AT)NAVY.MIL. B. CNRE 3049B PA REP/777-235-1012/DIMITRIUS.L.REID.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL OR 314-626-2967/GERLOCECIL.R.PARROTINA.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL. C. CNIC BWC/202-433-4321/CNIC.N36.BWC(AT)NAVY.MIL. D. USFFC N1 PERS WATCH/757-836-0584/USFF_NFLT_N1_CATPERS(AT)US.NAVY.MIL E. USFFC BWC/757-836-5397/5398/5359/BWC.FFC(AT)NAVY.MIL. F. NFAAS PROGRAM MGRS/202-765-6011/DOUGLAS.AGUILARQUEZADA.CIV(AT)US.NAVY.MIL OR/ETHAN.T.AUDETAT.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL.
7. RELEASED BY D. A. NELSON, CAPT, DIRECTOR, FLEET PERSONNEL DEVELOPMENT AND ALLOCATION, USFF N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 061/26 - ADVANCEMENT EXAMINATION READINESS REVIEW SCHEDULE FOR 3RD AND 4TH QUARTERS FISCAL YEAR 2026 AND IDENTIFICATION OF FLEET SUBJECT MATTER EXPERTS (CORRECTED COPY)
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0751942-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 161942Z MAR 26 MID320018576399U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 061/26
INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC //N1//
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/MAR//
SUBJ/ADVANCEMENT EXAMINATION READINESS REVIEW SCHEDULE FOR 3RD AND 4TH QUARTERS FISCAL YEAR 2026 AND IDENTIFICATION OF FLEET SUBJECT MATTER EXPERTS (CORRECTED COPY)//
REF/A/DOC/COMNAVPERSCOM/5NOV18//
AMPN/REF A IS MILPERSMAN ARTICLE 1401-020, ADVANCEMENT EXAM READINESS REVIEW (AERR)//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces the Advancement Examination Readiness Review (AERR) schedule from April 2026 through September 2026. AERR panels provide an opportunity for selected rating Fleet Subject Matter Experts (FSME) from Fleet and shore-based commands to positively impact their respective ratings by updating and validating the examination question banks. Selected Chief, Senior Chief, and Master Chief Petty Officers on Active Duty, Training and Administration of the Reserve, and Reservists currently on Active Duty for Operational Support may take part in the process. AERRs enable the Navy to advance the most qualified and rating-knowledgeable Sailors.
2. AERRs are held at Naval Education and Training Professional Development Center (NETPDC) in Pensacola, Florida. Temporary Duty Travel (TDY) in support of AERR events is considered mission critical and is Flag Officer approved. All costs pertaining to TDY are funded by NETPDC and will be conducted in accordance with applicable Joint Travel Regulations.
3. In line with reference (a), Type Commanders (TYCOM) and rating sponsors will recruit rating FSMEs, select AERR panel members plus 2 alternate members, and provide names and contact information no less than 60 days prior to the AERR convening date to NETPDC via e-mail at NETPDC_AERR_MAILBOX@us.navy.mil with a subject line of "AERR Selectees."
4. The following AERRs are scheduled from April through September 2026 and are subject to change:
Convene Date: Rating (number of FSMEs required) 12 - 18 April CTI (6), EN (6) 12 - 25 April AM (5-6), AWS (6), FCA (6), RS (6), SW (6), YNS (6) 26 April - 2 May ABF (4-6), SB (4-6) 26 April - 9 May CS (6), MR (6) 3 - 9 May AWO (6), EOD (4-6), ET (6) 3 - 16 May GSM (6) 10 - 16 May AD (6), CTR (6) 10 - 23 May ITN (6), ND (6), PS (7) 17 - 23 May CTT (6) 31 May - 6 June AWV (6), GM (6), MA (6) 31 May - 13 June ABH (6), AME (6), FC (6) 12 - 18 July AC (6) 12 - 25 July STG (6), UT (6) 19 July - 1 August HM (7) 2 - 8 August AZ (6), NCR (5-6), SO (6) 9 - 22 August LSS (7) 16 - 29 August STS (6), PR (6) 23 - 29 August FT (6) 13 - 26 September MN (6) 20 - 26 September QM (6)
5. In line with reference (a), nomination waivers with accompanying justification must be approved by NETPDC prior to TYCOM and rating sponsor selections. Due to financial considerations, outside continental United States applications will only be approved for emergent, rating-specific needs.
6. For more information on AERRs, including schedules and rating points of contact, log into My Navy Portal https://www.mnp.navy.mil/ under Career and Life Events/Advancement and Promotion/Navy Advancement Center/AERR.
7. Point of contact for this matter is the NETPDC Fleet Liaison,(850) 473-6176 or via e-mail at NETPDC_AERR_MAILBOX@us.navy.mil.
8. This NAVADMIN will remain in effect until superseded or canceled, whichever comes first.
9. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 059/26 - CONTINUUM OF CARE AND ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR SPONSORS AND DEPENDENTS DISPLACED FROM CENTCOM
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0731432-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 141432Z MAR 26 MID320018572751U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC SECDEF WASHINGTON DC JOINT STAFF WASHINGTON DC CNO WASHINGTON DC CMC WASHINGTON DC CDR USPACOM HONOLULU HI CDR USEUCOM VAIHINGEN GE CDR USAFRICOM STUTTGART GE CDR USCENTCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USNORTHCOM PETERSON AFB CO CDR USSOUTHCOM MIAMI FL CDR USSOCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USTRANSCOM SCOTT AFB IL CDR USSTRATCOM OFFUTT AFB NE DIRSSP WASHINGTON DC DARPA ARLINGTON VA DCMA HQ ALEXANDRIA VA DFAS HQ ARLINGTON VA DIA WASHINGTON DC DLA FT BELVOIR VA DTRA ALEX WASHINGTON DC NGA WASHINGTON DC MDA WASHINGTON DC NSA FT GEORGE G MEADE MD USCENTCOM COMMAND CENTER MACDILL AFB FL COMUSFLTFORCOM NORFOLK VA CNIC WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 059/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/MAR//
SUBJ/CONTINUUM OF CARE AND ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR SPONSORS AND DEPENDENTS DISPLACED FROM CENTCOM//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAVINST 3006.1/01MAR2011// REF/B/DOC/DODINST 3001.02/03MAY2010// REF/C/DOC/DODINST 1015.15/20MAR2008// REF/D/DOC/NAVSOP-1000/12DEC2002// REF/E/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171821ZMAR09// REF/F/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171846ZMAR09// REF/H/MSG/COMUSFLTFORCOM/062022Z MAR 26//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 3006.1, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH CATASTROPHIC EVENTS. REF B IS DODINST 3001.02, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH NATURAL OR MANMADE DISASTERS. REF C IS DODINST 1015.15, ESTABLISHMENT, MANAGEMENT, AND CONTROL OF NONAPPROPRIATED FUND INSTRUMENTALITIES AND FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT OF SUPPORTING RESOURCES. REF D IS FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT POLICY MANUAL. REF E IS ALARACT 070/2009, CONUS ORDERED DEPARTURE ALLOWANCES. REF F IS ALARACT 071/2009, NONCOMBATANT ORDERED DEPARTURE ORDER/OCONUS ORDERED DEPARTURE ALLOWANCES. REF H IS NAVADMIN 044/26, ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY IN BAHRAIN.//
RMKS/1. The Chief of Naval Operations and the Chief of Naval Personnel in partnership with Commander, Navy Installations Command (CNIC) and the MyNavy Career Center are resolutely committed to providing the resources and continuum of care for all displaced sponsors and dependents. OPNAV N1 will convene an Operational Planning Team (OPT) with U.S. Naval Forces Central Command (NAVCENT) N1, Navy Personnel Command, MNCC, CNIC, and Naval Supply Systems Command (NAVSUP) to facilitate Sailor and family movements and disposition. Type Commanders (TYCOM) with questions regarding status of personnel and forces permanently assigned to NAVCENT should consolidate requests for information and submit to the OPT via OPNAV N13. a. Additional information and resources listed below. 1) MNCC fact sheet for displaced persons can be found at https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/about-mynavy-hr/commands/mynavy-career-center/ 2) MNCC - please email your inquiries regarding ordered departure entitlements, early return of dependents' allowances, and any questions involving travel entitlements to askmncc@us.navy.mil or call 833-330-6622. 3) The Fleet and Family Support Center (FFSC) nearest to your safe haven location will be the most helpful for locality specific information including: lodging, medical facilities, school, and pet related information. Below link provides the contact information to over 60 FFSC spanning the globe. These local facilities can also initiate NACs for displaced individuals: https://ffr.cnic.navy.mil/portals/76/family_readiness/documents/ffsc%20directory%202025.pdf?ver=c54. 4) Tricare medical care information for displaced persons can be found at https://www.tricare.mil/displaced. 5) Information and guidance on filing claims for personal property that has been damaged, lost, stolen, or destroyed can be found at https://www.jag.navy.mil/legal-services/code-15/epic-fury-personal-property-damage-claimsinfo/. b. CNIC has established a central repatriation site at Baltimore Washington International (BWI) airport in Maryland for displaced personnel to report through enroute to their safe haven. CNIC will have Emergency Family Assistance Center (EFAC) services and resources to displaced personnel and ensure that needs assessment cases include all necessary future care. c. For personnel who did not process through BWI, contact your command COR or contacts in paragraph 9 to be added to the event (Bahrain Feb 26). EFAC resources can be found at https://www.navyregionmwrndw.com/efac.
2. This NAVADMIN directs the updating of current displaced/intermediate location in Navy Family Accountability and Assessment System (NFAAS) enroute to their safe haven and subsequent continuum of care. This order applies to all Navy Active Duty, Reserves, Department of the Navy (DoN) civilian employees, Non-Appropriated Funds (NAF) employees, Navy Exchange (NEX) employees, and applicable dependents within the established Geographic Area of Interest (GAOI) designated in paragraph 2 that required ordered departure.
3. The GAOI for this order is the Central Command (CENTCOM) Area of Responsibility (AOR). This message expands the GOAI specified in the prior order to account for Bahrain per reference (h).
4. All commands are expected to: a. Commanding Officers (CO) will ensure that their Command's NFAAS database is updated to account for all Navy Active Duty, Reserve, DoN civilian employees, NAF employees, NEX employees, and applicable dependents in the GAOI after ordered departure. Ensure that locations of any displaced personnel are updated. b. All Commanding Officer Representatives (COR)/sponsors in the affected GAOI will receive notification from NFAAS via email to update sponsors and dependents safe haven location in NFAAS. COs and/or CORs will ensure widest dissemination of requirements for accurate and timely accountability via NFAAS. If members are unable to access NFAAS, COs/CORs will determine accurate status and whereabouts of sponsor and dependents and COR will update location in NFAAS. c. Commands will direct CORs/sponsors to update safe haven location in NFAAS, at https://navyfamily.navy.mil. If NFAAS is not available, Commands/members are to report the status of Personnel Accountability (PA) location update to direct chain of command to manually update NFAAS. If unable to provide to direct chain of command, members/dependents should call MNCC at 1-833-330-MNCC (6622) or email askmncc@us.navy.mil. d. Do not delay updating safe haven location. Include the posting of amplifying data in the notes area on the individual's accounting line. e. Ensure widest dissemination of resources for displaced sponsors and displaced dependents, which are outlined in paragraph 10 of this message.
5. All CORs/sponsors are expected to: a. Update safe haven location for any sponsors and/or dependents in NFAAS at https://navyfamily.navy.mil/#. b. If unable to access NFAAS, sponsors should contact their COR, command mustering officer, or supervisor to report status. c. Sponsors will provide command representatives with current location of displaced dependents. d. Ensure widest dissemination of resources for displaced sponsors and displaced dependents, which are outlined in paragraph 10 of this message.
6. Sponsor requirement to update intermediate location and final safe haven of self and dependents: a. Login to NFAAS at https://navyfamily.navy.mil/##. b. If you have not previously been accounted for, you will be forced to account for sponsor (self) and any dependents in the GAOI via pop up message. Select the appropriate option for sponsor and for dependents. c. Sponsors must then navigate to the my info tab on the left side. d. Select the event information tab on the left side. e. If event information tab is not available, contact your COR or MNCC to be added to the (Bahrain Feb 26) event. f. On the top right corner select the edit tab. g. Sponsors are required to fill out all applicable fields, including displaced addresses for sponsor and for dependents. In the comments section: sponsors must indicate whether the displaced location is an intermediate location or final safe haven location. h. On the event drop down select (Bahrain Feb 26). i. After all fields are completed, select save.
7. COR requirement to update intermediate location and final safe haven of sponsor and dependents. a. Login to NFAAS at https://navyfamily.navy.mil/###. b. Verify if sponsor is in the event (Bahrain Feb 26). c. If sponsor is in the (Bahrain Feb 26) event and intermediate location or final safe haven is current, no further action required. If sponsor is not in the event, continue to step (d). If sponsor is in event continue to step (g). d. To add a sponsor select personnel tab. Input DoD ID or name and then select search. e. On left side drop down select (add to event). f. Select sponsor name. g. On the left side select (event information). h. On the top right corner select (edit). i. Fill out all applicable fields. j. On the event drop down select (Bahrain Feb 26). k. After all fields are completed, select save.
8. A Needs Assessment Case (NAC) will be opened for any displaced personnel. Once a NAC is established, a case manager will facilitate subsequent continuum of care. Once a case is created for the sponsor, an Emergency Case Manager (ECM) is assigned to provide support with any needs throughout their displaced journey until they reach their safe haven and throughout the entirety of their safe haven duration. Trained ECMs are a single source for support to Sailors, families, and civilians in the DoN with any of these needs during their recovery and repatriation process. We provide consistent outreach and engagement to assess and reassess needs during their recovery process.
9. For NFAAS technical support, contact NFAAS help desk via email at paas.fct@us.navy.mil.
10. If unable to provide physical accountability for any personnel or establish a NAC via NFAAS, your COR, your mustering Point of Contact (POC), or your chain of command, contact the below POCs (location/phone/email): a. MNCC POCs - please email your inquiries to askmncc@us.navy.mil or call 833-330-6622. b. Commander, Navy Region Europe, Africa, Central (CNREURAFCENT) COR /777-235-1012/dimitrius.l.reid.mil@us.navy.mil or 314-626-2967/gerlocecil.r.parrotina.mil@us.navy.mil. c. NFAAS Program Managers/202-765-6011/ douglas.aguilarquezada.civ@us.navy.mil or ethan.t.audetat.mil@us.navy.mil.
11. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

2. If a selected officer does not decline promotion in writing prior to the projected date of rank (noted above in paragraph 1), that officer is considered to have accepted the promotion on the date indicated. An officer who chooses to decline promotion must submit the declination in writing to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-806) within thirty (30) days of the release of this NAVADMIN.
3. The Commanding Officer (CO), the Secretary of the Navy, or his designee, has the authority to delay projected promotions. Upon the exercise of a delay, the officer shall be notified in writing before the effective date of the promotion and not be tendered the promoting appointment. The delay authority will immediately forward the notification of delay with supporting documentation to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-83), copy to PERS-806.
4. COs are reminded of their responsibility to ensure the officers they are promoting are mentally, physically, morally and professionally qualified in accordance with section 624 of title 10, U.S. Code. Per reference (a), officers are ineligible for promotion if they have failed the most recent Physical Fitness Assessment. COs shall verify the promoting officer's information in the Physical Readiness Information Management System to ensure compliance with reference (a) and delay the officers promotion in accordance with paragraph 3 of this NAVADMIN, if required.
5. Prepare a Delivery of Permanent Appointment (NAVPERS form 1421/7)(Rev 10-13). Use this NAVADMIN as reference (a). Use section 624 of title 10, U.S. Code as reference (b) for permanent lieutenants and above. Use section 578 of title 10, U.S. Code as reference (b) for chief warrant officers. Mail the completed form to PERS-806 or email via My Navy Career Center at askmncc.fct@navy.mil.
6. Officers should review reference (b) and (c) for minimum Time-In-Grade (TIG)/Service-In-Grade(SIG) requirements for purposes of voluntary retirement. (NOTE: TIG/SIG requirements for Active-Duty LCDR voluntary retirements changed with National Defense Authorization Act 2021; Update to reference (b) is pending.) Training and Administration of Reserve officers and those officers temporarily on active duty anticipating release from active duty under reference (d) may accept promotion without incurring any additional active-duty obligation.
7. New photographs are required for all officers within three months after acceptance of promotion. See reference (e) for specific requirements.
8. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 058/26 - C-NOte #5: The Backbone of the Fleet: Chief Petty Officers
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0722045-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 132045Z MAR 26 MID320018571109U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 058/26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/CNO/MAR//
SUBJ/C-NOte #5: The Backbone of the Fleet: Chief Petty Officers//
RMKS/1. On April 1st, we celebrate the 133rd birthday of the United States Navy Chief Petty Officer, honoring a legacy of deckplate leadership that has defined our force since 1893. Our Chiefs are the undisputed backbone of the Fleet; they are the seasoned experts who bridge the gap between intent and execution, turning rigorous standards into daily habits and molding our Sailors into elite warfighters. Simply put, the lethality and warrior ethos of our Navy is built upon the foundation of a strong, unified Chiefs Mess.
2. Chief Petty Officers are the Navy's center of gravity at the deckplates. Officers may chart the course, but Chiefs make that course real. They train Sailors, enforce standards, maintain readiness, and deliver repeatable excellence in peace and in combat. The bond of trust between the Wardroom and the Chiefs Mess is fundamental to command at sea. Chiefs give junior officers the leadership repetitions they need to grow, and they keep Commanding Officers firmly connected to the daily realities of the mission. The anchors they wear are not decoration - they are a solemn promise of technical mastery, calm leadership under pressure, and uncompromising standards at all times.
3. As we celebrate the legacy of the Chief Petty Officer, we must also be clear about what we expect of those who wear the anchors today. A Chief is not defined by paygrade, but by presence, practice, and performance. The anchors represent a commitment to lead from the front, to train relentlessly, to enforce standards without hesitation, and to fight for the readiness of our Sailors, our ships, and our Navy.
4. My expectations of every Chief are straightforward and uncompromising. They are highlighted in the following paragraphs.
5. Put people first. Know your Sailors. Know their strengths, their challenges, and their aspirations. Ensure mentorship, recognition, and accountability are never neglected. Warfighting readiness begins with Sailors who are trained, confident, and cared for with empathy and intrusive concern.
6. Drive professional mastery. Chiefs must be the Navy's technical and tactical experts. Our advantage at sea depends on deep rating knowledge, hands-on experience, and relentless attention to craft. Chiefs are the Fleet's most trusted authorities within their ratings, they must drive standards, mentor Sailors, and ensure warfighting excellence through mastery of their profession. Reviving our warrior ethos and creating lethal warfighting effects are central to that responsibility.
7. Exemplify and enforce the standard. From operational discipline to material readiness to personal conduct, Chiefs set the tone. Standards are not optional, and they are not situational. Pride, professionalism, and attention to detail at the deckplates determine how we fight at sea. Chiefs set the example for ownership while underwriting the culture of excellence onboard.
8. Train at the deckplates. Chiefs do not lead from behind a desk. They lead in the spaces, on the line, in the plant, and on watch. Hands-on training, warfare qualifications, and repetition under supervision build the confident, competent watchstanders our Navy depends on in combat.
9. Sustain readiness through transparency. Chiefs anticipate problems early and report them honestly. Maintenance, supply, and personnel challenges must be surfaced quickly so they can be fixed. Readiness is built on trust, and trust requires candor, genuineness, and effective communication skills.
10. Turn guidance into outcomes. Chiefs translate Commanding Officer's intent into executable action. Sailors must understand the task, the standard, the timeline, and the purpose. Chiefs ensure that intent becomes results by delegation with feedback from Sailors. Chiefs are a Commanding Officer's force multiplier on the deckplate.
11. Own the plan and execute with discipline. Before every evolution, Chiefs set the conditions for success. They verify safety, confirm procedures, and remain present throughout execution. When the moment comes, Chiefs anticipate and ensure the team performs to high standards while being outcome driven.
12. Debrief with intellectual honesty. Great teams improve every day in a pursuit of excellence. Chiefs lead candid, no-rank debriefs that capture lessons learned and drive continuous improvement. This is how we sharpen warfighting skills and maintain combat credibility. Chiefs turn brilliance on the basics into daily habits on the deckplates.
13. Lead in crisis. When casualties occur, Chiefs take charge at the scene. They direct damage control, steady the team, instill confidence, and restore order through experience and composure. In the most difficult moments, including the fog of war, the difference between losing control and saving the ship is often a Chief.
14. Build tomorrow's leaders. Chiefs develop junior officers and enlisted alike. They shape future Chiefs, Commanding Officers, and Master Chiefs through daily example, resource management, honest feedback, and high expectations. Leadership is a craft that blends art and science, and Chiefs are masters of this craft.
15. Great ships, squadrons, and commands are never accidents. They are built through intrusive leadership, clear communication, disciplined execution, and hard work at the deckplates. Chiefs weave together mission accomplishment, safety, ownership, and Sailor development. Through their hands, the Navy delivers quality of service for its people, builds mastery throughout the ranks, and remains ready to fight and win.
16. To our Chiefs: thank you for your grit, your wisdom, and your unwavering commitment to our mission. The anchors you wear carry the weight of our history and the responsibility of our future.
17. To the rest of the Fleet: look to your Chiefs. Emulate their professionalism and lean on their experience. We are a more lethal force because of their leadership.
18. Happy Birthday, Chief Petty Officers. See you on the deckplates.
19. Built in the Foundry - Tempered in the Fleet - Forged to Fight.
20. ADM Daryl Caudle, 34th Chief of Naval Operations sends.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

NAVADMIN 052/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/MAR//
SUBJ/SPECIAL DUTY ASSIGNMENT PAY PROGRAM OPNAVINST 1160.6D UPDATE//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAV/26JAN26// REF/B/DOC/DODI/11JAN19//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 1160.6D, SPECIAL DUTY ASSIGNMENT PAY PROGRAM. REF B IS DOW INSTRUCTION 1340.26 CHANGE 1, ASSIGNMENT AND SPECIAL DUTY ASSIGNMENT PAY.//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN provides notice of the release of the Special Duty Assignment Pay (SDAP) OPNAVINST 1160.6D instruction.
2. The major change to this instruction is a streamlined process for the annual certification of SDAP. A NAVADMIN announcing the SDAP recertification window will be published in Summer 2026. It is recommended that commands review the updated instruction to become familiar with the recertification process.
3. The instruction can be obtained from the MyNavy HR website at: https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/References/Pay-Benefits/SDAP//, then click the current OPNAVINST 1160.6 (Jan 2026).
4. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//

item.title: NAVADMIN 053/26 - ANNOUNCING NEW YEAR'S DAY DECK LOG POEM CONTEST FOR 2026
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0692122-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 102122Z MAR 26 MID320018557841U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 053/26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/MAR//
SUBJ/ANNOUNCING NEW YEAR'S DAY DECK LOG POEM CONTEST FOR 2026//
POC/NHHC DECK LOG PROGRAM//LOC: WASHINGTON, DC/EMAIL: NHHC-DECKLOGS(AT)US.NAVY.MIL//
RMKS/1. The Naval History and Heritage Command (NHHC) is excited to announce the annual New Year's Day Deck Log Poem Contest for 2026.
2. Celebratory congratulations to our winners from last year. First place: USS GETTYSBURG (CG 64), written by LT Mary Bolton. Second place: USS GRAVELY (DDG 107), written by ISC Casey Jones. Third place: USS ST. LOUIS (LCS 19), written by LT Victoria Cavazos. Thank you to everyone that submitted entries for last year's contest.
3. The deck log is the official record maintained by all commissioned U.S. Navy vessels. While regulations normally restrict the types of entries in logbooks, the Navy has long held the tradition of the Midnight New Year's Poem. The first entry of the New Year, written in verse, allows Sailors to express their creativity, shipboard experiences, and pride in their command. To highlight this unique tradition, NHHC will host a New Year's Day Deck Log Poem contest. Sailors, we want to hear your voice.
4. Contest details: a. Deadline for submissions is 2359 eastern daylight time, 31 March 2026. b. Due to the public-facing nature of the contest, all entries will be unclassified. c. Submissions will be accepted by email only. Email a pdf copy of the deck log page(s) with your poem to: nhhc-decklogs(at)us.navy.mil. d. Eligibility: (1) Entries must be from a commissioned U.S. Navy vessel, deployed or at shore. (2) Entries must be submissions to the ship's official deck log. (3) Entries must be written as the first entry on 1 January 2026. (4) All paygrades, active and reserve, are eligible. e. Poems can be submitted with either an identified or anonymous author. Commanding officers that would like to recognize the poem's author, please provide the author's contact information with the submission email. In the case of an anonymous author, the command will be recognized and receive the award. f. Selections will be made for first, second, and third place. The first place prize is a piece of historic copper sheathing from the USS CONSTITUTION. Winning entries will be featured on NHHC's website alongside examples from generations past. The NHHC director will also recognize the winners. g. Winners will be announced in April in coordination of national poetry month.
5. For examples of poems and more information, see NHHCs website: https://www.history.navy.mil/content/history/nhhc/research/archives/resources-for-the-fleet/deck-logs/new-years-contest.html
6. For questions, please contact the NHHC deck log program, email: nhhc-decklogs(at)us.navy.mil.
7. Built in the Foundry - Tempered in the Fleet - Forged to Fight.
8. ADM Daryl Caudle, 34th Chief of Naval Operations sends.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 051/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2026 TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE RESERVE AVIATION COMMAND RETENTION BONUS
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0681346-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 091346Z MAR 26 MID120041121569U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 051/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/MAR//
SUBJ/FISCAL YEAR 2026 TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE RESERVE AVIATION COMMAND RETENTION BONUS//
REF/A/DOC/SECNAV/6FEB18// REF/B/DOC/OPNAV/01OCT19//
NARR/REF A IS SECNAVINST 7220.88, AVIATION INCENTIVE PAYS AND BONUS PROGRAM. REF B IS OPNAVINST 7220.18A, AVIATION INCENTIVE PAY AND BONUS PROGRAM.//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces the Fiscal Year (FY) 2026 Training and Administration of the Reserve (TAR) Aviation Command Retention Bonus (ACRB), authorized in line with references (a) and (b).
2. A vital part of developing a total force strategy and maintaining combat readiness is to provide appropriate incentives to retain skilled personnel for critical Naval Aviation Enterprise (NAE) billets. The TAR Aviation Command Retention Bonus (ACRB) program incentivizes highly talented, hard working Naval Aviators and Naval Flight Officers screened for aviation milestone command to choose to remain on Active Duty. Our return on investment is the retention for continued Naval Service of our aviation warfighters, with their invaluable, irreplaceable skillsets and leadership.
3. TAR ACRB: a. The TAR ACRB is designed to retain those officers with the capabilities and command experience in our primary warfighting missions that are critical for the future of our Service. Our cadre of Commander (CDR) Commanding Officers (CO) lead our NAE today, and they are the major commanders and flag officers of tomorrow. Their skills and leadership experience are essential to the success of the Navy. The FY26 ACRB incentivizes their continued service in follow-on assignments requiring senior aviation leaders. b. ACRB applicants must be 13X7 designators and have screened during the Aviation Commander Command Screen Board (ACCSB) as CO of an eligible, Operational (OP) CDR command in order to apply. Eligible commands are those for which the annual ACCSB selects officers to serve as CO (excluding second-in grade/sequential/bonus commands). Consult the FY26 ACRB program information for specific eligibility criteria and limitations. The application opportunity commences with the release of this NAVADMIN and closes on 28 August 2026. c. All FY26 ACRB contracts will be worth $120K ($40k/year). Participation in the FY26 ACRB obligates contractees to three years of obligated service to include full completion of a Post Commander Command (PCC) tour, which may be 36 months depending on assignment. The minimum ACRB obligation ends after 22 years of commissioned service, the Projected Rotation Date (PRD) of the assigned PCC tour or 3 years from contract acceptance date, whichever is later. ACRB applications for those currently under ADHRB obligations will be accepted, held in abeyance, and activated upon ADHRB contract expiration.
4. Additional Aviation Bonus (AvB) Information: for further AvB details, eligibility and application procedures, visit the MyNavy Portal website at at https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil. From there, click through the following sequence: Career Management, Detailing, Officer, PERS-46 TAR and then open the TAR Aviation link under TAR Community Links.
5. AvB points of contact: PERS-46E LCDR Aaron Howell at (901) 874-4064/DSN 882 or via e-mail at aaron.d.howell6.mil@us.navy.mil.
6. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 044/26 - ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY IN BAHRAIN
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ PTTUZYUW RHOIAAA0001 0652022-UUUU--RHMCSUU. ZNR UUUUU P 062022Z MAR 26 MID120041112939U FM COMUSFLTFORCOM NORFOLK VA TO NAVADMIN INFO SECDEF WASHINGTON DC JOINT STAFF WASHINGTON DC CNO WASHINGTON DC CMC WASHINGTON DC CDR USPACOM HONOLULU HI CDR USEUCOM VAIHINGEN GE CDR USAFRICOM STUTTGART GE CDR USCENTCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USNORTHCOM PETERSON AFB CO CDR USSOUTHCOM MIAMI FL CDR USSOCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USTRANSCOM SCOTT AFB IL CDR USSTRATCOM OFFUTT AFB NE DIRSSP WASHINGTON DC DARPA ARLINGTON VA DCMA HQ ALEXANDRIA VA DFAS HQ ARLINGTON VA DIA WASHINGTON DC DLA FT BELVOIR VA DTRA ALEX WASHINGTON DC NGA WASHINGTON DC MDA WASHINGTON DC NSA FT GEORGE G MEADE MD USCENTCOM COMMAND CENTER MACDILL AFB FL COMUSFLTFORCOM NORFOLK VA CNIC WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
SUBJ/ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY IN BAHRAIN//
NAVADMIN 044/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/USFFC NORFOLK VA/N1/JUL//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAVINST 3006.1/01MAR2011// REF/B/DOC/DODINST 3001.02/03MAY2010// REF/C/DOC/DODINST 1015.15/20MAR2008// REF/D/DOC/NAVSOP-1000/12DEC2002// REF/E/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171821ZMAR09// REF/F/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171846ZMAR09//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 3006.1, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH CATASTROPHIC EVENTS, NAVY PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY POLICY. REF B IS DODINST 3001.02, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH NATURAL OR MAN-MADE DISASTERS. REF C IS DODINST 1015.15, PROCEDURES AND SUPPORT FOR NON-FEDERAL ENTITIES AUTHORIZED TO OPERATE ON DOD INSTALLATIONS (ENCL 4). REF D IS FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT POLICY MANUAL. REF E IS ALARACT 070/2009, CONUS EVACUATION ALLOWANCES. REF F IS ALARACT 071/2009, NONCOMBATANT EVACUATION ORDER/OCONUS EVACUATION ALLOWANCES.//
RMKS/1. THIS ORDER TO ACCOUNT APPLIES TO ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, NAF EMPLOYEES, NEX EMPLOYEES, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS ASSIGNED TO THE FOLLOWING UICs IN BAHRAIN UNDER CNREURAFCENT: 46199, 61108, 63005, AND 58054. ECHELON II COMMANDERS AND ALL REGIONAL COMMANDERS SHALL ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION TO ALL SUBORDINATE AND TENANT COMMANDS AND DETACHMENTS FOR IMMEDIATE ACTION UPON RECEIPT.
2. THE GEOGRAPHIC AREA OF INTEREST (GAOI) FOR THIS ACCOUNT IS BAHRAIN.
3. ALL COMMANDS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. COMMANDING OFFICERS (CO) AND/OR COMMANDING OFFICER REPRESENTATIVES (COR) WILL ENSURE THAT THEIR COMMAND'S NAVY FAMILY ACCOUNTABILITY AND ASSESSMENT SYSTEM (NFAAS) DATABASE IS CURRENT AND ACCURATE, TO INCLUDE PHONE NUMBERS AND ADDRESSES. ENSURE ANY RECENT CHECK-INS TO YOUR COMMAND WHO HAVE NOT HAD THEIR CONTACT INFORMATION UPDATED IN NFAAS, COMMUNICATE RECALL INFORMATION TO THEIR RESPECTIVE CHAIN OF COMMAND. B. IMMEDIATELY MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, NAF EMPLOYEES, NEX EMPLOYEES, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS IN THE GAOI. ALL CORS/SPONSORS IN THE AFFECTED GAOI WILL RECEIVE NOTIFICATION FROM NFAAS VIA TEXT/EMAIL TO MUSTER THEMSELVES AND DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS. COS AND/OR CORS WILL ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION OF REQUIREMENT FOR ACCURATE AND TIMELY ACCOUNTABILITY VIA NFAAS. IF MEMBERS ARE UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, COS/CORS WILL DETERMINE ACCURATE STATUS AND WHEREABOUTS OF THEIR PERSONNEL AND MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR THEM AT HOME, WORK, TELEWORK, EVACUATED, OR DISPLACED LOCATION, OR NOT IN THE GAOI WITH SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED, TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OTHER). C. COMMANDS SHALL DIRECT CORS/SPONSORS TO MUSTER IN NFAAS, AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL. IF NFAAS IS NOT AVAILABLE, COMMANDS/MEMBERS ARE TO REPORT THE STATUS OF PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY (PA) TO THE AFFECTED REGION OPERATIONS CENTER (ROC) OR DIRECT CHAIN OF COMMAND. D. DO NOT DELAY REPORTING ON PERSONNEL UNTIL ALL INFORMATION IS COMPLETE. REPORT AND ACCOUNT MUSTERING INFORMATION WHEN OBTAINED, INCLUDING THE POSTING OF AMPLIFYING DATA IN THE NOTES AREA ON THE INDIVIDUAL'S ACCOUNTING LINE.
4. ALL CORS/SPONSORS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. ACCOUNT FOR THEMSELVES AND DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS VIA THE ALERT! TEXT NOTIFICATION AND RESPONSE MESSAGE AND OR AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL. B. IF UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, SPONSORS SHOULD CONTACT THEIR COR, COMMAND MUSTERING OFFICER, OR SUPERVISOR TO CHECK IN (MUSTER). C. SPONSORS WILL PROVIDE COMMAND REPRESENTATIVES WITH CURRENT RESIDENCE, DISPLACED LOCATION, OR IF NOT IN THE GAOI, WITH SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED, TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OTHER).
5. A NEEDS ASSESSMENT HAS BEEN INITIATED.
6. FOR NFAAS TECHNICAL SUPPORT, CONTACT THE NFAAS HELP DESK AT 866-946-9183 OR VIA EMAIL AT NFAAS(AT).NAVY.MIL.
7. IF UNABLE TO MUSTER VIA NFAAS, YOUR COR, YOUR MUSTERING POC, OR YOUR CHAIN OF COMMAND, CONTACT THE BELOW POCS (LOCATION/PHONE/EMAIL): A. NPC ECC/877-414-5358/NPC.CAT.CAPTAIN(AT)NAVY.MIL. B. CNR 3049B PA REP/777-235-1012/DIMITRIUS.L.REID.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL OR 314-626-2967/GERLOCECIL.R.PARROTINA.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL. C. CNIC BWC/202-433-4321/CNIC.N36.BWC(AT)NAVY.MIL. D. USFFC N1 PERS WATCH/757-836-0584/USFF_NFLT_N1_CATPERS@US.NAVY.MIL E. USFFC BWC/757-836-5397/5398/5359/BWC.FFC(AT)NAVY.MIL. F. NFAAS PROGRAM MGRS/202-765-6011/DOUGLAS.AGUILARQUEZADA.CIV(AT)US.NAVY.MIL OR/ETHAN.T.AUDETAT.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL.
8. RELEASED BY D. A. NELSON, CAPT, DIRECTOR, FLEET PERSONNEL DEVELOPMENT AND ALLOCATION, USFF N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 050/26 - UNITED STATES NAVAL COMMUNITY COLLEGE FY 26 PROGRAM UPDATES AND CALL FOR FACULTY POSITION APPLICATIONS
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0651912-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 061912Z MAR 26 MID120041112744U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 050/26 PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N7// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N7//
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N7/MAR//
SUBJ/UNITED STATES NAVAL COMMUNITY COLLEGE FY 26 PROGRAM UPDATES AND CALL FOR FACULTY POSITION APPLICATIONS//
REF/A/MSG/SECNAV/171700ZJAN20// REF/B/MILPERSMAN 1306-945//
AMPN/REF A IS ALNAV 009/20, SECNAV VECTOR 7. REF B is MILPERSMAN for Flag Officer (Staff) Screening.//
RMKS/1. This message provides an update to the United States Naval Community College (USNCC) degree and certificate programs available to students for FY26 and solicits applications for two online asynchronous Academic Faculty positions. A. The USNCC has opened new programs in artificial intelligence and data analytics designed to enhance warfighter development. USNCC now offers over 40 Naval-relevant, regionally accredited associate programs and professional certificates. All degrees provide credit for military training and are awarded with a built-in pathway to 4-year degree programs at partner institutions. Tuition, books and fees are fully funded for enlisted service members. Courses are 100% online and asynchronous to avoid interference with duty requirements. Student applications for all programs are accepted year-round at https://www.usncc.edu/s/enrollment. Student eligibility requirements can be found on our website at https://www.usncc.edu/s/admissions/eligibility-requirements. B. Additionally, USNCC is seeking highly motivated and qualified E9s for two online asynchronous Academic Faculty positions. This NAVADMIN solicits enlisted applications meeting the academic institution requirements delineated in paragraph 3. Submission packages must be received via email to CSEL@usncc.edu no later than 10 April 2026. USNCC review and follow-on selection notifications will be made no later than 05 June 2026.
2. Program Updates. A. New degree programs offered include: (1) Associate of Arts in General Studies with Artificial Intelligence Specialization, University of Maryland Global Campus (2) Associate of Arts in General Studies with Data Analytics Specialization, University of Maryland Global Campus (3) Associate of Science in Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence, Arizona State University B. Standalone certificates are available for each of the above-mentioned degree programs. Programs offered include: (1) Certificate in Artificial Intelligence Foundations, University of Maryland Global Campus (2) Certificate in Data Analytics, University of Maryland Global Campus (3) Certificate in Foundations of Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence, Arizona State University C. A full list of available programs can be found on the USNCC website at https://www.usncc.edu/s/academics/available-programs. For additional information regarding program requirements and course details, please visit the USNCC website at https://www.usncc.edu/s/#.
3. Academic Faculty Eligibility. A. Personnel selected for the unique and demanding Academic Faculty positions for a three-year duty will represent the hundreds of thousands of Sailors serving throughout the United States Navy across the U.S. and abroad. Applicants must meet the service administrative and USNCC academic requirements listed below to be considered: (1) Applicants with a projected rotation date (PRD) of November 2026 or earlier are eligible. (2) Applicants beyond the above projected rotation date (PRD) may apply with command and detailer approval. (3) Applicants selected may detach from their current command as early as detailer approved. (4) Applicants must have passed the most recent (within the last year) PFA and be within body fat standards. (5) Applicants must pass Flag Screening IAW REF B. (6) Academic Faculty applicants must have completed an accredited graduate degree that supports teaching at least one of the following topics at the college 100 series course level; US Naval History, Political Science, Naval Force Design, International Affairs, Ethics, English, Critical Thinking, and/or Math. (7) Experience teaching in an online asynchronous community college setting is preferred but not required.
4. Academic Faculty position submission package must contain the following documents: A. Last 5 years of evaluations (all SSNs redacted). B. Biography with official photo (2 pages maximum). C. Proof of formal education with a minimum of a master's degree (submit all relevant college level transcripts - All SSNs and student IDs redacted) D. Command endorsement from CO or Commander. E. Personal letter describing why applicant desires to teach at the USNCC and the value of higher education for the enlisted ranks (1 page maximum).
5. The USNCC POC is CMDCM Toyna Grace. CMDCM Grace can be contacted via e-mail at Latoyna.grace@usncc.edu. For additional information about USNCC, visit the USNCC website at https://www.usncc.edu/#.
6. This NAVADMIN will remain in effect until superseded or canceled, whichever occurs first.
7. Released by VADM Y. M. Davids, Deputy Chief of Naval Operations for Operations, Plans, Strategy, and Warfighting Development (N3N5N7).//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 049/26 - SOLICITATION FOR THE 2026 EXPEDITIONARY WARFARE EXCELLENCE AWARDS (EWEA)
item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ ROUTINE R 061608Z MAR 26 MID120041111745U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 049/26
PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N95// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N95//
MSGID/GENADMIN/DCNO WASHINGTON OPNAV N95//
SUBJ/SOLICITATION FOR THE 2026 EXPEDITIONARY WARFARE EXCELLENCE AWARDS (EWEA)//
REF/A/DOC/DECISION PAPER/20200610// AMPN/REF A IS THE DECISION PAPER FOR THE ESTABLISHMENT OF THE EWEA//
PRI POC/LCDR SAM LIEBER/OPNAV N95/COM (703)697-9801/EMAIL: SAMUEL.J.LIEBER.MIL@US.NAVY.MIL// SEC POC/MS. JACKIE LOPEZ/OPNAV N95/COM (703)692-1574/ JACKLYN.I.LOPEZ.CTR@US.NAVY.MIL//
GENTEXT/REMARKS/1. PURPOSE. THE DEPUTY COMMANDANT FOR COMBAT DEVELOPMENT AND INTEGRATION (DC, CD&I) AND THE DEPUTY CHIEF OF NAVAL OPERATIONS FOR WARFIGHTING REQUIREMENTS AND CAPABILITIES (DCNO, N9) ARE PLEASED TO SOLICIT NOMINATIONS FOR THE ANNUAL EXPEDITIONARY WARFARE EXCELLENCE AWARDS (EWEA). THE EWEA RECOGNIZES THOSE MARINES AND SAILORS WHO HAVE DISTINGUISHED THEMSELVES THROUGH EXCEPTIONAL PERFORMANCE WHILE SERVING IN AN EXPEDITIONARY WARFARE ASSIGNMENT AND HAVE SIGNIFICANTLY INFLUENCED THE FUTURE OF NAVAL EXPEDITIONARY WARFARE AND AMPHIBIOUS CAPABILITIES, DOCTRINE AND TRAINING THROUGH INNOVATIVE CONCEPTS, LEADERSHIP, AND TEAMWORK. THE EWEA CEREMONY WILL TAKE PLACE AT THE 2026 MARINE CORPS ASSOCIATION COMBAT DEVELOPMENT DINNER ON OR AROUND 20 AUGUST 2026.
1.A. INCLUSIVE DATES FOR THE AWARDS ARE 1 JANUARY 2025 TO 31 DECEMBER 2025.
1.B. THE EWEA FEATURES FOUR AWARDS: MARINE CORPS EXPEDITIONARY WARFARE OFFICER OF THE YEAR (CWO3 - CWO4, O3-O4), MARINE CORPS EXPEDITIONARY WARFARE STAFF NON-COMMISSIONED OFFICER OF THE YEAR (E6-E8), NAVY EXPEDITIONARY WARFARE OFFICER OF THE YEAR (CWO3-CWO4, O3-O4), AND NAVY EXPEDITIONARY WARFARE ENLISTED SAILOR OF THE YEAR (E6-E8). THIS AWARDS PROGRAM IS APPLICABLE TO THE ACTIVE AND RESERVE COMPONENTS OF THE UNITED STATES MARINE CORPS AND THE UNITED STATES NAVY.
1.C. AN AWARDS BOARD, CONVENED BY CD&I AND OPNAV N9, WILL REVIEW AND EVALUATE ALL SUBMITTED PACKAGES TO SELECT A WINNER FROM BOTH THE NAVY AND MARINE CORPS FOR EACH CATEGORY. THESE SELECTIONS WILL THEN BE FORWARDED TO THE DC, CD&I AND OPNAV N9 FOR FINAL APPROVAL.
THE AWARD WINNERS WILL BE ANNOUNCED VIA NAVADMIN.
2. ACTION
2.A. COMMANDS WITH PERSONNEL MEETING THE CRITERIA ARE HIGHLY ENCOURAGED TO SUBMIT NOMINATIONS VIA THE FIRST O-6 IN THE CHAIN OF COMMAND TO THE POCS LISTED IN THIS MESSAGE.
2.B. NOMINATION PACKAGES MUST BE RECEIVED NO LATER THAN 15 MAY 2026.
2.C. NOMINATION PACKAGES SHALL BE SENT ELECTRONICALLY VIA EMAIL AND WILL INCLUDE ONE FILE IN MICROSOFT WORD FORMAT AND ONE FILE IN PORTABLE DOCUMENT FORMAT (PDF) PER NOMINEE. THE SUBJECT LINE SHOULD READ: "CALENDAR YEAR 2025 EWEA ICO RANK/NAME", NOMINATION PACKAGES WILL INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING:
2.C.1. A NOMINATION LETTER FROM THE FIRST O-6, AT A MINIMUM, IN THE CHAIN OF COMMAND.
2.C.2. UNIT POINT OF CONTACT TO INCLUDE PHONE NUMBER AND EMAIL ADDRESS.
2.C.3. PHONE NUMBER AND EMAIL ADDRESS FOR EACH NOMINEE.
2.C.4. A SUMMARY OF PERFORMANCE THAT PROVIDES A CONCISE RESUME OF QUALIFICATIONS, INCLUDING THE BILLET DESCRIPTION AND SIGNIFICANT CONTRIBUTIONS, NOT TO EXCEED TWO PAGES USING 12-PITCH COURIER NEW FONT.
2.C.5. A BIOGRAPHY OF THE NOMINEE.
2.C.6. A PROPOSED CITATION NOT TO EXCEED 10 TYPEWRITTEN LINES USING 12-PITCH COURIER NEW FONT.
3. CD&I WILL FUND TRAVEL FOR THE INDIVIDUAL AWARDEE TO ATTEND THE COMBAT DEVELOPMENT DINNER FOR THE PRESENTATION OF THE AWARD. EVERY EFFORT SHALL BE MADE BY LOCAL COMMANDERS TO ENSURE THE PRESENCE OF THE AWARDEE AT THE CEREMONY. INDIVIDUALS OTHER THAN THE AWARDEE WHO DESIRE TO ATTEND MUST DO SO AT THEIR OWN EXPENSE.
4. ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION OF THE CONTENTS OF THIS MESSAGE.
5. RELEASE AUTHORIZED BY REAR ADMIRAL M. BEHNING, DEPUTY CHIEF OF NAVAL OPERATIONS FOR WARFIGHTING REQUIREMENTS AND CAPABILITIES.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/
