item.title: NAVADMIN 027/26 - SPRING FISCAL YEAR 2026 TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE RESERVE OFFICER TRANSFER AND REDESIGNATION BOARD
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0492105-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 182105Z FEB 26 MID120041034353U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 027/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/FEB//
SUBJ/SPRING FISCAL YEAR 2026 TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE RESERVE OFFICER TRANSFER AND REDESIGNATION BOARD//
REF/A/DOC/COMNAVPERSCOM/06DEC23// REF/B/DOC/COMNAVPERSCOM/09JUL24// REF/C/DOC/COMNAVPERSCOM/18DEC23//
NARR/REF A IS MILPERSMAN ARTICLE 1001-020, TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE RESERVE (TAR) NAVY RESERVE OFFICER PROGRAM. REF B IS MILPERSMAN ARTICLE 1301-213, OFFICER SPECIAL ASSIGNMENTS - RESERVE FLEET LOGISTICS SUPPORT SQUADRON (VR). REF C IS MILPERSMAN ARTICLE 1321-100, ORDERS FOR OFFICER SPECIAL PROGRAMS.//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces the convening of the Spring Fiscal Year 2026 Training and Administration of the Reserve (TAR) Officer Transfer and Redesignation Board to be held on 27 April 2026. Applications from qualified Active Component (AC) and Selected Reserve (SELRES) officers will be considered by this board. In line with reference (a), this board will accept applications from TAR officers requesting redesignation into another TAR officer community, to include 13X7 officers that were previously selected as aviation in a reserve management capacity, that are requesting consideration for TAR aviation department head per paragraph 6.
2. Applicants can find requirements and procedures on the TAR redesignation board page via the MyNavy HR Portal at https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/Career-Management/Community-Management/Officer/Reserve-OCM/TAR/TAR-Redesignation/. Completed applications with associated enclosures must be received no later than 2359 Central Time (CT) on 21 March 2026. Letters to the board must be received no later than 2359 CT on 16 April 2026.
3. Eligibility. a. All applicants must meet eligibility requirements outlined in references (a) and (b), in addition to the TAR redesignation requirements located under "Redesignation Documents" on the TAR redesignation board page via the MyNavy HR Portal, and the following: (1) Projected Rotation Date (PRD). Applicants should have a PRD no later than 31 October 2027. Waiver requests must be endorsed by the applicants commanding officer on the command endorsement. (2) Minimum Service Requirement (MSR). AC applicants should have completed their MSR prior to 31 October 2027. Waiver requests (no additional documentation required by the applicant) will be considered on a case-by-case basis by the applicants AC Officer Community Manager (OCM). (3) Year Group. All AC applicants are subject to AC OCM manning needs, and all TAR applicants are subject to TAR OCM manning needs. No action is required by the applicants. (4) Aviation Bonus (AvB). Aviation officers with obligated service as a result of AvB are eligible to apply if the officer will be within 12 months of completing AvB obligation or department head tour at the time the board convenes. Redesignation immediately following a department head tour may result in cancellation of AvB, which will be adjudicated by Aviation Officer Assignments (PERS-43). (5) Failure of Selection (FOS). AC officers in a two-time (2x) FOS status for promotion to lieutenant commander are eligible to apply for TAR regardless of MSR and AvB contract term, and a waiver request is not required. SELRES officers in a 2x FOS status seeking transfer/redesignation to a TAR community are eligible to apply. Those SELRES officers with three or more FOS are not eligible. b. All AC applications will be routed by the TAR OCM through the applicable AC OCM for community release prior to applications being sent to the board. Applicants not released by the AC OCM will not be eligible for the board. AC officers with a resignation submitted in the Navy Standard Integrated Personnel System (NSIPS) and in routing at Navy Personnel Command, or lieutenants in a 2x FOS status are eligible to apply and do not require AC OCM release. c. Limited duty officers who possess a four-year baccalaureate degree are eligible to apply for the TAR program. Chief warrant officers are not eligible to apply.
4. Officers applying for multiple aviation platforms will be selected based on qualifications and Navy mission requirements.
5. In addition to the TAR communities applied for, applicants may be considered for selection by the TAR community associated with the applicants current designator, if an equivalent TAR community exists (e.g., 1520 may be considered for 1527, even if the applicant does not list 1527).
6. TAR Aviation in a Reserve management capacity. a. Officers applying to TAR pilot or Naval flight officer aviation communities may also request consideration for redesignation into TAR aviation in a Reserve management capacity. Applicants interested in these non-flying roles should indicate this preference on their application preferences. After all aviation community selections are completed, applicants who are not selected for their requested aviation designators and have indicated interest in Reserve management on their application will be screened for available TAR Reserve management opportunities. b. Officers selected for this transition will be offered TAR conversion to redesignate as 13X7, but will not be detailed in a Department Head status and therefore are not eligible for the TAR Aviation Department Head Retention Bonus. Members selected for this may be eligible to reapply for TAR Aviation Department Head selection on future TAR redesignation boards in line with the applicable board NAVADMIN and subject to the applicable TAR community management quota limitations at that time. Please contact the TAR aviation OCM for additional questions regarding this opportunity.
7. All officers selected via the TAR Transfer and Redesignation Board will incur a three-year TAR obligation. All TAR obligations will begin on the date they redesignate as a TAR officer, unless otherwise stated in reference (c).
8. Upon approval of the board proceedings, the results of the board will be posted on the TAR redesignation board website. Applicants can expect to see the results posted within 30 days of board adjournment. Within two weeks of the board results being posted, selectees will receive an acceptance package and will have 10 days upon receipt to accept or decline TAR transfer or redesignation.
9. Points of contact: a. CDR Elijah J. Waddle, Director Reserve Affiliation and Redesignation Division (PERS-92), (901) 874-4512/DSN 882 or pers-92-admin@us.navy.mil. b. Mr. Keith Reitz (PERS-92), (901) 874-4599/DSN 882 or at pers-92-admin@us.navy.mil.
10. This NAVADMIN will remain in effect until superseded or canceled, whichever occurs first.
11. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 026/26 - COMMEMORATION OF THE BATTLE OF IWO JIMA - 81ST ANNIVERSARY MESSAGE TO THE FLEET
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0482133-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 172133Z FEB 26 MID120041029135U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 026/26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N00/FEB//
SUBJ/COMMEMORATION OF THE BATTLE OF IWO JIMA - 81ST ANNIVERSARY MESSAGE TO THE FLEET//
RMKS/1. On 19 February 1945, American Sailors and Marines began one of the fiercest battles in our Nation's history. Against determined resistance and under extraordinary conditions, they fought with relentless courage, unwavering commitment, and an unbreakable will to win. The Battle of Iwo Jima stands as a testament to honor, sacrifice, and the fighting spirit that defines our Naval Service.
2. The fight for Iwo Jima lasted more than five weeks and proved decisive in the Pacific campaign. The island's capture provided critical operational advantages that accelerated Allied victory. That victory came at a tremendous cost, secured by the grit, determination, and teamwork of Sailors and Marines fighting side by side.
3. We will forever honor the thousands who fought on Iwo Jima, many of whom made the ultimate sacrifice. Twenty-seven Medals of Honor were awarded for actions during the battle, reflecting acts of conspicuous gallantry and intrepidity that remain unmatched in our history. Their legacy reminds us that freedom is preserved by those willing to endure hardship, assume risk, and make sacrifices in defense of our Nation.
4. The spirit displayed at Iwo Jima remains central to how we prepare to fight and win. Our Navy must be ready to operate decisively in contested environments, across all domains, and against determined adversaries. This demands a force that is ready, lethal, resilient, and adaptable.
5. I encourage all commands to observe this anniversary with appropriate commemorative events, professional discussions, and reflections on our heritage. Use this opportunity to educate the next generation of Sailors about the courage and sacrifice that shaped our Service and to reinforce the standards expected of those who wear the uniform today.
6. The flag raised over Mount Suribachi symbolized more than a battlefield victory - it symbolized resolve. In the words of Fleet Admiral Chester Nimitz, "Uncommon valor was a common virtue." That resolve lives on in today's Navy. As we honor the heroes of Iwo Jima, let us recommit ourselves to their example.
7. Built in the Foundry - Tempered in the Fleet - Forged to Fight.
8. ADM Daryl Caudle, 34th Chief of Naval Operations sends.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 024/26 - NAVY COUNSELOR PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT TRAINING SYMPOSIUM
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0401923-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 091923Z FEB 26 MID320018527995U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 024/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/FEB//
SUBJ/NAVY COUNSELOR PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT TRAINING SYMPOSIUM//
REF/A/DOC/DOD/DNS/5JAN2026/NOTAL// REF/B/DOC/DOD/06AUG1998// REF/C/DOC/JTR MANUAL//
NARR/REF A IS DEPARTMENT OF NAVY STAFF APPROVED LETTER FOR NAVY COUNSELOR PROFESSIONAL DEVELOPMENT TRAINING SYMPOSIUM.
REF B IS DOD PUBLICATION 5500-7-R, JOINT ETHICS REGULATIONS REGARDING OFFICIAL PARTICIPATION IN NON-FEDERAL ENTITIES.
REF C IS THE JOINT TRAVEL REGULATIONS MANUAL//.
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces the Chief of Navy Personnel, Navy Counselor Professional Development Training Symposium (NCPDTS) from 4-7 May 2026 at the Naval Air Station Base Theater, in Lemoore, California. The symposium will focus on developing Command Career Counselors (CCC) and addressing key "force of the future" initiatives to enhance career readiness and improve fleet wide retention.
2. All rated Navy Counselors, and Command Career Counselors holding the (NEC 806-R) are encouraged to attend. Approved attendees are expected to participate in all four days of the symposium.
3. An additional training session specifically for Training and Administration of Reserves (TAR) Counselors will be held on 8 May 2026 at the Naval Reserve Center Lemoore. Both TAR and Active Component (AC) Counselors are welcome.
4. The curriculum will enhance the skills and knowledge of Career Counselors and will include the following topics: a. CCC and NC rate health, manning and distribution process b. CCC organization skills and best practices c. Career Way Points(C-WAY)/Professional Apprentice Career Track d. Navy Retention Monitoring Systems (NRMS) e. Transition Assistance Program (TAP) f. Career Information Program Reviews (CIPR) g. Career Development Boards (CDB) h. Billet Based Advancement (BBA) i. Career guidance and the future of the CCC force
5. Travel Authorization. In line with reference (b), Commanding Officers are authorized to support attendance for the CCCs and may issue orders for attendance at government expense. In line with reference (c) temporary assigned duty (TAD) orders may cover expenses incidental to attendance and participation in the NCPDTS including transportation, lodging and per diem. Commands are encouraged to send only one representative for the CCC training to minimize travel costs. Incoming flights should be scheduled to arrive 3 May 2026. Return flights should not be scheduled prior to 8 May 2026 unless also attending the TAR Career Development Training.
6. All participants are required to submit registration no later than 14 April 2026 via the website at: https://forms.osi.apps.mil/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=AD4z43fIh0u2rUXpQt4XUAjd_BMPlHtOiPj8K5583uxURFI4TlpYUjZBNFE4V0E4VTJLUzZGTEU3MC4u
7. Points of Contact: a. Personnel, Manpower, and Training, Fleet Career Counselor. NCCM James Osborne, at (703)604-5353/DSN 664 or via e-mail at james.r.osborne.mil@us.navy.mil. b. U.S. Fleet Forces, Fleet Career Counselor. NCCM Justin McNicol, at (757)836-7780/DSN 836 or via email at justin.r.mcnicol.mil@us.navy.mil. c. U.S. Pacific Fleet, Fleet Career Counselor. NCCM Jason Caldera, at (808)474-5848/DSN 315 or via email at jason.caldera.mil@navy.mil. d. Naval Forces Europe and Africa, Fleet Career Counselor. NCCM Lalia M. Canlas, at +39-081-568-3497/DSN 314-626-3497 or via e-mail at lalia.m.canlas.mil@us.navy.mil e. Commander, Naval Installation Command Force Counselor. NCCM Yenier Ramirez, at 202-433-2919 or via email at yenier.ramirez2.mil@us.navy.mil f. Chief of Naval Reserve Force Counselor. NCCM Melody Rivas, at 703-693-8594 or via email at melody.b.rivas.mil@us.navy.mil g. Registration: NCC David Butler, at 850-882-4117 or via email at david.b.butler10.mil@us.navy.mil
8. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//

item.title: NAVADMIN 025/26 - 2026 ACTIVE DUTY FUND DRIVE IN SUPPORT OF THE NAVY-MARINE CORPS RELIEF SOCIETY
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0402000-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 092000Z FEB 26 MID320018528064U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 025/26
SUBJ/2026 ACTIVE DUTY FUND DRIVE IN SUPPORT OF THE NAVY-MARINE CORPS RELIEF SOCIETY//
REF/A/DOC/SECNAVINST 5340.7B/02JUN2023// REF/B/DOC/DOD WASHINGTON DC/15MAY2024// REF/C/DOC/FUNDRAISING ACTIVITIES/23SEP2024//
NARR/REF A IS SECNAVINST 5340.7B, FUNDRAISING AND SOLICIATION IN SUPPORT OF THE NAVY-MARINE CORPS RELIEF SOCIETY. REF B IS DOD 5500.07-R, JOINT ETHICS REGULATION. REF C IS STANDARDS OF ETHICAL CONDUCT FOR EMPLOYEES OF THE EXECUTIVE BRANCH, FUNDRAISING ACTIVITIES (5 C.F.R.2635.808).//
RMKS/1. Per reference (a), all U.S. Navy commands and activities with Navy and Marine Corps personnel will conduct an Active Duty Fund Drive (ADFD) in support of Navy-Marine Corps Relief Society (NMCRS) from 1 March to 30 April 2026. For 122 years, NMCRS has been a light when dark times have fallen on fellow shipmates and Marines. The annual fund drive is an opportunity to support them. The campaign theme, "By Our Own, For Our Own," embodies our shared commitment to take care of our community.
2. In 2025, the Society provided about $50 million in critical financial assistance through grants and interest-free loans to more than 48,000 members of our Navy-Marine Corps community. On average, over 200,000 Sailors, Marines, and family members will use their programs and services each year.
3. The Society does not receive government funding; it only relies on the support of Active Duty and retired Sailors and Marines and the generosity of corporations, foundations, and individual donors
4. The confidential financial assistance NMCRS provides to our Sailors and Marines gives peace of mind when a financial crisis exceeds their financial ability and keeps them mission ready. Your contributions support interest-free loans and grants for shipmates facing financial challenges, including those dealing with basic living expense issues and family emergencies. In addition, the budget counseling and financial education provided by NMCRS volunteers allows our Navy and Marine Corps families to be better prepared to address the financial responsibility of adding a new family member or being prepared for emergencies.
5. Success in meeting our goals requires engaged leadership and management at all levels. Early assignment of motivated fund drive coordinators and key people ensures time to reach 100 percent of our Sailors and Marines. Staying engaged throughout the drive is imperative to meeting the goal of 100 percent meaningful contact to provide the opportunity to learn about the critically important programs and services NMCRS provides and offering the opportunity to donate.
6. During the NMCRS ADFD, Sailors and Marines are authorized to endorse, support, and participate in fundraising events as long as commands and organizations uphold the Department of the Navy's policy of truly voluntary giving. a. Commands and organizations should review the regulations governing fundraising for NMCRS to determine which activities are permissible during the fund drive. Per reference (a), authorized ADFD fundraising activities in support of NMCRS include, but are not limited to command-sponsored raffles, lotteries, bake sales, car washes, or bowling tournaments. Fundraising events must be conducted on Department of War owned or leased property; otherwise, the event loses its "By Our Own, For Our Own" designation and cannot be command-endorsed. Commands and organizations are also strongly encouraged to communicate the benefits of NMCRS and its programs and achieving 100 percent meaningful contact through speeches, meetings, and videos, both in person and shared on social media. Limited appropriated fund support is permissible for kick-off events, award ceremonies, and other non-solicitation events to build support. b. References (a) through (c) prohibit any coercive activities counter to the policy of truly voluntary giving, such as setting unit-level dollar goals or participation goals. Any action, or perceived action, that does not allow Service Members to have free choice to give, or not to give, or any action, or perceived action that Service Members do not have a free choice to publicize their gifts or keep them confidential are prohibited. The following fundraising activities are also prohibited: selling civilian clothes privileges or special liberty, raffling government parking spaces, sponsoring casino-like games of chance, soliciting to civilian or contractor personnel, or soliciting outside of the federal workplace. Civilian or contractor personnel are allowed to support the ADFD through online donations and participating in fundraisers, but may not be solicited to do so. c. Unit coordinators for the NMCRS ADFD should consult their Staff Judge Advocate or ethics official for further guidance with regard to specific questions.
7. I am confident that with the active support and engagement of our leadership, NMCRS will continue the critical work of helping our Sailors, Marines, and their families. I encourage all who are able to consider making a contribution through payroll allotment, online credit card, or cash to help our brothers and sisters in need. We need to take full advantage of this annual opportunity to help our shipmates and fellow Marines through the 2026 ADFD campaign.
8. Built in the Foundry - Tempered in the Fleet - Forged to Fight.
9. ADM Daryl Caudle, 34th Chief of Naval Operations sends.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 023/26 - CHIEF OF NAVAL OPERATIONS FY25 THIRD QUARTER GOLD DISK AWARDEES
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ RTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0401619-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU R 091619Z FEB 26 MID320018527557U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN COMMARFORCOM COMMARCORSYSCOM LCES QUANTICO VA INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
SUBJ/CHIEF OF NAVAL OPERATIONS FY25 THIRD QUARTER GOLD DISK AWARDEES
NAVADMIN 023/26//
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC //N8//FEB//
REF/A/JFMM VOLUME VI, CHAPTER 8//
AMPN/REF A IS THE JOINT FLEET MAINTENANCE MANUAL 2M ELECTRONIC REPAIR CHAPTER//
REMARKS/1. In 1997, the Deputy Chief of Naval Operations (Logistics) implemented a CNO Gold Disk Awards Program. This program was designed to encourage fleet personnel in the maritime community to develop gold disk test routines and repair circuit card assemblies and electronic modules. The resulting benefits have resulted in improved operational readiness of a wide range of command, control, communications, computers, combat systems, intelligence and hull mechanical and electrical systems in addition to significantly improved OPTAR cost avoidance. The award consists of a letter of Commendation signed by Deputy CNO for Integration of Capabilities and Resources and a military cash award of up to five thousand dollars.
2. The cash award criteria is a graduated dollar value dependent upon cost avoidance thresholds. The award program not only rewards the top repair technician, but every Technician that meets the following quarterly cost avoidance thresholds: $150k to $199k of cost avoidance yields a $2k award, $200k to $249k of cost avoidance yields a $3k award, $250k to $299k of cost avoidance yields a $4k award, $300k and over of cost avoidance yields a $5k award.
3. The CNO Gold Disk Awards Program is administered by the NAVSEA Miniature Microminiature Module Test and Repair (2M MTR) program manager. Fleet 2M MTR policy and responsibilities are contained in reference (a). Gold Disk awardees are selected by an awards board at NUWC Detachment Field Engineering Office in Norfolk. The MTR engineering agents selection is based on 2M repair metrics submitted in the module test and repair tracking system or verification of recently submitted Gold Disk test routines.
4. I am proud to announce the 3rd quarter FY25 awardees: (READ IN TWO COLUMNS) ET1 KEVIN ADAMS USS FARRAGUT (DDG 99) IC1 STEVEN BROWN USS RAFAEL PERALTA (DDG 115) ET1 LOUIS CATANZARO SERMC MAYPORT FL IC1 DAMIEN CONTRERAS NNSY CAL CENTER NORFOLK VA ET1 MEREDYTH WALLS FACSFAC SAN DIEGO CA ET2 BRENDAN CAREY NAVSUBSUPFAC NEW LONDON CT ET2 LUIS CASTANEDA SWRMC SAN DIEGO CA ET2 JOSEPH EARLEN USS COLE (DDG 67) ET2 WILLIAM FROMMEL USS ROBERT SMALLS (CG 62) ET2 TIMOTHY MORROW USS GEORGE H W BUSH (CVN 77) ET2 JULIUS RAHNEY USS GEORGE WASHINGTON (CVN 73) ET2 GERARDO ROSADO-CORDOVA USS DONALD COOK (DDG 75) ET2 JAKE SMITH SWRMC SAN DIEGO CA ET2 WINSTON WYLIE MARMC NORFOLK VA CPL CHRISTIAN GONZALEZ ELMACO 2ND MAINT BN ET3 DUSTIN SCHWARTZ USS CHAFEE (DDG 90)
5. NUWC DET FEO NORFOLK POC is Mr. Mike Bruzan, 2M/MTR Technical Project Manager, (757) 746-0094 or via e-mail, michael.s.bruzan.civ(at)us.navy.mil. The NAVSEA 2M MTR PM, is Mr. RichardR. Stark, comm (850) 207-6985 or via e-mail richard.r.stark.civ(at)us.navy.mil
6. Released by Mr. Neil W. T. Hogg, SES, Assistant Deputy Chief of Naval Operations, N8B.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 019/26 - EXERCISE-EXERCISE-EXERCISE ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY IN SUPPORT OF U.S FORCES JAPAN FOR KEEN EDGE EXERCISE 2026
item.description:
UNCLASSIFIED/ PTTUZYUW RUOIAAA0001 0302200-UUUU--RHSSSUU. ZNR UUUUU P R 302200Z JAN 26 ZYB MID320018508670U FM COMPACFLT PEARL HARBOR HI TO NAVADMIN INFO CDR USAFRICOM STUTTGART GE CDR USCENTCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USEUCOM VAIHINGEN GE CDR USNORTHCOM PETERSON AFB CO CDR USPACOM HONOLULU HI CDR USSOCOM MACDILL AFB FL CDR USSOUTHCOM MIAMI FL CDR USSTRATCOM OFFUTT AFB NE CDR USTRANSCOM SCOTT AFB IL CMC WASHINGTON DC CNIC WASHINGTON DC CNO WASHINGTON DC COMFLEACT CHINHAE KOR COMNAVFORKOREA BUSAN KOR COMNAVREGKOREA CHINHAE KOR COMPACFLT PEARL HARBOR HI COMUSFLTFORCOM NORFOLK VA DARPA ARLINGTON VA DCMA HQ ALEXANDRIA VA DFAS HQ ARLINGTON VA DIA WASHINGTON DC DIRSSP WASHINGTON DC DLA FT BELVOIR VA DTRA ALEX WASHINGTON DC JOINT STAFF WASHINGTON DC MDA WASHINGTON DC NGA WASHINGTON DC NSA FT GEORGE G MEADE MD SECDEF WASHINGTON DC USCENTCOM COMMAND CENTER MACDILL AFB FL BT UNCLAS UNCLAS
SUBJ/EXERCISE-EXERCISE-EXERCISE ORDER TO ACCOUNT FOR THE NAVY FAMILY IN SUPPORT OF U.S FORCES JAPAN FOR KEEN EDGE EXERCISE 2026//
NAVADMIN 019/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/USPACFLT/N1/JAN//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAVINST 3006.1/01MAR2011// REF/B/DOC/DODINST 3001.02/03MAY2010// REF/C/DOC/DODINST 1015.15/20MAR2008// REF/D/DOC/NAVSOP-1000/12DEC2002// REF/E/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171821ZMAR09// REF/F/MSG/PTC WASHINGTON DC/171846ZMAR09//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 3006.1, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH CATASTROPHIC EVENTS, NAVY PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY POLICY. REF B IS DODINST 3001.02, PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY IN CONJUNCTION WITH NATURAL OR MAN-MADE DISASTERS. REF C IS DODINST 1015.15, PROCEDURES AND SUPPORT FOR NON-FEDERAL ENTITIES AUTHORIZED TO OPERATE ON DOD INSTALLATIONS (ENCL 4). REF D IS FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT POLICY MANUAL. REF E IS ALARACT 070/2009, CONUS EVACUATION ALLOWANCES. REF F IS ALARACT 071/2009, NONCOMBATANT EVACUATION ORDER/OCONUS EVACUATION ALLOWANCES.//
RMKS/1. THIS ORDER TO ACCOUNT APPLIES TO ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, NAF EMPLOYEES, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS WITHIN THE ESTABLISHED GEOGRAPHIC AREA OF INTEREST (GAOI) DESIGNATED IN PARAGRAPH 2. ECHELON II COMMANDERS AND ALL REGIONAL COMMANDERS SHALL ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION TO ALL SUBORDINATE AND TENANT COMMANDS AND DETACHMENTS FOR IMMEDIATE ACTION UPON RECEIPT.
2. THE FOLLOWING GAOI HAS BEEN ESTABLISHED FOR KEEN EDGE 2026 EXERCISE: CNRJ/CNFJ UIC: 57006, 61076, 3997K, 61057, 46184, 61060, 46185, 3941J, 61058, 46195, 58059, 3994J, 61054, 46193, 58058, 3995J, 61056, 46186, 58057, 68539, 46198, 58060, 3942J, 43666.
3. ALL COMMANDS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. COMMANDING OFFICERS (CO) AND/OR COMMANDING OFFICER REPRESENTATIVES (COR) WILL ENSURE THAT THEIR COMMAND'S NAVY FAMILY ACCOUNTABILITY AND ASSESSMENT SYSTEM (NFAAS) DATABASE IS CURRENT AND ACCURATE, TO INCLUDE PHONE NUMBERS AND ADDRESSES. ENSURE ANY RECENT CHECK-INS TO YOUR COMMAND WHO HAVE NOT HAD THEIR CONTACT INFORMATION UPDATED IN NFAAS, COMMUNICATE RECALL INFORMATION TO THEIR RESPECTIVE CHAIN OF COMMAND. B. FOLLOWING THE EVENT, MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR ALL NAVY ACTIVE DUTY, RESERVE, DON CIVILIAN EMPLOYEES, NAF EMPLOYEES, NEX EMPLOYEES, AND APPLICABLE DEPENDENTS IN THE GAOI. ALL CORS/SPONSORS IN THE AFFECTED GAOI WILL RECEIVE NOTIFICATION FROM NFAAS VIA TEXT/EMAIL TO MUSTER THEMSELVES AND DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS. COS AND/OR CORS WILL ENSURE WIDEST DISSEMINATION OF REQUIREMENT FOR ACCURATE AND TIMELY ACCOUNTABILITY VIA NFAAS. IF MEMBERS ARE UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, COS/CORS WILL DETERMINE ACCURATE STATUS AND WHEREABOUTS OF THEIR PERSONNEL AND MUSTER AND ACCOUNT FOR THEM AT HOME, WORK, TELEWORK, EVACUATED, OR DISPLACED LOCATION, OR NOT IN THE GAOI WITH SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED, TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OTHER). C. COMMANDS SHALL DIRECT CORS/SPONSORS TO MUSTER IN NFAAS, AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL/CAS/LOGIN?SERVIC. IF NFAAS IS NOT AVAILABLE, COMMANDS/MEMBERS ARE TO REPORT THE STATUS OF PERSONNEL ACCOUNTABILITY (PA) TO THE AFFECTED REGION OPERATIONS CENTER (ROC) OR DIRECT CHAIN OF COMMAND. D. DO NOT DELAY REPORTING ON PERSONNEL UNTIL ALL INFORMATION IS COMPLETE. REPORT AND ACCOUNT MUSTERING INFORMATION WHEN OBTAINED, INCLUDING THE POSTING OF AMPLIFYING DATA IN THE NOTES AREA ON THE INDIVIDUAL'S ACCOUNTING LINE.
4. ALL CORS/SPONSORS ARE EXPECTED TO: A. ACCOUNT FOR THEMSELVES AND DEPENDENTS IN NFAAS AT HTTPS://NAVYFAMILY.NAVY.MIL/CAS/LOGIN?SERVIC. B. IF UNABLE TO ACCESS NFAAS, SPONSORS SHOULD CONTACT THEIR COR, COMMAND MUSTERING OFFICER, OR SUPERVISOR TO CHECK IN (MUSTER). C. SPONSORS WILL PROVIDE COMMAND REPRESENTATIVES WITH CURRENT RESIDENCE, DISPLACED LOCATION, OR IF NOT IN THE GAOI, WITH SPECIFIED CIRCUMSTANCE (DEPLOYED,TDY/TAD/IA, ON LEAVE, SEPARATED/RETIRED, TRANSFERRED, UA/DESERTER, OTHER).
5. A NEEDS ASSESSMENT N/A.
6. FOR NFAAS TECHNICAL SUPPORT, CONTACT THE NFAAS HELP DESK AT 866-946-9183 OR VIA EMAIL AT NFAAS(AT).NAVY.MIL.
7. IF UNABLE TO MUSTER VIA NFAAS, YOUR COR, YOUR MUSTERING POC, OR YOUR CHAIN OF COMMAND, CONTACT THE BELOW POCS (LOCATION/PHONE/EMAIL): A. NAVY REGION JAPAN N14/315-243-3155, DERRON ALLEYNE, DERRON.C.ALLEYNE.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL. B. NAVY REGION JAPAN N15/315-243-2903, RUBEN WILLIAMS II, RUBEN.WILLIAMS5.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL C. CNIC BWC/202-433-4321/CNIC.N36.BWC(AT)NAVY.MIL. D. PACFLT CAT BWC/808-474-5452/5453/CPF.CATBWC(AT)NAVY.MIL. E. NFAAS PROGRAM MGRS/202-207-4402/TERENDA.B.RUFFIN.CIV(AT)NAVY.MIL OR 202-433-6770/MICHAEL.Z.PRENDINGUE.MIL(AT)NAVY.MIL.
8. RELEASED BY CAPT KEITH ARCHIBALD, N1B, U.S. PACIFIC FLEET/808-471-0470/KEITH.ARCHIBALD.MIL(AT)US.NAVY.MIL.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 021/26 - NOTICE OF CONVENING FISCAL YEAR 2027 NAVY RESERVE TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE RESERVE RETENTION AND CONTINUATION SELECTION BOARDS
item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ ROUTINE R 291932Z JAN 26 MID320018505026U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 021/26
PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/JAN//
SUBJ/NOTICE OF CONVENING FISCAL YEAR 2027 NAVY RESERVE TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION OF THE RESERVE RETENTION AND CONTINUATION SELECTION BOARDS//
REF/A/DOC/SECNAVINST//24JUL19// REF/B/DOC/SECNAVMEMO//10DEC24// REF/C/DOC/SECNAVINST//22JAN19//
NARR/REF A IS SECNAVINST 1920.6D, ADMINISTRATIVE SEPARATION OF OFFICERS. REF B IS THE FISCAL YEAR 2025 AND 2026 NAVY RESERVE OFFICER RETENTION AND CONTINUATION POLICY AND PLAN. REF C IS SECNAVINST 1920.7C, CONTINUATION ON ACTIVE DUTY OF REGULAR COMMISSIONED OFFICERS AND RESERVE OFFICERS ON THE RESERVE ACTIVE STATUS LIST IN THE NAVY AND MARINE CORPS.
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces the Fiscal Year (FY) 2027 Navy Reserve Training and Administration of the Reserve (TAR) Officer Retention and Continuation Selection Boards. For Selected Reserve Officers, retention or continuation during FY-27 is administered in accordance with the annual Navy Reserve Officer Retention and Continuation Policy and Plan. The TAR Lieutenant (LT) and Lieutenant Commander (LCDR) Continuation Selection Boards will convene, when required, immediately following the corresponding FY-27 Navy Reserve TAR Promotion Selection Boards. The TAR Commander (CDR) Selective Retention (SELRET) Board will convene 2 June 2026 and the Captain (CAPT) Selective Retention (SELRET) Board will convene 14 September 2026. Eligible TAR officers on the Reserve Active Status List (RASL) who are fully qualified to remain on Active Duty and meet the requirements in paragraphs 3 through 5 will be considered for retention on Active Duty, and if required, continuation on the RASL.
2. Please read this NAVADMIN in its entirety as there is significant information throughout that may affect eligibility for consideration by the respective boards.
3. Effect of Failure of Selection for Promotion: a. A Navy TAR LT who twice failed of selection (2XFOS) for promotion to LCDR and whose name is not on a promotion list is subject to involuntary release from Active Duty, and if required, removed from the RASL in line with reference (a) enclosure (5) paragraph 4.a and 4.c, unless the officer is retained or continued on Active Duty and, if required, retained or continued on the RASL (1) under section 12686 of Title 10, U.S. Code (18-year sanctuary for a regular retirement) or (2) by a TAR LT Retention and Continuation Selection Board as provided for under section 14701 of Title 10, U.S Code, as applicable. In line with reference (b), TAR officers in the grade of LT may be continued on Active Duty for up to four opportunities for promotion to LCDR on the RASL. b. A Navy TAR LCDR who 2XFOS for promotion to CDR and whose name is not on a promotion list is subject to involuntary release from Active Duty, and if required, removed from the RASL in accordance with ref (a) enclosure (5) paragraph 4.b and 4.c, unless the officer is retained or continued on Active Duty and, if required, retained or continued on the RASL (1) under section 12686 of Title 10, U.S. Code (18-year sanctuary for a regular retirement) or (2) by a TAR LCDR Retention and Continuation Selection Board, as applicable.
4. TAR LT: a. 2XFOS LTs with less than 18 Years of Active Service (YOAS) on the date of mandatory release from Active Duty under paragraph 3. Officers selected for retention and continuation will be continued on Active Duty and on the RASL, subject to the limits of paragraph 4.d, and not to exceed four opportunities in line with reference (b). Officers released from Active Duty with less than 20 Years of Commissioned Service (YCS) may request affiliation as a drilling reservist in the Selected Reserve (SELRES). To affiliate as a drilling reservist, contact your nearest Navy Recruiting Reserve Talent Acquisition Group (RTAG). Officers released from Active Duty under paragraph 3 with 20 or more YCS will not be continued on the RASL in accordance with section 14701 of Title 10, U.S. Code. b. 2XFOS LTs with 18-20 YOAS on the date of mandatory release from Active Duty under paragraph 3. Retention Selection board action is not required. Officers will be retained on Active Duty, and if required, continued on the RASL until eligible for a regular retirement under section 8323 of Title 10, U.S. Code, subject to the limits of paragraph 4.d. c. 2XFOS LTs with 20 or more YOAS on the date of mandatory release from Active Duty under paragraph 3, who are eligible for a regular retirement under section 8323 of Title 10, U.S. Code, will be retired. d. In no case will any LT be retained or continued on Active Duty or on the RASL beyond the last day of the month in which the officer completes 20 YCS under section 14701 of Title 10, U.S. Code or the last day of the month in which the officer becomes 62 years of age, whichever is earlier.
5. TAR LCDR: a. 2XFOS LCDRs with less than 14 years of active service (YOAS) on the date of mandatory release from Active Duty under paragraph 3. Officers selected for retention and continuation will be continued on Active Duty and on the RASL for a period of 3 years, subject to the limits of paragraph 5.e. Officers released from Active Duty with less than 20 YCS may request affiliation as a drilling reservist in the SELRES. Officers released from Active Duty with 20 or more YCS may request continuation on the RASL and may request consideration for affiliation as a drilling reservist. To affiliate as a drilling reservist, contact your nearest Navy Recruiting RTAG. b. 2XFOS LCDRs with 14-18 YOAS on the date of mandatory release from Active Duty under paragraph 3. Officers selected for continuation will be continued on Active Duty and on the RASL until eligible for a regular retirement under section 8323 of Title 10, U.S. Code, subject to the limits of paragraph 5.e. Officers released from Active Duty with less than 20 YCS may request affiliation in the SELRES as a drilling reservist. Officers released from Active Duty with 20 or more YCS may request continuation on the RASL and may request consideration for affiliation as a drilling reservist. To affiliate as a drilling reservist, contact your nearest Navy Recruiting RTAG. c. 2XFOS LCDRs with 18-20 YOAS on the date of mandatory release from Active Duty under paragraph 3. Retention Selection board action is not required. Officers will be retained on Active Duty, and if required, continued on the RASL until eligible for a regular retirement under section 8323 of Title 10, U.S. Code, subject to the limits of paragraph 5.e. d. 2XFOS LCDRs with 20 or more YOAS on the date of mandatory release from Active Duty under paragraph 3. Officers with 20 or more YOAS, who are eligible for a regular retirement under section 8323 of Title 10, U.S. Code, will be retired. e. In no case will any LCDR be retained or continued on Active Duty or on the RASL beyond the last day of the month in which the officer completes 24 YCS under section 14701 of Title 10, U.S. Code or the last day of the month in which the officer becomes 62 years of age, whichever is earlier.
6. TAR CDR SELRET Board: a. The TAR CDR SELRET Board is authorized to retain TAR commanders who were: (1) twice considered and failed of selection to Captain with more than 17 YOAS as of 1 September 2026, (2) are not on a promotion list to a higher grade in order to meet requirements and authorized end strength, and (3) were not previously considered by a TAR CDR SELRET Board. b. TAR Commanders selected for retention that would otherwise exceed 28 YCS as a result of their selection will undergo an additional screen by the SELRET board for continuation in line with references (a) through (c). In this process, selection for retention is a prerequisite for continuation screening, however, selection for retention does not equate to approval for continuation. In line with reference (c), the board will make a continuation approval determination based on the needs of the Navy for TAR officers who exhibit superior performance in reserve management holding one or more of the following Additional Qualification Designation (AQD) codes: 2D1 or 2D2 Command eligible or qualified, 2N1 or 2N2 Reserve Command eligible or qualified. Approval for both retention and continuation by the board meets the exceptions to policy set forth in enclosure (5), paragraph 5.f. of reference (a). c. In line with reference (a), TAR O5s not selected for retention will retire or be released from Active Duty not later than the first day of the seventh calendar month beginning after the month in which the SELRET board results are released. TAR officers will be retained on Active Duty, if necessary, to enable the officer to become eligible for a regular Active Duty retirement. Except as provided by exception in reference (a), retention of TAR O5s on Active Duty will not normally extend beyond the first day of the month following the month in which the officers complete 28 YCS unless also approved for continuation by the SELRET board. O5s retained by this SELRET board, to include those that may receive continuation approval, will be released from active duty not later than the first day of September of the FY following the SELRET board.
7. TAR CAPT SELRET Board: a. The TAR CAPT SELRET Board is required to retain TAR Captains beyond 3 years time in grade bringing TAR inventory in line with authorized end strength, allowing for stable promotion opportunity. b. TAR Captains who were promoted during FY-25 will be considered for their first gate and TAR captains who were promoted during FY-22 will be considered for their second gate. All eligible officers will be notified by community management. In line with reference (a), second gate TAR Captains not selected for retention will retire or be released from Active Duty no later than 1 September 2027, and first gate TAR Captains not selected for retention will retire or be released from Active Duty no later than 1 September 2028. In either case, TAR officers will be retained on Active Duty, if necessary, to enable the officer to become eligible for a regular Active-Duty retirement. c. TAR Captains who have been selected for second gate retention on a previous CAPT SELRET Board, may request continuation beyond 30 YCS in line with references (a) through (c). In this process, prior selection for retention is a prerequisite for continuation screening, however, selection for retention does not equate to approval for continuation. In line with reference (c), the board will make a continuation approval determination within Title 10 control grade limitations, and based upon the needs of the Navy for TAR officers who exhibit superior performance in reserve management holding one or more of the following AQD codes: 2D4 Major Command screened, or RB6 Human Resource Captain Command or Milestone qualified. Approval for continuation by the board meets the exceptions to policy set forth in enclosure (5), paragraph 5.f of reference (a).
8. TAR Officers on the RASL who meet any of the criteria in paragraphs 3-5 shall check BUPERS Online, via MyNavy Portal at https://my.navy.mil/quick-links.html, after release of the respective selection ALNAV message, to determine if they were selected for retention and continuation. Once logged into BUPERS Online, click on Advancements/Selection Boards and then on the appropriate officer (Promotion/Continuation) board to determine the results.
9. Promotion selection board, retention and continuation selection board, and SELRET board convening dates are as follows: a. The FY-27 Reserve TAR O-5 Staff Corps Promotion Selection Board convenes 3 March 2026. The FY-27 Reserve TAR O-4 Staff Corps Retention and Continuation Selection board will follow this board. b. The FY-27 Reserve TAR O-5 Line Promotion Selection Board convenes 10 March 2026. The FY-27 Reserve TAR O-4 Line Retention and Continuation Selection board will follow this board. c. The FY-27 Reserve TAR O-4 Staff Corps Promotion Selection Board convenes 26 May 2026. The FY-27 Reserve TAR O-3 Staff Corps Retention and Continuation Selection board will follow this board. d. The FY-27 Reserve TAR O-5 SELRET Board convenes 2 June 2026. e. Reserve TAR O-4 Line Promotion Selection Board convenes 15 June 2026. The FY-27 Reserve TAR O-3 Line Retention and Continuation Selection board will follow this board. f. The FY-27 Reserve TAR O-6 SELRET Board convenes 14 September 2026.
10. Correspondence addressed to the promotion selection board will not be forwarded to a subsequent continuation selection board or selective retention board for consideration. Officers must communicate with each board separately.
11. Only eligible officers may communicate with a continuation selection board via the Letter to the Board (LTB) process. Officers are encouraged to use the Electronic Submission of Selection Board Documents (ESSBD) process for LTB submission, accessible through My Navy Portal (MNP). ESSBD improves the speed, transparency and confidence of receipt over current LTB submission methods, such as hard copy and e-mail submitted letters and related attachments. a. ESSBD is available for all promotion, advancement and selection boards. b. ESSBD provides board candidates with the capability to electronically submit LTBs in a standard, consistent format, with or without attachments and will allow the candidate to see (for content and clarity) exactly what will be delivered and presented to the board. c. To utilize ESSBD, candidates must access document services through MNP at https://mn3p.navy.mil/web/myrecord/overview. On-screen instructions will direct the submitter thereafter. d. Submission and subsequent receipt acknowledgement for LTBs submitted via ESSBD or other means does not constitute confirmation of eligibility for the boards to which LTBs are submitted. If required, a full visual user guide is available through MNP at https://mn3p.navy.mil/web/advancement-and-promotion. e. LTBs must be received by Navy Personnel Command no later than 2359 (Central Standard Time) (CST) 10 days prior to the convening date of the respective board, as noted below. Submitters are cautioned to not wait until the deadline date to submit LTBs via ESSBD or other available avenues.
Board Convening Date Due Date / Time TAR O4 Staff Ret/Cont Board 03 MAR 26 20 FEB 26 / 2359 CST TAR O4 Line Ret/Cont Board 10 MAR 26 27 FEB 26 / 2359 CST TAR O3 Staff Ret/Cont Board 26 MAY 26 15 MAY 26 / 2359 CDT TAR O5 SELRET/Cont Board 02 JUN 26 22 MAY 26 / 2359 CDT TAR O3 Line Ret/Cont Board 15 JUN 26 04 JUN 26 / 2359 CDT TAR O6 SELRET/Cont Board 14 SEP 26 03 SEP 26 / 2359 CDT
f. If unable to use ESSBD, the CSCSELBOARD@navy.mil mailbox account is capable of receiving encrypted correspondence in accordance with Department of Defense policy. If sending a board package from a non-NMCI network (bumed.mil, eu.navy.mil, etc.), please review procedures listed in NAVADMIN 220/19. g. The written communication may call attention to the board, any matter concerning the record of the officer that the officer considers important. Written communication may include, as enclosures, correspondence from any individual concerning the eligible officer. Correspondence not originated by the eligible officer, including endorsement to the letter of the eligible officer and letters written on behalf of that officer, must contain a written acknowledgement by the eligible officer that he or she desires such correspondence be presented to the board. Correspondence without such an acknowledgement is considered third party correspondence and will be returned without being presented to the board. Classified correspondence will not be accepted.
12. Questions regarding LTB submission should be directed to MyNavy Career Center by calling 1-833-330-MNCC (1-833-330-6622/DSN: 882-6622).
13. All officers are reminded of their responsibility to ensure the completeness and accuracy of their official records prior to the convening date of the board.
14. Amplifying information can be accessed via MyNavyHR website at https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/Career-Management/Community-Management/Officer/Reserve-OCM/TAR/TAR-Continuation/. The authorized number of selections for retention will be posted as part of the board convening order after the board adjourns.
15. This NAVADMIN will be remain in effect until superseded or 30 September 2027, whichever occurs first.
16. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 020/26 - PUBLICATION OF THE NAVY WARFIGHTING CONCEPT VERSION 1.0
item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ ROUTINE R 290017Z JAN 26 MID320018502981U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N3N5N7//
NAVADMIN 020/26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N3N5N7/JAN//
SUBJ/PUBLICATION OF THE NAVY WARFIGHTING CONCEPT VERSION 1.0//
REF/A/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/151949ZJAN26// AMPN/NAVADMIN 010/26, C-NOte #4: The Way We Fight//
RMKS/1. As announced and summarized in REF A, the Chief of Naval Operations has approved and published the Navy Warfighting Concept Version 1.0 (NWC 1.0).
2. CNO directs all Navy leadership to read, understand, and implement the NWC 1.0 using rigorous analysis, wargames, exercises, and evaluations to design, posture, and employ the Fleet for the challenges ahead.
3. NWC 1.0 can be found on the Navy Warfare Development Center SIPRNET: https://nwdc.navy.smil.mil/Pages/home.aspx
4. Feedback and recommendations can be directed to the OPNAV N57 staff at M_OPNAV_N57_UD(at)navy.smil.mil
5. Released by VADM Y. M. Davids, Deputy Chief of Naval Operations for Operations, Plans, Strategy, and Warfighting Development (N3N5N7).//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 017/26 - ACADEMIC YEAR 2026-2027 WARFIGHTER EDUCATION AND PROFESSIONAL STUDIES (WEPS) PROGRAM AND NAVAL WAR COLLEGE FLEET SEMINAR FULL TIME PILOT OPPORTUNITY
item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ ROUTINE R 231709Z JAN 26 MID320018491726U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 017/26
PASS TO OFFICE CODES: INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC/N3N5N7//
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N3N5N7/JAN//
SUBJ/ACADEMIC YEAR 2026-2027 WARFIGHTER EDUCATION AND PROFESSIONAL STUDIES (WEPS) PROGRAM AND NAVAL WAR COLLEGE FLEET SEMINAR FULL TIME PILOT OPPORTUNITY//
REF/A/DOC/CNO WASHINGTON DC/07JAN15// REF/B/DOC/CNO WASHINGTON DC/08JAN15// REF/C/DOC/NAVPERS 15839I// REF/D/MEMO/SECWAR/22MAY25// REF/E/RMG 031922ZAPR25// REF/F/RMG 251459ZOCT18//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 1520.23C CH-3, GRADUATE EDUCATION. REF B IS OPNAVINST 1520.24D, OFFICER SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM. REF C IS MANUAL OF NAVY OFFICER MANPOWER PERSONNEL CLASSIFICATIONS. REF D IS OSW MEMORANDUM, PERMANENT CHANGE OF STATION TARGETED REDUCTIONS REVIEW AND PERSONNEL POLICY CHANGES. REF E IS NAVADMIN 070/25, ACADEMIC YEAR 2025-2026 NAVAL WAR COLLEGE FLEET SEMINAR PROGRAM. REF F IS NAVADMIN 263/18, UPDATE TO NAVY GRADUATE EDUCATION PROGRAM. //
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN announces the Academic Year (AY) 2026-2027 Warfighter Education and Professional Studies (WEPS) program and Fleet Seminar Full Time (FSFT) pilot program.
2. The Warfighter Education and Professional Studies - Full Time (WEPS-FT) program is a Navy talent and warfighter development initiative that selects highly performing Unrestricted Line (URL) and Information Warfare Line (IWL) Officers to attend in-residence graduate education at civilian institutions (CIVINS). WEPS is the follow-on program to the previous Fleet Scholars Education Program (FSEP). a. In accordance with reference D, student officers are highly encouraged to select CIVINS within commuting distance of current (or pending) duty stations within the United States. Hybrid programs allowing the student officer to stay at their permanent duty station and travel (self-funded) to the selected civilian institution for in-person cohort time, are acceptable and credit for In Resident Graduate Education (IRGE) requirement (per reference F) will be granted. Selectees for WEPS-FT will be administratively assigned to the closest senior Navy Reserve Officer Training Corps (NROTC) or Navy Support unit. b. WEPS-FT is available at the Lieutenant (LT) and Lieutenant Commander (LCDR) paygrades. Top performing Lieutenant Junior Grade (LTJG) Officers may be considered if scheduled to be promoted to LT before reporting to their selected school for graduate education. c. WEPS-FT participants detailed to a self-selected CIVINS will have up to 24 months to complete a graduate degree while in a full-time student status. Participants will be required to complete a degree that will earn a subspecialty code (SSC) indicated by the approved areas of study listed in paragraph 5. d. WEPS-FT will also select officers for attendance at Purdue Military Research Institute (PMRI) and Georgia Tech Research Institute (GTRI), as part of WEPS-FT. PMRI and GTRI are still available to newly commissioned officers part of the Immediate Graduate Education Program (IGEP). (1) Officers selected for PMRI will attend Purdue University in- residence and have up to 24 months to complete a master's degree, or up to 36 months to complete a PhD. Details on PMRI can be found at https://engineering.purdue.edu/PMRI. (2) Officers selected for GTRI will attend Georgia Institute of Technology in-residence as a Military Graduate Research Assistant and have up to 24 months to complete a master's degree. Details on GTRI can be found at https://gtri.gatech.edu/outreach/mgrp. e. OPNAV will fund up to $40,000/yr of tuition and fees. Any graduate education program with tuition and fees that exceed that amount may be covered via a non-Navy funded scholarship in accordance with Officer Scholarship Program (reference B), by the student officer's respective community, or self-funded via Post-9/11 G.I. Bill or other means (Tuition Assistance is prohibited). PRMI and GTRI are fully funded opportunities. f. Officers who have already received fully funded Navy provided graduate education are not eligible to participate. Graduate degrees obtained through Naval War College, Post 9-11 G.I. Bill, Tuition Assistance, or the Navy College Program for Afloat College Education are not disqualifiers. Selectees will be assigned an Additional Qualification Designation (AQD) for tracking and career management purposes. g. Final selections will be made via community processes. h. Community specific quotas allocated in the Strategic Education and Alignment (SEA) Plan are as follows:
CIVINS GTRI PMRI SURFOR 1110 6 1 1 SUBFOR 1120 5 1 1 NSW 1130 2 1 AIRFOR 13X0 4 1 1 IWL 1810 1 1 1820 1 1 1830 1
i. Upon notification of selection to WEPS-FT, selectees should apply to no more than three accredited graduate education institutions that have an existing Education Services Agreement (ESA). For a list of schools associated with an ESA, please contact OPNAV N711 at OPNAV_N711@us.navy.mil. Participants may be reimbursed for up to those three application and transcript fees after beginning their first semester/quarter. Participants are responsible for researching and completing prerequisites including the Graduate Record Examination (GRE) where applicable (Navy will not reimburse for GRE fees). Enrollment will begin no earlier than 01 July 2026 for the fall or winter term of AY26-27.
3. The Warfighter Education and Professional Studies - Part Time (WEPS-PT) program provides an opportunity for selected Officers and Senior Enlisted personnel assigned to shore duty to attain Navy relevant graduate education in selected areas of study. WEPS-PT is the follow-on program to the previous Graduate Education Voucher (GEV) and Advanced Education Voucher (AEV) programs. Hybrid graduate education programs are encouraged as completion of the program will grant IRGE credit per reference F. a. For AY 26-27, WEPS-PT quotas are listed below, subject to funding availability, and all quotas must be allocated by 19 June 2026. b. Community specific quotas allocated in the SEA Plan are as follows:
Quotas SURFOR 1110 9 SUBFOR 1120 7 NSW 1130 2 AIRFOR 13X0 11 Any URL/RL/IWL 10 (3211 Operations Research or 6XXX Artificial Intelligence) Senior Enlisted 10 (Selected via board chaired by OPNAV N3N5N7 CMDCM)
4. Areas of Study: WEPS-FT and WEPS-PT participants shall choose from the following areas of study that grant the associated SSC per reference C. National Security / Strategic / Regional Studies - 2XXX Public Policy / Public Administration / - 2300 Operations Research /Analysis / Data Science - 3211 Engineering and Technology - 5XXX Systems Operations / Artificial Intelligence / Computer Science - 6XXX a. For officer selectees, all Education Plans will be reviewed against the Educational Skill Requirements (ESR) and approved by the respective Subject Matter Expert (SME) listed in reference C. b. The full list of subspecialty codes' respective ESRs can be found online at https://www.mynavyhr.navy.mil/Career-Management/Education/Subspecialty/. For Senior Enlisted selectees, all degree programs shall be consistent with the student's rating.
5. Application and Selection Process a. Community sponsors will develop and implement their own application criteria and selection process to identify their most talented officers with proven past performance and high potential future performance for WEPS-FT participation. Officer community sponsors will determine their nominees for selection no later than 31 January 2026 and submit them to the Director for Warfighter Development (OPNAV N71) via the Civilian Institutions (CIVINS) program office at OPNAV_N711(at)us.navy.mil and CIVINS(at)us.navy.mil. Enlisted personnel interested in WEPS-PT should apply through their detailers.
6. The Naval War College Fleet Seminar - Full Time pilot program builds upon the proven model of the Fleet Seminar Program announced in reference D for LT and senior URL and IWL officers. This is a small pilot program with limited opportunities. This program leads to a Master of Arts in Defense and Strategic Studies from the Naval War College via the Fleet Seminar Program and College of Distance Education. This pilot program provides officers time to attend the three required core JPME I courses (Strategy and War, Theater Security Decision Making, and Joint Maritime Operations) concurrently for 10 months as a full-time student. Upon completion of JPME I, the student officer will have up to an additional 8 months to complete the electives required to earn a master's in National Security Studies from the U.S. Naval War College's College of Distance Education per NWC program requirements (https://usnwc.edu/college-of-distance-education/Graduate-Degree-Program/Electives-and-Area-of-Study). Elective coursework will be completed via NWC program requirements which includes in person and distance learning options. Officers assigned to Fleet Seminar - Full Time will be administratively assigned to the closest senior Navy Reserve Officer Training Corps (NROTC) or Navy Support unit. The JPME Phase I core courses will commence in early September 2026 and conclude in late May 2027. a. Officers selected for this pilot program must be assigned to one of the Fleet Concentration Areas (FCA) listed below. Enrollment period is 01 April to 31 May 2026. Courses begin in early September 2026. b. Community specific quotas allocated in the Strategic Education and Alignment (SEA) Plan are as follows: NORFOLK SAN DIEGO PEARL HARBOR SURFOR 1110 1 1 1 SUBFOR 1120 1 1 NSW 1130 1 1 EOD 1140 1 AIRFOR 13X0 1 1 IWL 1810 1 1820 1 1830 1 1
7. Service Obligation: In line with reference A, all selectees shall agree in writing to remain on active duty for three times the number of months spent in full-time education up to a total of three years of obligated service time following graduation or withdrawal from the program. A member who voluntarily or through misconduct fails to complete the active-duty time agreed upon shall be required to reimburse the cost of the education received, prorated for the obligated time served.
8. Points of contact. a. For AY 26/27 WEPS quota and FSFT pilot opportunity availability, contact your detailer. b. For policy questions, e-mail OPNAV N711 (OPNAV_N711(at)us.navy.mil).
9. This message will remain in effect until superseded or 31 May 2026, whichever occurs first.
10. Released by VADM Y. M. Davids, Deputy Chief of Naval Operations for Operations, Plans, Strategy and Warfighting Development (N3N5N7).//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 018/26 - ACADEMIC YEAR 2026-2027 MIT SEMINAR XXI CALL FOR APPLICATIONS
item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ ROUTINE R 231817Z JAN 26 MID320018491950U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS UNCLAS
NAVADMIN 018/26
PASS TO OFFICE CODES: INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC/N3N5N7// MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N3N5N7/JAN//
SUBJ/ACADEMIC YEAR 2026-2027 MIT SEMINAR XXI CALL FOR APPLICATIONS//
RMKS/1. This NAVADMIN is the call for applications for the Academic Year (AY) 2026-2027 Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) Seminar XXI. A separate call for applications for the 2026-27 Dark Trident program will follow.
2. Program Information. Seminar XXI represents a unique opportunity for future policy leaders to share perspectives, while cultivating their understanding of the intricate relationships among economy, society, and culture in the international environment. Seminar XXI Fellows develop broad perspectives and analytical skills that enable them to evaluate and formulate national policy. Seminar XXI is an educational development program for current and future leaders in the U.S. national security and foreign policy communities. Administered by MIT's Center for International Studies, the program is intended for professionals with significant potential to move into key decision-making roles in the next 5-10 years. Program participants, known as Fellows, come from a wide range of sponsoring organizations including the armed forces, Federal agencies, non-governmental organizations, and private companies. The goal of Seminar XXI is to equip rising military officials and civilian executives with the multi-dimensional analytic skills needed to understand foreign relations and shape effective policy options for the United States.
3. Course Schedule. Participants will meet for a total of nine sessions during the 2026-27 academic year, beginning in September 2026 and ending in May 2027. Sessions will include one (1) introductory session; three (3) weekend sessions in Warrenton, VA; and five (5) weeknight sessions in Washington, D.C.
4. Eligibility. Navy Seminar XXI Fellows will be Navy officers (O5-O7) and civilians (GS14-15, SES, or equivalent) with clear promotion potential, and opportunity to apply these skills in their future assignments in the Navy. This application process is open only to individuals serving in Navy organizations, on the Joint Staff, or in the Office of the Secretary of War (OSW), or other offices without dedicated Seminar XXI quotas who are attached to commands located within a 225-mile driving distance from the National Capital Region for the entire period of September 2026 through May 2027. Navy personnel in agencies with MIT Seminar XXI programs must apply through their parent organizations. Please contact the Navy Fellowship Office at: navy.fellowships@us.navy.mil for questions on eligibility.
5. Program Specifics. For AY26-27, MIT will select a total of 80 personnel across the Federal Government. Of these, 12 will be Navy nominees. A total of twelve (12) Navy officers and Civilians will be selected for the AY26-27 Seminar XXI fellowships. Selectees must meet minimum attendance requirements and are encouraged to attend every class with the full support of their command. There is no tuition cost to the participants or their individual commands, regardless of duty station. However, participants or their commands are responsible for all non-tuition expenses, including travel, for Seminar XXI meetings and activities.
6. Admission Process. A Navy selection panel will choose from all Navyapplicants those 12 individuals with the best combination of experience, accomplishments, and clear potential for significant future advancement and leadership positions. MIT Seminar XXI nominees will be notified upon completion of panel deliberations by early April 2026. OPNAV N3N5N7 will provide the list of the 12 Navy nominees selected by the panel to the MIT Seminar XXI Admissions Board by early April 2026. Navy nominees accepted by MIT will be notified by mid-June 2026.
7. Service Obligation. Active-duty MIT Seminar XXI Fellows incur a service obligation of one year upon completion.
8. Application Package. Each nomination package must include the following: an application form, a curriculum vitae (CV), and two letters of recommendation (one of which must be from the first Flag officer or SES in the applicant s chain of command). Applicants outside the National Capitol Region must also include a command endorsement letter acknowledging individual/command responsibilities. The deadline for submission of all individual application materials to the Navy Fellowship Office is 23 February 2026. Please visit the MIT Seminar Information Request Portal (see link below) for additional information on MIT Seminar XXI and the application process, and to request application templates. For issues accessing the portal, please contact the Navy Fellowship Office at: navy.fellowships@us.navy.mil MIT Seminar XXI Information Request Portal: https:(slash)(slash)play.apps.appsplatform.us/play/e/d7f9e96a-011c-4baf-80c5-c5e4831ecebe/a/3ee70929-a39b-49d1-a5aa-afbd52f38407?tenantId=e3333e00-c877-4b87-b6ad-45e942de1750&hint=51ffef8c-ea0a-4845-af10-d860e478c903 Intelink Short URL: https:(slash)(slash)go.intelink.gov/-DXsUiz
9. Organization Rankings. Organizations with more than one applicant should rank all candidates who are submitting application packages within their command. Military and civilian applicants should be ranked separately. A template for the ranking memorandum is available upon request from the Navy Fellowship Office at: navy.fellowships(at)us.navy.mil The deadline to provide rankings is 02 March 2026.
10. Released by VADM Y. M. Davids, Deputy Chief of Naval Operations for Operations, Plans, Strategy, and Warfighting Development (N3N5N7).//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 015/26 - FISCAL YEAR 2025 COPERNICUS AWARD WINNERS ANNOUNCEMENT
item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ ROUTINE R 231233Z JAN 26 MID320018490994U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CMC WASHINGTON DC CNO WASHINGTON DC COMDT COGARD WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS
NAVADMIN/015-26
MSGID/NAVADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N2N6/JAN//
SUBJ/FISCAL YEAR 2025 COPERNICUS AWARD WINNERS ANNOUNCEMENT//
POC/FISCHETTI/CIV/AFCEA EDUCATIONAL FOUNDATION/-/TEL: (703) 631-6147/EMAIL: EDFOUNDATION(AT)AFCEA.ORG
REF/A/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/151714Z SEP 25//
AMPN/REF A IS NAVADMIN 197/25, FISCAL YEAR 2025 COPERNICUS AWARD NOMINATION//
RMKS/1. The Chief of Naval Operations is pleased to congratulate the fiscal year 2025 Copernicus Award winners. The Copernicus Award recognizes individual contributions to Naval Warfare in the disciplines of Command, Control, Communications, Computers, and Intelligence (C4I), Information Systems or Information Warfare (IW). AFCEA International and the U.S. Naval Institute (USNI) have selected the following Copernicus Award winners for fiscal year 2025:
a) LCDR Ryan M. Benito, USN, Carrier Strike Group TWO b) ITC Jeffrey D. Claypool, USN United States SEVENTH Fleet c) LT George T. Denove, USN, Cryptologic Warfare Activity 67 d) LTJG Jadyn A. Dessalines, USN, Navy Cyber Defense Operations Command e) LtCol Karl E. Fisher, USMC, Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency f) Maj Malia E. Haselton, USMC, 1st Marine Regiment, 1st Marine Division g) CTMCS Darrell R. Herschel, USN, Navy Information Operations Command Georgia h) CDR Thomas E. Hollinberger, USCG, Assistant Commandant & PEO for C5I (CG-C5I) i) LTJG Christopher M. Howard, USN, CYBER SQUADRON ONE j) LT James B. Hutchinson, USN, Special Operations Command South k) Sgt Matthew A. Kattich, USMC, Marine Wing Communications Squadron 28 l) GySgt Quinn W. Larkin, USMC, USMC Support Company m) CWO Alan Lindo, USCG, 1790 Cyber Protection Team n) CWTCS Patrick J. Lucia, USN, Navy Information Operations Command Georgia o) Maj Adam L. McCoy, USMC, United States Strategic Command p) 1stLt Maximilian L. Mendez, USMC, Marine Wing Communications Squadron 38 q) Mr. James M. Murphy, NAVY INFORMATION OPERATIONS COMMAND PENSACOLA r) Maj William T. Oblak, USMC, Expeditionary Warfare Training Group, Atlantic s) ITC Sean T. Petesch, USN, Tactical Operations Control Squadron ELEVEN t) LCDR Dean W. Porter, USN, FIFTH Fleet u) LT Vincent O. Potente, USN, USS JOHN PAUL JONES (DDG 53) v) LT Alex V. Psichas, USN, FIWCPAC/COMPACFLT w) Maj Victor G. Ray, USMC, 9th Communication Battalion x) Maj Trevor O. Raymond, USMC, Information Command, Control, Communications, and Computers (IC4) y) CWO4 Christopher L. Reynolds, USMC, U.S. Marine Corps Forces Pacific, G6/G39 z) Mr. Joshua D. Smak, U.S. Coast Guard Command, Control, Communication, Computers, Cyber, and Intelligence Service Center aa)Chief Samuel V. Swymer, USN, Naval Special Warfare Development Group ab)CWO3 Crystal F. Thomas, USN, Information Warfare Training Group Norfolk ac)ITCS John R. Tiger, USN, Naval Special Warfare Development Group ad)IT1 Cody A. Whitt, USCG, U.S. Coast Guard Cutter KIMBALL
2. All Copernicus Award winners will receive an invitation from AFCEA and USNI inviting them, their spouse/guest, and their Commanding Officer to attend an awards reception and presentation ceremony held at the AFCEA International/USNI WEST 2026 Conference in San Diego, California, at the San Diego Convention Center on 10 February 2026.
3. Released by Ms. Jennifer Edgin, Acting Deputy Chief of Naval Operations for Information Warfare, OPNAV N2N6.//

2. If a selected officer does not decline promotion in writing prior to the projected date of rank (noted above in paragraph 1), that officer is considered to have accepted the promotion on the date indicated. An officer who chooses to decline promotion must submit the declination in writing to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-806) within thirty (30) days of the release of this NAVADMIN.
3. The Commanding Officer (CO), the Secretary of the Navy, or his designee, has the authority to delay projected promotions. Upon the exercise of a delay, the officer shall be notified in writing before the effective date of the promotion and not be tendered the promoting appointment. The delay authority will immediately forward the notification of delay with supporting documentation to COMNAVPERSCOM (PERS-83), copy to PERS-806.
4. COs are reminded of their responsibility to ensure the officers they are promoting are mentally, physically, morally and professionally qualified in accordance with section 624 of title 10, U.S. Code. Per reference (a), officers are ineligible for promotion if they have failed the most recent Physical Fitness Assessment. COs shall verify the promoting officer's information in the Physical Readiness Information Management System to ensure compliance with reference (a) and delay the officer’s promotion in accordance with paragraph 3 of this NAVADMIN, if required.
5. Prepare a Delivery of Permanent Appointment (NAVPERS form 1421/7)(Rev 10-13). Use this NAVADMIN as reference (a). Use section 624 of title 10, U.S. Code as reference (b) for permanent lieutenants and above. Use section 578 of title 10, U.S. Code as reference (b) for chief warrant officers. Mail the completed form to PERS-806 or email via My Navy Career Center at askmncc.fct@navy.mil.
6. Officers should review reference (b) and (c) for minimum Time-In-Grade (TIG)/Service-In-Grade(SIG) requirements for purposes of voluntary retirement. (NOTE: TIG/SIG requirements for active-duty LCDR voluntary retirements changed with National Defense Authorization Act 2021; Update to reference (b) is pending.) Training and Administration of Reserve officers and those officers temporarily on active-duty anticipating release from active duty under reference (d) may accept promotion without incurring any additional active-duty obligation.
7. New photographs are required for all officers within three months after acceptance of promotion. See reference (e) for specific requirements.
8. Released by Vice Admiral Jeffrey J. Czerewko, N1.//
BT #0001 NNNN CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/

item.title: NAVADMIN 011/26 - NAVY RESERVE PROMOTIONS TO THE PERMANENT GRADES OF CAPTAIN, COMMANDER, LIEUTENANT COMMANDER, LIEUTENANT, AND CHIEF WARRANT OFFICER IN THE LINE AND STAFF CORPS
item.description:
CLASSIFICATION: UNCLASSIFIED/ ROUTINE R 161726Z JAN 26 MID320018476093U FM CNO WASHINGTON DC TO NAVADMIN INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC BT UNCLAS PASS TO OFFICE CODES: FM CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1// INFO CNO WASHINGTON DC//N1//
NAVADMIN 011/26
MSGID/GENADMIN/CNO WASHINGTON DC/N1/JAN//
SUBJ/NAVY RESERVE PROMOTIONS TO THE PERMANENT GRADES OF CAPTAIN, COMMANDER, LIEUTENANT COMMANDER, LIEUTENANT, AND CHIEF WARRANT OFFICER IN THE LINE AND STAFF CORPS//
REF/A/DOC/OPNAV/22APR22// REF/B/DOC/OPNAV/28FEB12// REF/C/MSG/CNO WASHINGTON DC/041935ZAUG22 REF/D/DOC/SECNAV/24JUL19// REF/E/DOC/COMNAVPERSCOM/05APR24//
NARR/REF A IS OPNAVINST 6110.1K, PHYSICAL READINESS PROGRAM. REF B IS OPNAVINST 1811.3A, VOLUNTARY RETIREMENT AND TRANSFER TO THE FLEET RESERVE OF MEMBERS OF THE NAVY SERVING ON ACTIVE DUTY. REF C IS NAVADMIN 176/22, POLICY UPDATE CONCERNING SERVICE-IN-GRADE RETIREMENT ELIGIBILITY FOR ACTIVE DUTY AND TRAINING AND ADMINISTRATION FOR RESERVE LIEUTENANT COMMANDERS. REF D IS SECNAVINST 1920.6D, ADMINISTRATIVE SEPARATION OF OFFICERS. REF E IS MILPERSMAN ARTICLE 1070-181, OFFICER PHOTOGRAPHS.//
RMKS/1. Congratulations to the following officers on their promotions to the ranks indicated in this message. This NAVADMIN is the authority for effecting permanent officer promotions on the dates indicated. Columns read name and designator.
